title: Cafe Beverage Sticker Redesign
desc:
I need our existing beverage sticker taken from “good enough” to truly shelf-worthy. The café has three bottled items—Cold Coffee, Cold Cocoa and an Ice Tea—and each share the same core layout today. I want a full refresh that keeps the size and basic die-cut shape but upgrades everything else: colour palette, font choice and visual accents.

Font: the current typeface feels dated and heavy. I’d like something cleaner, balanced and legible at arm’s length when the bottles sit in a cooler.

Colour scheme: each flavour should feel distinct yet obviously from the same brand family. You’re free to propose new tones that complement a warm café identity while still popping against condensation and low-light fridges.

Imagery: I’m leaning toward abstract graphics rather than literal product photos or stereotypical café icons. Think subtle patterns, shapes or textures that evoke cool refreshment and give each sticker memorable shelf presence.

Deliverables
• Three print-ready sticker files (AI or layered PSD plus press-quality PDF)  
• One mock-up of each design on a bottle for quick stakeholder approval  
• Colour, font and graphic usage guide so we can replicate the look across future SKUs

I’ll provide the current artwork, logo files and exact die-line the moment we start. Let me know your timeline, any clarifying questions and a quick note on your typical revision process so we can keep momentum once the first concepts land.

Abstract patterns/textures (no literal product photos) — from template/stock assets
Outputs: 3 print-ready sticker files (AI or layered PSD + press PDF) + bottle mock-up each + colour/font/graphic usage guide

title: Cosmetics Label Sticker Design

I’m open to your creative vision on colour palette and overall style, provided the final look feels cohesive with a modern cosmetics brand. title: Supplement Label / Packaging Designer Needed for Premium DTC Brand

About the brand: This brand lives in the world of clean DTC, quiet tech longevity, and modern performance wellness. The brand should feel: * premium * modern * restrained * intelligent * trustworthy * design-led It should not feel: * clinical * bodybuilding / gym supplement * loud Amazon nootropic * cheap private label * “old person vitamin” Think more in the territory of: * Hims * Ritual * AG1 * modern longevity / wellness brands with taste Product: Product: Daily Clarity Format: Capsules Bottle Count: 60 capsules Serving: 2 capsules daily Supply: 30-day supply Daily Clarity is a stimulant-free cognitive support product designed to support: * focus * attention * mental clarity * reduced mental fatigue Key ingredients include: * Citicoline * L-Theanine * Rhodiola rosea extract * affron saffron extract Important manufacturing context We are already working with a manufacturer and have real production constraints in place. Current production context: * V1 will use a standard black bottle * manufacturer bottle is a 200 cc PET bottle * manufacturer has already provided the label dieline / template * current label direction is a metallic label with matte finish * we need a label that works beautifully on a black bottle and is fully production-ready This means we do not just need a nice concept. We need a designer who can create something premium that also works in real-world manufacturing. Scope of work: We need help with the first production label for Daily Clarity. 1. Front-label concepts Create 2 to 3 front-label design directions for the bottle. The directions should feel: * premium * modern * minimal * credible * design-forward * strong in product photography and e-commerce 2. Refine selected concept into full label system Take the chosen direction and build it into a full production label that can accommodate: * front branding * product name * descriptor * 60 capsules / 30-day supply * Supplement Facts * ingredients / other ingredients * suggested use * warnings * distributor info * lot / expiration handling * all other required compliance content 3. Build to actual dieline Use the manufacturer’s exact label template for the 200 cc PET bottle and prepare a real print-ready file. 4. Bottle mockups Provide polished bottle mockups on the selected black bottle so we can evaluate the design before approving production. Brand system context: We already have a designer working on the logo. You do not need to redesign the logo from scratch, but the label should work cohesively with the brand system and feel like part of one premium visual language. What we are looking for: Please apply only if you have strong experience with: * supplement labels * packaging design * bottle labels * print production * dielines * compliance-heavy layouts Bonus if you have worked on: * wellness brands * skincare * food/beverage packaging * premium DTC products Deliverables: * 2 to 3 initial front-label concepts * refined selected direction * full flat label layout * bottle mockups * production-ready print files built to manufacturer dieline * source files File deliverables: Please provide: * AI * PDF * print-ready production files * high-res PNG/JPG mockups To apply Please send: 1. portfolio examples of actual packaging / bottle label work 2. any supplement / wellness / skincare label examples 3. confirmation that you are comfortable designing from a manufacturer dieline We are looking for someone with both taste and technical packaging ability, not just a general graphic designer.

The goal is to create packaging that feels like a premium skincare product: clean, refined, current, trustworthy, and visually memorable. The design should work well on a clean spray bottle with premium-looking hardware. The direction should balance: Premium skincare Clean clinical credibility Millennial-level maturity Gen-Z freshness Gen-Alpha-influenced modernity Minimal luxury Strong shelf appeal Practical CMYK-friendly print execution We are looking for a designer who can explore strong design directions rather than simply placing text on a label. The final design should feel intentional, balanced, and brand-building. Brand Feel The design should feel: Modern Clean Premium Quiet luxury Fresh but not childish Minimal but not boring Clinical but not hospital-like Youthful but still mature Skincare-focused, not disinfectant-like Contemporary without depending on short-term trends What to Avoid Please avoid designs that feel: Too medical or pharmaceutical Too playful or childish Too loud, colorful, or cheap Too dated or overly millennial Too TikTok-trendy without premium value Too decorative or feminine-only Like a cleaning spray Like a generic beauty product Overloaded with icons, droplets, leaves, sparkles, or cliché skincare elements Deliverables For the first stage, we would like: Initial label design concepts Front label layout direction Color palette suggestions suitable for CMYK printing Typography direction Mockups on the bottle if possible Final print-ready label file after approval Editable source files If the first product design goes well, there will be future work for additional skincare, surface-care, and everyday health products under the same brand. Ideal Designer You should be comfortable designing for skincare, cosmetics, beauty, wellness, or premium consumer goods. Experience with modern skincare packaging for younger and mature audiences is a plus. title: Label Designer for CPG Products

If you have a passion for design and a keen eye for detail, we would love to hear from you! --- **What You'll Be Doing** - Designing a product label for one of our cleaning products - Working from our existing logo and developing a cohesive color palette, typography, and layout - Delivering print-ready, production-spec files (AI, PDF, or equivalent) - Incorporating all required label elements: product name, usage instructions, ingredients/warnings, net weight, barcode area, and brand identity --- **What We Provide** - Brand logo (vector format) - Product details, copy, and legal text - Reference examples and direction on the look and feel we're after - Fast feedback and clear communication throughout --- **Who We're Looking For** - Proven experience in CPG, household, or cleaning product label design (portfolio required) - Strong understanding of print specs and dieline formats - Ability to turn around initial concepts within 2 to 3 business days - Attention to detail — especially around compliance text, hierarchy, and readability - Someone who asks smart questions upfront rather than guessing --- **Project Scope** - One-time project - Single label design (revisions included) - Final deliverables: layered source file + print-ready export --- **To Apply** Please include: 1. 2 to 3 examples of product label work from your portfolio (cleaning, household, or CPG preferred) 2. title: Convert Existing Label Into Wraparound Label

I already created separate front and back labels for my 1 oz Ritual Dose bottle. I now need those combined into a single wraparound label for the same bottle. This is not a rebrand or a new bottle design. The goal is simply to rework the current label layout into a cohesive wraparound format while keeping the overall aesthetic aligned with the existing VARA packaging, especially the larger full-size bottle label that will remain unchanged. What I need: * Combine my existing front + back labels into one wraparound label * Optimize spacing, hierarchy, and readability for the curved bottle * Keep the design elevated, minimal, and cohesive with the current VARA branding * Prepare final print-ready files * Open to thoughtful suggestions on layout, sizing, spacing, etc. while staying very close to the existing design direction Looking for someone with: * Experience in luxury beauty, wellness, skincare, or haircare packaging * Strong typography/layout skills * Understanding of print production and label setup for round bottles Please send: * Relevant packaging examples * Experience with wraparound labels * Estimated timeline Thank you! Brittney

1 oz Ritual Dose bottle dimensions + wraparound dieline
Combined copy from both labels
VARA aesthetic to stay aligned with (esp. full-size bottle label)
Print-ready spec for round bottle (bleed, curvature allowance)

mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (client's SVG label + logo + QR) -> asset_inline_preview (inspect missing text layers) -> fill_text (re-add the wording that didn't import) -> document_render_vector / document_convert_pdf (print-ready PDF + SVG at 3.9375 x 1.625 in with bleed) -> asset_preview_file

Brand logo file + the real QR code image (must scan)
Label size: 3.9375 x 1.625 in (OnlineLabels template); bleed + safe zone for a 1 oz curved bottle
Missing text wording to re-add

title: Corporate Back Cover Design

The visual direction is firmly professional and corporate, so please build the composition around cool blues and balanced grays. I will supply the official logo and the approved copy; those two items should stand out clearly, with no graphics so the typography and brand mark remain the focal points.

Deliverables  
• Print-ready PDF in CMYK, crop marks included  
• Layered source file (AI or PSD) so our in-house team can make future edits  

Once I hand over the assets, I’d appreciate a first proof within 24 hours so we can iterate quickly and send the book to press on schedule.

title: Book Launch Social Assets

Here is what I need from you:  
• A set of eye-catching promotional images sized correctly for each platform’s feed, story and thumbnail requirements, all consistent with my cover design and existing site mock-ups.  
• Concise, persuasive ad copy that fits each network’s character limits and motivates clicks to the website’s pre-order page.

Everything should maintain a polished, professional tone while still feeling approachable to new readers. I’ll supply the book cover, a short synopsis and brand colours; you bring the creative spark, the correct export specs and messaging that converts.

Deliverables will be approved when:  
– Images render crisply on all four platforms.  
– Copy reads naturally, stays on-brand and meets each platform’s ad guidelines.  
– Final assets arrive in layered source files plus ready-to-post PNG/JPEG and text docs.

If you have recent examples of book or product launches, even better—share them and let’s get this story in front of the world.

inputs: Book cover image + short synopsis + brand colours (client supplies)
Promo graphics sized per platform: Facebook, Instagram (feed + story), TikTok, YouTube thumbnail
Persuasive ad copy per network (character-limited) driving to the pre-order page
Delivered as ready-to-post PNG/JPEG (+ layered source) + copy docs

title: Experienced KDP Book Cover Designer Needed – Series Branding & Reusable Cover Template
desc: Experienced KDP Book Cover Designer Needed – Series Branding & Reusable Cover Template Overview I am seeking an experienced book cover designer to create a professional, reusable cover system for an ongoing series of books. I have already created the AI artwork and will provide both the original image and my preferred front-cover crop. The successful candidate will professionally refine the artwork, prepare it for publication, and create a reusable series template that can be used across multiple future books. This is not a project to redesign the artwork from scratch. The character, colour palette, visual style, and overall concept have already been approved. About the Project The books are spiritual, metaphysical, self-development, and channeled works. The same central character will become the visual identity of the series, with different titles being applied to future books. The successful designer must understand Amazon KDP specifications and how cover dimensions change depending on page count and spine width. What I Need Artwork Refinement The supplied artwork is AI-generated and may require professional refinement, including: * Correcting AI-generated lettering and symbols * Correcting the spirit board lettering layout where required * Refining tattoos and small visual details * Cleaning up any AI-generated inconsistencies * Improving overall production quality while maintaining the existing style Wraparound Cover Creation The supplied image is currently designed as a front cover. The successful designer will: * Extend the artwork to create a full wraparound cover * Create a spine design * Prepare space for: * Book title * Subtitle * Author name * Back cover blurb * Author bio * Barcode The background artwork may need to be seamlessly extended across the spine and back cover. Typography & Readability Future titles will vary significantly in length. The designer should be comfortable: * Creating subtle gradients where necessary * Darkening or lightening areas of the artwork to improve readability * Creating a flexible title placement system * Maintaining a professional commercial appearance For example, the upper section of the spirit board may need to be subtly darkened to allow titles to stand out while preserving the artwork underneath. Deliverables * Amazon KDP Paperback Cover * Amazon KDP eBook Cover * Editable layered PSD files * Reusable series template * Font recommendations * Future title replacement instructions Ideal Candidate * Experienced with Amazon KDP * Strong Photoshop skills * Experience working with AI-generated artwork * Experience designing book series * Strong typography skills * Excellent attention to detail Ongoing Work This is intended to become an ongoing relationship. There will be multiple books within this series requiring future title changes, spine adjustments, and cover preparation. To Apply Please provide: * Examples of previous book covers * Examples of KDP projects * Examples of series branding work * Confirmation that you understand KDP spine calculations and varying cover dimensions Please include the words: "KDP Series Designer" in your application so I know you have read the entire brief.
task_type: KDP Wraparound Cover — extend front-only AI artwork to spine + back
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (supplied front-cover image) -> image_generative_expand (outpaint background across spine + back to full wraparound) -> image_crop_and_resize (KDP wrap trim + bleed for page count) -> fill_text (title/subtitle/author placement) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Supplied AI-generated front cover image (character/palette already approved) + preferred front crop
KDP paperback wrap dimensions for the page count (spine width) + eBook size
Space to reserve for title, subtitle, author, back blurb, author bio, barcode
Reusable series template + font recommendations + title-replacement guidance
note: image_generative_expand can extend the background across the spine/back and the connector adds title text; correcting AI lettering/symbols, refining tattoos, building the reusable layered PSD series template and exact KDP spine calc need a human in Photoshop.

title: Freelance Cover Designer/Branding — Book - KDP (Print + eBook) and Logo| Long-Term Potential
desc: About the Project I'm seeking an experienced, dependable, and highly recommended freelance cover and logo designer to create a consistent cover and branding - 1. Create the cover for my non-fiction book on buying real estate (7 chapters, ~33,600 words). The book will be self-published on Amazon KDP in both print (paperback) and eBook (Kindle) formats 2. Create a logo consistent with the concept design of the cover, to use for branding for video sales page and workbooks Strong communication throughout the project is essential — I value designers who check in regularly, ask clarifying questions, and keep the project on track. This is the first book in a series, with a second book already in progress and additional projects planned. I'm specifically looking to build a long-term working relationship with the right designer who can establish a consistent visual identity across the series. Candidates open to ongoing collaboration are highly preferred - not ready to hire full time at this point; looking for on-going project-specific working relationship for the right designer. Scope of Work - Book 1. Initial concept consultation to discuss genre conventions, target audience, tone, and visual direction 2. Two to three initial cover concepts/mockups for review 3. Revisions based on feedback (please specify how many rounds are included) 4. Final cover delivered in all formats required for KDP: a. eBook cover — Kindle-compliant JPEG (1600 x 2560 px recommended) b. Print paperback cover — full wraparound PDF (front cover, spine, back cover) sized to KDP specifications based on final page count, with appropriate bleed and margins 5. Editable source files (PSD, AI, or equivalent) so the design can be adapted for future books in the series Scope of Work - Logo Design 1. Initial concept consultation (can be combined with the cover consultation) to discuss genre conventions, target audience, tone, and visual direction 2. A custom vector logo that is created with these considerations in mind: a. original geometry b. clean scalable icon system c. trademark-clear branding 3. usable file for use for email, worksheets (KDP and PDF), and other mediums Design Considerations Book Cover:  Look professional and competitive within the real estate / personal finance / non-fiction how-to category on Amazon  Be legible and visually striking at thumbnail size (critical for Amazon browsing)  Establish a visual template or style system that can carry across future books in the series Both the cover and logo should:  Communicate credibility and authority appropriate for the subject matter  Establish a visual template or style system that can carry across both Deliverables  Final eBook cover (JPEG, KDP-compliant)  Final print cover (full wraparound PDF, KDP-compliant)  Editable source files  Any fonts, stock photo licenses, or other assets used (with documentation of licensing)  Usable website assets  Print-quality graphics  Strong legal ownership Timeline 15 to 30 days from the date you accept the project. I'd like work to begin within 10 days of hiring. Budget Please share your rate structure and what's included (number of concepts, revision rounds, formats) in your response. Rates for subsequent covers in the series can be discussed once we've established a successful working relationship. How to Apply Please respond within 3 business days with the following: 1. Confirmation that you can begin work within the 10 days after hire 2. Your anticipated completion timeframe (number of days or target date) 3. A portfolio link or samples of previous book cover work, especially in non-fiction, real estate, business, or personal finance genres or similar 4. A portfolio link or samples of branding or logo design 5. Your proposed rate, including what's covered (number of concepts, revision rounds, file formats) 6. Your interest in and availability for ongoing cover design work for additional books in the series. I'll review applications promptly (within 3 business days) and respond to candidates I'd like to move forward with. Thank you for your interest — I look forward to hearing from you.

title: Book Cover Design
desc: Here's a fuller brief for the designer: Book Cover Design Brief - Speedrunning Capitalism Hello, I have finished writing my book and require a front cover designed, with a back cover to follow once the physical edition is confirmed. The Book Title: Speedrunning Capitalism Subtitle: How Business Acquisition Accelerates Your Wealth Author: Brandon Lipman Speedrunning Capitalism is a business acquisition guide for the middle class. It's direct, practical and unapologetic. Not a traditional business book - no corporate tone, no fluff. Think closer to Alex Hormozi's visual aesthetic than a Harvard Business Review publication. Current Draft I have attached a draft cover I've put together in Canva. You're welcome to improve on this layout or take it in a completely different creative direction - I'm open to both. The Image The current draft uses an arrow breaking through a horizontal line. The concept is: something fast, precise and powerful breaking through a ceiling or orbit. The arrow works as a metaphor for both a traditional arrow and a rocket - deliberately ambiguous. I specifically don't want a rocket. Rockets are overused on business books and too literal. The arrow-breaking-through-a-line concept captures the same idea without being cliche. If you have a stronger creative idea that captures the same energy - acceleration, breaking through, upward trajectory - I'm open to seeing it. Design Direction Dark background - current draft uses near-black (#1a1a1a) White title text, gold accent (#C8A84B) for author name bar and details Clean, bold, typographic - the title should dominate SPEEDRUNNING on one line, CAPITALISM on the next line below it - these are the two biggest elements on the cover Author name: Brandon Lipman Subtitle at the bottom: How Business Acquisition Accelerates Your Wealth Should feel confident and modern - not corporate, not academic What I Need: One front cover in high resolution Delivered as both a print-ready PDF and a screen-optimised PNG/JPG A5 format (148mm x 210mm) with 3mm bleed for print Timeline and Questions: Happy to discuss timeline and pricing. Please share examples of previous book cover work if available.
task_type: Book Cover — 'Speedrunning Capitalism', A5 typographic, front
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express book-cover template, bold dark typographic / business) -> fill_text ('SPEEDRUNNING' / 'CAPITALISM', subtitle, author) -> change_background_color (#1a1a1a) -> document_convert_pdf (A5 148x210mm + 3mm bleed print PDF) + export PNG/JPG -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Title 'Speedrunning Capitalism' (two lines), subtitle 'How Business Acquisition Accelerates Your Wealth', author 'Brandon Lipman'
Colours: near-black #1a1a1a background, white title, gold accent #C8A84B
A5 (148mm x 210mm) with 3mm bleed; deliver print-ready PDF + screen PNG/JPG
Arrow-breaking-through-a-horizontal-line motif (simple vector shape, no rocket)
Existing Canva draft as layout reference

Visual Concept: A winding path leading toward warm golden light on the horizon. Photographic style preferred, not illustrated. The emotional target is "familiar but universal" — the image should feel like somewhere the viewer has been before, but they can't quite place it. Warm golden palette. Hopeful, not heavy. I have reference images and a complete brief ready to share once we connect. AI-generated reference mockups are attached as mood references only — the final cover must be original work using real photography or photographic composite at 300 DPI. No AI imagery in the final product. Deliverables: Front cover for Kindle ebook (high resolution, KDP-ready) Full paperback wrap for 6x9 trim size (front + spine + back), 300 DPI Spine width confirmed once page count is finalized by my formatter Thumbnail test file at 200px wide for Amazon legibility check Scope: 2-3 initial concept directions before selecting one to develop 2 revision rounds minimum included Royalty-free imagery (you handle licensing) Requirements: Portfolio showing memoir, wellness, or nonfiction book covers Familiarity with KDP file specifications Designs that work at Amazon thumbnail size (small-scale legibility is essential) Delivers layered source files Timeline: Flexible. Quality over speed. Realistic completion: 6-8 weeks from project start. To Apply: Please include 2-3 portfolio samples of book covers most relevant to memoir or wellness nonfiction. Briefly tell me about your experience with self-published authors and KDP specifications.

title: Book Cover Designer for [spirituality/self-help/memoir] [nonfiction] Book
desc: Looking for a talented and professional book cover designer to create a genre aligned cover for my book, Falling Madly in Self-Love. This is a nonfiction book in spirituality/self-help/memoir. If the budget is not within your rate, please respond with your preferred rate, as budget is negotiable. The final product should feel magnetic, stand out even as a small thumbnail, and match the expectations of readers browsing Amazon in my genre. Output required is: 1. Paperback cover 2. Ebook cover 3. Audiobook cover (optional) There will be 2 stages to this project: - Stage 1: creating the cover design - Stage 2: finalizing the cover for publication when I have the final page count I’m looking for a designer with: - A designer with experience designing commercial-quality book covers - Someone who understands genre trends and how to appeal to the Amazon shopper’s eye - Strong typography skills and ability to make a title stand out as a thumbnail - Someone collaborative, responsive, and able to make a few rounds of revisions I am seeking a design that feels bold, inspiring, and accessible - something that communicates both depth and hope. The design should reflect the themes of love, remembrance, and transformation while appealing to a broad, modern audience of readers who are seeking meaning, healing, and manifesting their dream life. I'm thinking of a rose, quartz pink color and gold (for the cover design). Something chic, elegant, but bold and classic, timeless, and memorable. My actual brand colors are #ff1493, #8a2be2, #ffd700, 00bfff, 6a0dad but I feel deeply called to the rose, quartz pink color and gold. I am open to a creative concept that maybe blends both so my book is still visually on brand but also okay if it is not. I provided you a mood board and list of book covers, to give you a feel for what I like in the genre (see attachments). Please respond with the phrase "purple butterfly" and let me know why you're a good fit for the job and a link to your portfolio with images of covers you’ve created in my genre. Thanks!

title: Premium Book Cover Designer for Disruptive Anti-Self-Help Book (Amazon KDP & eBook)
desc: I'm looking for a top-tier book cover designer for Stop Fixing Yourself! Why fixing yourself keeps the problem alive. Disruptive anti-self-help book challenging modern therapy clichés, the wellness industry, and toxic positivity. Croatian edition is selling out in physical bookstores. American edition launches on Amazon 2026. Strategic context (read first) This book belongs on the shelf next to Mark Manson, Ryan Holiday, Robert Greene, Cal Newport. It does not belong near Brené Brown, Atomic Habits, or Mel Robbins. The cover must communicate authority and seriousness, not relief or transformation. My reader has already read everything, tried every method, and is still stuck. The cover should make that reader feel seen, not promised to. Critical differentiation The Croatian edition uses a black cover with broken red chains. The American edition cannot copy this. Broken chains have become a cliché in the Amazon anxiety category and would position the book inside the genre it's critiquing. Push the chain metaphor harder and stranger, or replace it entirely with a stronger concept. If you go with chains, they cannot look like any current anxiety book on Amazon. If you go without, typography and composition must carry the full weight. Visual direction Minimalist, bold, premium, slightly dark. Deep blacks, dark greys, high-contrast typography. References in spirit: Holiday, Manson, Greene, Newport. Typography-driven covers usually outperform illustration-driven ones in this category. Strong typographic treatment on dark background with one striking element (or none). Strictly avoid No lotus flowers, sunrises, meditating people, or mountain silhouettes. No smoke, magic wands, mysterious eyes, or hypnosis-as-magic-show imagery. No motivational stock. No bright pastels. No generic broken-chain illustrations that look like every other anxiety book. Filter element on the front cover Include a small but visible line on the front, positioned subtly (above or below title, or as a small badge/stamp): No techniques. No exercises. No protocols. This is a deliberate filter that tells the wrong reader to put the book down. Small enough not to compete with the title, visible at Amazon thumbnail size. Typography Modern impactful sans-serif or elegant modern serif. Author name is Timoteo Crnković with Croatian diacritics. Font must natively support č, š, ć, ž without distortion or manual kerning. Non-negotiable. The Croatian rendering is also a deliberate signal of European authorship in a market saturated with generic American self-help, so it stays. References Three covers I want this to live next to: Mark Manson Everything Is Fucked, Ryan Holiday The Obstacle Is the Way, Cal Newport Deep Work. Croatian edition for reference only, do not replicate: https://prestaniraditinasebi.com https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Keovs434aTAhTvvM57Cbpq9pwnadL29N Audience Adults 28 to 55 in US, UK, Canada, Australia. Have read multiple self-help books. Familiar with CBT, mindfulness, breathwork, somatic experiencing. Frustrated with the genre but still buying. Not looking for another method. Looking for a different conversation. Deliverables Kindle eBook cover, high-res JPG to current KDP specs. Amazon KDP paperback full cover (front, spine, back) to exact KDP bleed specs. Page count provided once layout is final. Include space for KDP barcode/ISBN, publisher logo (Mental Reset d.o.o.), and one to two QR codes. Back cover must accommodate ~150 to 200 words of body copy, one pull quote, brief author bio, and optional author photo. Copy provided separately. Fully layered source files (PSD or AI), fonts embedded or clearly listed. Three to five high-quality 3D book mockups for marketing, social media, and stopfixingyourself.com. How to apply Start your proposal with the word RESET. Portfolio must specifically highlight dark, minimalist, or premium typography-driven covers. Prioritize examples in non-fiction, philosophy, business, or psychology. Generic portfolios will not be considered.

No exercises. No protocols.', author 'Timoteo Crnković') -> change_background_color (deep black) -> document_convert_pdf (KDP full cover + eBook JPG) -> asset_preview_file

title: Cover Artist for Hip-Hop Single - "Vision Board" (Trial Project, Long-Term Potential)
desc: Cover Art Designer — Hip-Hop Single (Paid Trial → Ongoing Work) Job Title (for the Upwork listing) Cover Artist for Hip-Hop Single — "Vision Board" (Trial Project, Long-Term Potential) About the project I'm 28, an independent hip-hop artist (stage name Bastian) with a catalog of 30+ tracks now rolling out as singles through a global distributor. I'm looking for a designer to create the cover art for my upcoming single "Vision Board," and — if it's a strong fit — to become my go-to cover artist for every release going forward. To be upfront: this is a paid trial. I'm hiring a few designers for this first cover so I can find the one person I want to build a long-term relationship with. If we click, you become my dedicated cover artist — regular, recurring, paid work on a predictable release schedule. I'd much rather find one great collaborator than re-hire from scratch every single. A bit about me (so you know what you're working with) I'm not your typical hip-hop artist. I grew up homeless in New York City, mostly Harlem, so grit is in my bones haha. Early on I was working jobs just to get by: working as an extra on TV shows/movies, doing sales at a gym in Spanish Harlem, construction, etc. I’m a hiphop head by nature, grew up listening to Biggie, Tupac, 50, early days Drake, MobbDeep. I dropped out of college and dove into entrepreneurship, and since 2017 I’ve been building businesses in the online/digital marketing space, while simultaneously perfecting my songwriting and lyrical skills as a hip hop artist. I know the hustle and grind intimately of being an entrepreneur, building your own business, and trying to make it. I’ve been blessed to have some pretty big wins in my journey so far, along with plenty of f***-ups, and my music is my space for me to share both. The song: "Vision Board" "Vision Board" is aspirational, motivational hip-hop - what I'd call "white collar rap." It's about manifesting a bigger life from humble beginnings: came from nothing, bootstrapped businesses, and is now experiencing the world fully. The energy is confident and earned, not flashy for its own sake. Lyrical world / imagery to pull from: "7 figures all on my vision board" "Experience life to the fullest, that's what I'm living for" Bucket-list cities, flights, "cloud skipping" Mediterranean settings — Tuscany, swimming in the Med, high-rise hotels, fine dining Came from poverty, built it in private, "putting the work in indoors" Quiet luxury and discipline — espresso with cinnamon, not champagne showers My overall visual aesthetic across the catalog is what I call "scenic realism" — cinematic, grounded, real-world atmosphere over cartoonish or overly digital looks. Here's how the song factors in: I'm keeping the track private until release, so it isn't linked here. But if this brief lights you up and you apply, I'll send you a private link to the full song (and the complete lyrics) so you can actually sit with it. Seeing the concept you come back with - after you've heard it - is a big part of how I'll decide who I book for the paid trial. Creative direction I want to see your interpretation, so I'm giving you the world of the song rather than dictating the exact image. A few guardrails: Lean into: cinematic/photographic feel, warmth, Mediterranean or aspirational-travel atmosphere, a sense of "earned" success, mood and depth, strong use of light. Avoid: generic "money/cars/chains" rap clichés, cheesy stock-photo collages, busy/cluttered compositions, anything that looks like a default Canva template. Surprise me - but make it feel like the song. Deliverables Final cover art as a high-resolution square image (see specs below) The layered source file (PSD / AI / or editable equivalent) 1–2 alternate concepts or variations if you have them (optional but a plus) Technical specs (streaming/DSP-ready) Dimensions: 3000 × 3000 px minimum, 1:1 square Color mode: RGB Format: high-quality JPG or PNG (flattened final, plus layered source) No website URLs, social handles, streaming-service logos, or promotional/pricing text in the artwork All imagery, fonts, and assets must be fully licensed for commercial use — please confirm (I'll confirm any additional distributor requirements before final delivery.) Timeline & budget Trial budget: $125 for this first cover Deadline for first draft: 24-48hrs after starting the project Ongoing rate for the long-term relationship is open to discussion once we've worked together. What I'm looking for in the right person A genuine eye — a portfolio that shows range and taste, not just technical skill Reliability and clear communication (this is recurring work; I need someone I can count on) Ability to take a creative concept and run with it Bonus: experience with music cover art, album artwork, or editorial/cinematic imagery How to apply This runs in two steps: Step 1 — Raise your hand. In your application, tell me: Why this resonates with you — what about the project, the story, or the world of the song makes you want in A link to your portfolio (and any music / album cover art you've done specifically) Your rate and turnaround for a single cover Step 2 — Hear it, then pitch a concept. If your application stands out, I'll send you a private link to the full track and lyrics. You sit with the song and come back with your concept and direction for the cover. The designers whose concepts I'm most excited about get booked for the paid trial. Please start your application with the word "7FIGS" so I know you read the full brief. Applications that don't will be skipped. I'll be reviewing portfolios closely and moving quickly. Looking forward to seeing your work.

specifications, including any spine, trim size, bleed, margin, and page-count-related requirements Preparing final print-ready files suitable for Amazon KDP upload Creating an ebook version of the book Exporting and testing an EPUB file Creating a KPF file suitable for Kindle publishing through Amazon KDP Required Experience The ideal candidate should have: Strong experience with Adobe InDesign Experience formatting books for print and digital publishing Familiarity with Amazon KDP print and Kindle ebook specifications Experience creating EPUB and KPF files Ability to work with Microsoft Word files and transfer content cleanly into InDesign Strong eye for typography, layout consistency, and professional book design Experience adjusting book covers or title designs based on page count and print specifications Attention to detail and ability to preserve the existing style of a book Deliverables Final deliverables should include: Updated Adobe InDesign file Print-ready interior PDF final file, if applicable Additional Notes The existing InDesign file, Word document containing the prologue, and current cover/title design files will be provided. title: Bilingual Educational Slides for Kids
desc:
I have a series of raw lesson notes, worksheets, and short activities that need to be turned into a fully designed slide deck in both English and Arabic. The tone should feel colorful and vibrant—bright palettes, playful shapes, and big, readable type—yet still organised so teachers can move through each lesson smoothly.  

The audience is children roughly 5 – 15 years old, so every slide has to balance clear learning objectives with an inviting look that keeps younger students focused. Because the deck is bilingual, I need someone comfortable aligning right-to-left Arabic alongside left-to-right English, making sure typography, spacing, and icons never feel cramped or uneven.

Here’s what I’ll provide:
• Plain text content, any illustrations or logos I already have, and a rough slide outline.  
• Immediate feedback as you work, since the timeline is tight.  

Here’s what I need back:
• An editable presentation file (PowerPoint or Google Slides) laid out in both languages.  
• Consistent master layouts, title and content styles, and a simple colour guide so future lessons match perfectly.  
• All fonts, images, and icons packaged or linked with full usage rights.  

Turnaround is important, but I’m flexible on hours—as long as you communicate progress and hit agreed milestones. If you’ve created bilingual or educational slides before, especially for this age group, attach a few of your best samples with your proposal and note any tools you prefer to work in (PowerPoint, Google Slides, Figma, etc.). We’ll discuss timelines, milestones, and a fair rate once I’ve seen your portfolio.
task_type: Presentation — bilingual (EN/Arabic RTL) kids educational deck
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express presentation template, colorful/playful for kids) -> fill_text (lesson copy, EN + Arabic) -> asset_add_file (client illustrations/logos) -> animate_design (optional) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Plain-text lesson content (English) + Arabic translations; rough slide outline
Client-supplied illustrations / logos
Bright playful palette, big readable type; audience ages 5-15
Right-to-left Arabic aligned alongside left-to-right English
Deliverable: EDITABLE PowerPoint/Google Slides with consistent master layouts + a simple colour guide; fonts packaged with usage rights
note: Express template + fill_text produces a designed bilingual deck, but the explicit deliverable is an editable PPTX/Google Slides with reusable master layouts, and clean RTL Arabic typesetting/alignment — a human finishes that in PowerPoint.

title: Investor-Ready Pitch Deck Design
desc:
I need a polished, data-driven pitch deck that convincingly secures investment for my company. The deck must be equally at home in front of funding organisations—angel groups, venture capital teams, even the occasional private-equity audience—so every slide has to feel both sophisticated and easy to scan.

Here’s what I want to shine through:

• Business model – a clear, visually intuitive walk-through of how we make money, including revenue streams and cost structure.  
• Market analysis – concise TAM/SAM/SOM numbers, trend graphics, and competitor positioning that back our growth story with evidence.  
• Unique selling points – crisp, benefits-oriented messaging that shows why we beat existing alternatives and why now is the right time to invest.

I’ll provide raw financials, market reports, and branding assets. You turn them into an investment-grade narrative, complete with compelling visuals, infographics, and a clean slide master I can reuse for day-to-day business presentations.

Deliverables  
1. Editable PowerPoint (or Keynote if you prefer)  
2. Print-ready and email-friendly PDF  
3. Source graphics and icon set, licensed for commercial use

Acceptance criteria  
• 12–15 slides maximum, each purpose-built—no filler.  
• Consistent typography, colour palette, and iconography aligned with my brand guide.  
• All numbers double-checked, citations included where needed.  
• Final files handed over within the agreed timeline and fully editable.

If you’ve raised money with decks before or have a design/finance hybrid skill set, I’d love to see examples.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express pitch-deck template) -> fill_text (business model, market, USP copy) -> asset_add_file (logo, brand assets, charts) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Raw financials, market reports, branding assets (client-supplied)
Business model (revenue streams, cost structure); TAM/SAM/SOM numbers; competitor positioning; USPs
12-15 slides max, on-brand typography/colour/iconography per brand guide
Deliverables: editable PowerPoint/Keynote + print/email PDF + source graphics/icon set
Brand guide (colours, fonts)
note: An Express pitch-deck template filled with the client's copy delivers the narrative; the bespoke TAM/SAM/SOM infographics, custom data charts and a reusable editable .pptx master are a human's finishing work (fill_text can't build charts or restyle).

title: High School Educational Presentation Design
desc:
I need a polished educational slide-deck created specifically for high-school students. The topic itself will be shared once we begin, but expect material that should feel current, visually engaging and easy for teenagers to digest in one class period. 

What I’m after:
• Around 8 slides that break the subject down into clear, digestible sections.  
• Consistent, modern styling with graphics, icons or short video snippets where appropriate to hold their attention.  
• Brief speaker notes under each slide so any teacher can pick it up and teach confidently.  

You’re free to build in PowerPoint, Google Slides or Keynote—whichever lets you achieve crisp visuals and smooth animations. Please keep text concise, use high-contrast colours for readability, and cite any images or data you include.

Once we’re happy with the overall flow I’ll request one revision pass for tweaks. Deliver the final deck plus an editable source file so future updates are easy.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express presentation template, modern, high-contrast) -> fill_text (sectioned topic copy) -> asset_add_file (cited images/icons) -> animate_design (optional smooth transitions) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The lesson topic + content (shared on start)
~8 slides broken into clear digestible sections; modern high-contrast styling
Graphics/icons (and short video snippets where appropriate)
Brief speaker notes under each slide
Editable source file for future updates
note: Express template + fill_text gives a designed 8-slide deck; per-slide speaker notes and a truly editable source (.pptx) are not produced by the template path — a human adds notes and hands off an editable file. fill_text sets copy only.

title: Event Pitch Deck PowerPoint Design
desc:
I’m preparing for an upcoming industry event and need a polished, professional-looking PowerPoint deck that will help me secure a panel-discussion slot and impress prospective clients in the room. The presentation has to carry a strong corporate tone—sleek, on-brand visuals, concise copy, and data points that speak directly to a client audience.

Here’s what I’m after:
• 8–10 slide deck, fully editable in PowerPoint  
• Consistent typography, colour palette and iconography in line with typical corporate standards  
• Visual storytelling that highlights why the panel discussion is valuable, how it aligns with the event’s theme, and the benefits attendees (especially clients) will gain  
• Space for stats, speaker bios, and a call-to-action I can customise as details finalise  
• Subtle animation or transitions to keep attention without distracting from the message  

I’ll supply the raw content (speaker info, key figures, brand guidelines) once we start; your job is to transform it into a client-facing narrative that looks boardroom-ready and delivers a clear, persuasive pitch. Please share a couple of sample slides or links to past corporate decks so I can gauge your style.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express pitch-deck template, corporate) -> fill_text (panel value prop, stats, speaker bios, CTA) -> asset_add_file (logo, brand assets) -> animate_design (subtle transitions) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Raw content: speaker info, key figures, brand guidelines (supplied on start)
8-10 slides; corporate tone; consistent type/colour/iconography
Space for stats, speaker bios, customisable CTA
Subtle animation/transitions
Deliverable: fully editable in PowerPoint
note: Express template + fill_text delivers the designed 8-10 slide pitch; exact brand-matched styling/iconography and a fully editable native .pptx are a human's finishing work — fill_text changes copy only.

title: Custom Print Image Design
desc:
I’m putting together a presentation and I need a single, polished image that is ready both for print and for the screen. The core of the job is straightforward: create a high-resolution graphic that can be sent to the printer without any extra adjustments and still look crisp when dropped into my slide deck.

Here’s what will help the project run smoothly:

• Final artwork delivered in CMYK (print) and RGB (presentation)  
• Minimum size: 300 DPI at 8.5" × 11" (or larger if you recommend it)  
• Editable source file (AI or PSD) plus a flattened PDF/PNG for quick use  

Feel free to suggest the best layout, color treatment, or typography to make the message pop. Once I approve the concept, a single round of minor tweaks should get us to the finish line.
task_type: Print/Slide Graphic — single 8.5x11 image, CMYK + RGB
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express poster/flyer template, 8.5x11 / letter) -> fill_text (the message copy) -> asset_add_file (logo / supporting imagery) -> document_convert_pdf (export flattened PDF) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The message/copy the graphic must convey
8.5x11 in (or larger), 300 DPI; deliver CMYK (print) + RGB (presentation)

title: White Tiger Tourism Infographic Design

My priority is visual clarity. Bold headings, an easy-to-follow data flow, and a palette that complements the tiger’s white-and-black coloring will keep the piece on brand. I’m developing a compact, fold-out brochure that will entice visitors to a beautiful tourist spot in North India. The single aim is to attract tourists, so every panel needs to burst with energy, colour and a clear call to explore. The spotlight is on outdoor adventures—think treks, river-runs, mountain drives and any other adrenaline-friendly activity the region offers. Cultural snippets or food tips can appear as supporting touches, but the core narrative must celebrate the open-air thrills.

I’ll supply location details, photos and basic copy points once we start. You’ll shape them into a smart foldable layout that prints cleanly and reads effortlessly in hand. Clean typography, high-resolution imagery and a logical information flow are essential. Feel free to suggest paper size or folding style beyond the classic tri-fold if it serves the design better.

When you respond, link to past work that proves you can handle tourist-focused print pieces or similarly persuasive collateral. No formal proposal needed—your portfolio should speak for itself.

Deliverables 
• Print-ready PDF (CMYK, 300 dpi, with bleeds)  
• Editable source file (AI, INDD or PSD)  
• A quick mock-up preview for approval before final export

I’ll review samples quickly and move forward with the designer whose style makes the destination impossible to resist.
task_type: Foldable Tourist Brochure — tri-fold, CMYK print
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (location photos) -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (pop colour) -> search_design (Express tri-fold brochure template) -> fill_text (panel copy: treks, river-runs, drives) -> asset_add_file (logo) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (CMYK print PDF)
inputs: Location details + copy points (provided once started)
High-resolution destination photos (treks, river-runs, mountain drives)
Logo + brand colours/fonts — confirm
Format: fold-out (tri-fold or other) — open to suggestion
Deliverable: print-ready PDF (CMYK, 300dpi, with bleeds) + editable source (AI/INDD/PSD) + mock-up preview
Panel info flow / fold style
note: Express brochure template + fill_text places copy/photos and exports a PDF; true CMYK/300dpi-with-bleeds pre-press setup and the editable AI/INDD source file need a human in Express/InDesign.

title: Graphic design
desc: Hi, I'm looking for a designer to turn my document into a professional digital guide/PDF. I don't want it to look like a basic document. I want it to be visually appealing, easy to read, and feel like a premium resource. What I'm looking for: • Professional cover page • Clean and modern layout • Consistent branding, colors, and fonts • Infographics to simplify information • Icons, graphics, and visual elements throughout • Tables, comparison charts, and checklists where needed • Callout boxes for important tips and key information • Clickable table of contents and links • Mobile-friendly design The content is focused on healthcare/nursing education and career pathways, so experience designing eBooks, guides, workbooks, or digital products is a plus. Final deliverables: • Editable Canva file (preferred) • High-quality PDF • Mobile-optimized version Please share samples of similar digital products you've designed.
task_type: Premium Digital Guide / eBook PDF — healthcare/nursing
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (source document) -> search_design (Express ebook/guide cover + interior templates) -> fill_text (sections, callouts, checklists) -> asset_add_file (icons/graphics) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (clickable TOC/links)
inputs: Source document content (healthcare/nursing education + career pathways)
Cover page + modern multi-page layout
Infographics, icons, tables/comparison charts, checklists, callout boxes
Clickable TOC + links; mobile-friendly
Brand colours/fonts + logo — confirm
Deliverable: editable file (Canva preferred) + high-quality PDF + mobile version
note: Connector builds the cover + section pages from Express templates and exports PDF; assembling the full premium multi-page guide with consistent custom infographics and a clickable TOC across all pages is finished by a human (no custom multi-page authoring tool).

title: Design a white paper for a branded ingredient (Nutraceutical / Supplement Industry)
desc: We're looking for an experienced designer to lay out and design a scientific white paper for a calcium ingredient brand. The content is already written and edited. This is purely a design project. The ideal candidate has experience designing B2B scientific or technical documents in the health, nutrition, or supplement space. We want something that feels credible, authoritative, and data-forward, with strong typography, dynamic layout, pull-quote treatments, color blocking, and infographic-style data callouts where appropriate. We will provide reference documents from comparable brands in our industry so you have a clear sense of the aesthetic and tone we're after. What we need: Full layout and design of white paper Typography selection appropriate for scientific/B2B documents Integration of photography, icons, and data callout elements Final deliverable in print-ready PDF and editable source file (InDesign preferred) Ideal candidate: Strong portfolio of scientific, medical, or nutraceutical white papers or reports Experience with B2B supplement or ingredient brands a big plus Fast turnaround preferred Please include relevant samples from your portfolio when applying. Note: Compensation is flexible and will be tied to experience. Feel free to submit proposals below or above the listed budget.
task_type: Scientific White Paper Layout — calcium ingredient (B2B)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (written content + reference docs + photography) -> search_design (Express report/whitepaper cover + interior template) -> fill_text (copy, pull-quotes, data callouts) -> asset_add_file (icons/charts) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (print-ready)
inputs: Final written + edited white-paper content (provided)
Reference docs from comparable brands (aesthetic/tone)
Photography, icons, data to integrate
Brand colours/fonts — confirm
Deliverable: print-ready PDF + editable source (InDesign preferred)
Pull-quote treatments, colour blocking, infographic data callouts
note: Cover, stock/icons and template pages are doable; full multi-page editorial white-paper layout with bespoke pull-quote/colour-block treatments (InDesign) is finished by a human — connector has no from-scratch multi-page authoring.

title: Need Graphic Designers for Course Workbook
desc: Organization: Giving Hope Finding Hope (Non-Profit) Project Type: Contract / Freelance Summary Giving Hope Finding Hope, a non-profit dedicated to supporting individuals aging out of foster care and those from under-resourced communities, is seeking skilled and creative Graphic Designers to develop visual assets for our different life-skills courses. We are building several standalone educational courses (not one module-based curriculum), and each course needs clear, engaging, youth-friendly graphics to support both teachers and students. These courses include topics such as: • Financial Literacy • Hygiene & Self-Care • Transportation • Housing • Home Maintenance • Cooking & Nutrition • Etiquette & Communication • Career & Academic Success …and more. Designers will help bring educational content to life through accessible visuals that make these courses easier to understand for teens, young adults, and older learners. ________________________________________ What We Are Looking For We need graphic designers with experience in one or more of the following: 1. Educational & Instructional Graphics • Infographics • Diagrams & flowcharts • Step-by-step visuals • Icons & symbols • Worksheets and handouts 2. Course Branding & Layout Design • Consistent course theme/identity • Page layouts for workbooks • Section dividers, headers, and visual cues • Canva or Adobe-based course templates 3. Workbook & Teaching Material Graphics • Teacher guides visual layouts • Student workbook pages • Printable templates • Role-play cards • Visual checklists 4. Character or Scenario Illustrations (Bonus) • Simple, diverse characters (e.g., Samuel & Ashley from our storyline) • Scene-based illustrations for real-life examples • Light educational cartoons About Giving Hope Finding Hope Giving Hope Finding Hope is a Christian-based nonprofit dedicated to empowering individuals aging out of foster care and those from under-resourced communities. Through education, support, and practical life-skills training, we aim to equip each person with the tools they need to thrive as independent adults.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express workbook / worksheet / infographic templates) -> fill_text (course content, steps, checklists) -> asset_add_file (icons/logo) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (printable)
inputs: Course content per topic (Financial Literacy, Hygiene, Transportation, Housing, Cooking, etc.)
Deliverables: infographics, diagrams/flowcharts, step-by-step visuals, icons, worksheets/handouts, section dividers, workbook page layouts
Consistent course theme/identity
Brand colours/fonts + logo — confirm
Youth-friendly, accessible style
Bonus characters (Samuel & Ashley) — OUT OF SCOPE for connector
note: Per-page infographics, worksheets and icons come from Express templates; full multi-page workbook assembly and the bonus diverse-character illustrations are human/illustrator work.

title: Graphic Designer for Nonprofit Annual Report
desc: We’re looking for a highly skilled graphic designer to create a polished, visually compelling Annual Report for our nonprofit organization, the Healing and CPTSD Foundation . This is not a basic document formatting project. We are looking for someone who can create a professional, modern, emotionally resonant annual report that feels aligned with high-quality nonprofit and advocacy organizations. The report will include: Our mission and impact 2025 milestones and accomplishments Advocacy and systems change initiatives Community and awareness growth Financial overview Strategic priorities and future goals Photos, graphics, statistics, and branded visual elements We already have: The written content Branding direction Logos and visual assets Data and statistics What we need: Strong layout and editorial design Clean typography and visual hierarchy Professional infographic/stat graphics Consistent branding throughout Print-quality and digital PDF delivery A report that feels elevated, credible, and emotionally impactful Style direction: Modern nonprofit / advocacy aesthetic Clean but visually engaging Editorial / magazine-quality feel Trauma-informed and human-centered in tone Professional enough for sponsors, partners, media, and grant opportunities Deliverables: Final high-resolution PDF Editable source files (Canva, Adobe InDesign, or Illustrator preferred) Optimized web version if possible Please include in your proposal: Relevant annual report or nonprofit portfolio examples Experience with editorial/report design Estimated turnaround time Preferred design platform/software Bonus if you have experience with: Nonprofits Mental health or advocacy organizations Infographic/report storytelling Data visualization We are looking for someone who can take information and transform it into a visually powerful report that reflects the scale and credibility of our work.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (content, photos, data, logo) -> search_design (Express annual-report cover + interior + infographic templates) -> fill_text (mission, milestones, financials, stats) -> asset_add_file (stat charts) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (print + web)
inputs: Written content (provided): mission/impact, 2025 milestones, advocacy, community growth, financials, strategic priorities
Logos + visual assets + branding direction (provided)
Data/statistics for infographic stat-graphics
Photos to place
Deliverable: high-res print PDF + editable source + optimized web version
Modern, editorial/magazine, trauma-informed tone
note: Stat infographics, cover and template spreads are doable; the full multi-page magazine-quality report layout is assembled by a human — connector can't author a bespoke multi-page editorial document.

title: Creating a Canva Presentation for a Webinar for an exclusive & high ticket river cruise experience
desc: Looking for someone to make a presentation for a 1 hour webinar for the Ultimate Foodie Cruise (UltimateFoodieCruise.com): an exclusive, fully customized foodie cruise experience on the Rhine River. I have a full script, notes, and rough timeline for the webinar. I will need some infographics (to compare our cabin pricing with what it would cost to do this trip independently) and pricing with and without the discount code (to increase sales in the first 48 hours), an aesthetic that matches the landing page, and a high end yet approachable vibe that lends to closing high ticket sales. There are also notes that need to be added to each slide, which will be provided. I will give you the link to the cruise line and this itinerary, the link to the landing page (provided above), Dropbox access with scenery, food, and traveler pictures and videos to use throughout. I need someone with an eye who can take these components and turn it into one streamlined piece that is eye catching, drives FOMO, and helps convert sales.
task_type: Webinar Presentation + Pricing Infographics — Foodie Cruise
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (scenery/food/traveler photos + videos from Dropbox) -> search_design (Express presentation template) -> fill_text (slide copy from script) -> search_design (Express comparison-infographic template) -> fill_text (cabin pricing vs independent; with/without discount code) -> asset_add_file (slide notes) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Full webinar script, notes, and rough 1-hour timeline (provided)
Infographic 1: cabin-pricing comparison vs doing the trip independently
Infographic 2: pricing with vs without the 48-hour discount code
Dropbox assets: Rhine River scenery, food, and traveler photos/videos
Landing-page aesthetic to match (UltimateFoodieCruise.com) — high-end yet approachable
Per-slide speaker notes (provided)
Brand colours/fonts — confirm; deliverable format (Canva/PPTX) — confirm
note: Express presentation + comparison-infographic templates with fill_text place the script copy, pricing tables and photos; matching the exact landing-page aesthetic/brand font and the FOMO 'high-end' polish needs a human in Express, and fill_text changes copy only.

title: Create Editable PowerPoint presentation with animation from image based slides
desc: Job description We are looking for a professional PowerPoint designer who can recreate an existing image based presentation into a fully editable, high quality PowerPoint file with clean layout, professional animation, and modern visual design. The current presentation is available as images or screenshots. The selected freelancer must convert each slide into an editable PowerPoint format, not just place the image inside PowerPoint. Scope of work The freelancer will recreate the presentation using editable PowerPoint elements, including: Editable text boxes Editable shapes and cards Editable icons and graphic elements Editable tables, charts, timelines, workflow diagrams, and infographics Professional slide layout and alignment Consistent colors, fonts, spacing, and branding Smooth slide transitions Simple professional animations for text, cards, icons, charts, and process flows Master slide setup for consistent branding Final PowerPoint file ready for presentation and future editing Design style required The design should follow a modern corporate presentation style with: Clean and professional layout High fidelity UI and UX inspired presentation style Figma style visual structure Pixel perfect alignment Modern dashboard style elements where needed Professional use of icons, cards, tabs, charts, and section dividers Consistent brand color system Clear visual hierarchy Suitable design for investors, donors, government institutions, and business partners Animation requirements The PowerPoint should include professional but not excessive animation, such as: Fade in text animation Smooth entrance animation for cards and icons Step by step process animation Animated arrows or workflow movement where suitable Section transition effects Dashboard or data reveal animation Clean closing slide animation Animations must be simple, elegant, and suitable for formal business presentations. Deliverables The freelancer must provide: Fully editable PowerPoint file in PPTX format All slides recreated from the image based presentation Editable slide master Editable charts, shapes, icons, and text Animated version of the presentation Clean final version without design errors PDF export version Source assets used, where applicable Important requirements Please do not simply paste the image screenshots into PowerPoint. Each slide must be rebuilt as an editable presentation using PowerPoint objects, text layers, shapes, icons, tables, charts, and design components. The final presentation must allow us to easily edit text, colors, icons, layout, and animation later. Freelancer skills required The ideal freelancer should have strong experience in: Microsoft PowerPoint advanced design Corporate presentation design PowerPoint animation Pitch deck design Image to editable PowerPoint conversion Figma style UI presentation design Business proposal and donor presentation formatting Infographic design Slide master design Professional layout and visual storytelling Application instructions Please include the following in your proposal: Examples of previous editable PowerPoint presentations Examples of animated PowerPoint work Experience converting image based slides into editable PPTX Your estimated delivery time Your price for recreating the presentation Confirmation that all slides will be fully editable Preferred freelancer We prefer a freelancer who can understand corporate, government, donor, and technology sector presentations. Experience with professional company profiles, software platform presentations, investment decks, or institutional proposals will be an advantage. Final output standard The final PowerPoint should look professional, modern, and polished, suitable for presentation to senior decision makers, investors, government officials, donors, and institutional partners.

title: Brand Designer Needed: Build Complete Brochure System for Architectural Products Company
desc: **Brand Designer Needed: Build Complete Brochure System for Architectural Products Company** We're Sundance Architectural Products (sdap.com), an Orlando-based specialty contractor that designs, engineers, fabricates, and installs exterior architectural elements, ACM cladding, canopies, pergolas, themed structures, sunshades, and more. Our clients are architects, general contractors, commercial developers, and theme park developers. We need a designer to build out our complete brochure system from the ground up. We have finalized brand assets (logo, colors, fonts) and ~50–100 high-res project photos ready to provide. --- **SCOPE OF WORK** **1. Brand Foundation** - Create master brand template system using our finalized brand assets - Organize and tag our project photography library - Build reusable design components / layout grids that ensure consistency across the suite **2. Brochure Design Suite** - **Product-specific brochures (2–4 pages each):** Estimated 10–12 brochures covering ACM cladding, cabanas, pergolas, sunshades, themed structures, metal canopies, polycarbonate canopies, Signs, and decorative panels. - **Audience-specific brochures (4–6 pages each):** Four tailored brochures — Architects, General Contractors, Commercial Property Owners. - **Full Capability Brochure (12–16 pages):** Comprehensive company overview showing our full product/service range. **3. Deliverables** - Print-ready PDFs with bleed marks and CMYK color profile - Digital PDFs optimized for email and web - Editable master source files (in whatever tool you propose — see below) - A short Loom walkthrough showing us how to make basic edits ourselves (text swaps, photo swaps, contact info updates) --- **IMPORTANT: TOOL FLEXIBILITY** We're open to your recommendation on design software (InDesign, Canva, Affinity Publisher, etc.). However, **a major requirement is that we be able to make minor in-house edits without a designer.** In your proposal, please tell us: - What tool you'd use and why - What's required on our end to make basic edits afterward (software subscriptions, training, etc.) - Trade-offs of your recommended approach --- **REQUIRED SKILLS** - Strong portfolio of B2B brochures, ideally in architecture, construction, building products, or industrial design - Print production expertise (bleeds, CMYK, resolution, prepress) - Clean, architectural design sensibility, minimalist, photo-forward, no stock-template feel - Excellent typography and grid systems - Multi-page document design experience **NICE TO HAVE** - Background designing for architecture/AEC industry - Experience writing or editing marketing copy (we can provide raw content but appreciate refinement) --- **TO APPLY, PLEASE INCLUDE:** 1. Links to 2–3 brochure projects from your portfolio (B2B or architecture-related preferred) 2. Your recommended design tool and a brief rationale 3. Your plan for how we'll handle in-house edits after handoff 4. Your proposed approach: What order would you tackle this in? 5. Your estimated timeline 6. Your fixed-price quote (or hourly with cap) 7. One sentence: What's the first question you'd ask us before starting? Thank you and looking forward to seeing your work. Christopher Sundance Architectural Products

title: Graphic Designer | Corporate Design
desc: Flow Keeping is looking for a reliable freelance graphic designer to support the creation of branded marketing assets for our funnel, Google Ads campaigns, social media content, landing pages, and ongoing digital marketing needs. We are building out a full marketing system and need someone who can help us create high-quality visuals that are clean, modern, polished, and aligned with our brand. This role is ideal for a designer who understands how to design for marketing performance, not just aesthetics. The right person should be able to take direction, work from existing brand assets, follow creative briefs, and produce designs that feel consistent across multiple channels. We need someone who can move quickly, communicate clearly, and help us get a strong volume of useful creative without exceeding the budget. This will begin as a part-time role with a maximum of 20 hours per week. If the working relationship is successful, this may become an ongoing role for future campaigns, content launches, ad creative, and brand development. About Flow Keeping Flow Keeping helps customers better understand and manage their water usage through smart, practical, and easy-to-understand solutions. Our brand needs to feel trustworthy, modern, helpful, clean, and professional. The design style should support a business that is building credibility, educating customers, and converting visitors through paid ads, organic social content, landing pages, and funnel-based marketing assets. Main Responsibilities Create polished, professional marketing graphics for Flow Keeping. Design assets that support our funnel, Google Ads campaigns, and social media content. Work from creative briefs, written content, examples, and brand direction. Create multiple design variations when needed for testing. Keep all creative consistent with the Flow Keeping brand. Organize final files clearly so they can be used by our marketing team. Deliver editable source files when requested. Communicate clearly about timelines, design questions, and project needs. Use time efficiently and stay within the approved weekly hour limit. Ideal Candidate The ideal designer should have experience with: Graphic design for digital marketing Social media design Google Ads or paid ad creative Landing page graphics Brand-consistent design systems Canva, Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, Figma, or similar tools Creating templates for ongoing marketing use Designing for conversions, not only visual appeal Working with small businesses or startups with limited budgets We are especially interested in designers who can create clean, modern, trustworthy visuals without making the brand feel too generic, overly corporate, or cluttered. Design Style We Are Looking For The creative should feel: Clean Modern Trustworthy Professional Easy to understand Conversion-focused Organized Approachable Consistent with the Flow Keeping brand Weekly Workflow We expect the designer to work up to 20 hours per week. Each week may include a combination of: Social media graphics Ad creative Funnel graphics Revisions to existing designs Template development Campaign-specific creative Brand asset cleanup We will prioritize the most important assets first so the budget is used wisely. Budget and Time Management Because our marketing budget is already stretched, we need a designer who is comfortable working within clear priorities and time limits. We are not looking for someone who spends excessive time on unnecessary design exploration. We need someone who can understand the direction, create strong work efficiently, and help us produce the assets needed to support active marketing campaigns. To keep the project manageable, we will: Limit work to 20 hours per week Assign tasks by priority Start with the most urgent funnel, ad, and social assets Approve larger batches of work before additional hours are used Focus on reusable templates wherever possible Avoid unnecessary redesigns unless they directly improve performance or brand consistency Required Skills Graphic Design Digital Marketing Design Social Media Design Ad Creative Design Canva Adobe Photoshop Adobe Illustrator Figma Brand Design Marketing Funnel Design Layout Design Template Design Not Required but a plus Skills Experience designing Google Ads creative Experience with conversion-focused design Experience with startup or small business marketing Experience creating design systems or reusable templates Experience designing for home services, utilities, SaaS, smart technology, or consumer products Basic understanding of marketing funnels Basic understanding of paid ad creative testing Deliverables Depending on the project, deliverables may include: PNG files JPG files PDF files Editable Canva files Adobe source files Figma files Organized folders of completed creative Multiple size variations for ad placements Social media post sizes Google Ads display sizes Reusable branded templates Application Requirements Please include the following in your proposal: A short introduction explaining why you are a good fit for this role. A link to your portfolio. Examples of social media graphics, ad creative, or funnel-related design work. Your hourly rate. The design tools you prefer to use. Your availability each week. A brief explanation of how you manage design work efficiently when a client has a limited budget.

desc: We are seeking freelance graphic designers to collaborate with individual clients (students) on short, paid design tasks. This project is part of a university learning activity in which students prepare a design brief and work collaboratively with a designer to complete a real design deliverable. Each designer will be paired with one student client. Project Scope: • Deliver one completed design based on the student’s choice — choose only one of the following options: a) A simple logo or brand mark b) A social media graphic and caption c) A one-page flyer or poster • Provide an initial version and one revision based on feedback • Communicate with the client to clarify requirements Work Requirements: • Total expected effort: up to 2 hours, including communication • Communication may occur via Upwork Messages or short calls • Deliverables should be completed within the project window Required Feedback (Important for the Project): • After completing the design task, you will be required to complete a short feedback survey about your experience collaborating with the student client and working through the task. • Your feedback will not be shared individually; it will be used only in anonymized, aggregate form to support student learning and help inform improvements in the educational program. • Completing this survey is a required part of the task and therefore included in the project’s fixed price. Payment & Duration: • Fixed-price: USD 25 per project (covers design effort and required feedback survey) • Project duration: 1 March – 15 April • Work should be completed within this timeframe Who Should Apply: • Graphic designers experienced with small design tasks • Clear communication via Upwork Messages • Ability to meet the expected delivery timeline
