task_type: High-volume Batch Auto-Tone / Preset Apply & Export (~300,000 images)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file -> image_apply_preset (or image_apply_auto_tone) -> image_adjust_exposure -> asset_preview_file (batched per image, preserving filenames)
inputs: ~300,000 images / 131 GB to process
The preset/look to apply uniformly (confirm — they reference Lightroom presets)
Must preserve original filenames and folder structure
CSV report of input vs output files requested
note: Per-image auto-tone/preset apply + export is fully connector-doable; note this is automation-scale (Lightroom-Classic catalog management, folder org and CSV manifest are the client's workflow wrapper, not an editing step).

title: Title: Wedding Photo Editor Needed – Lightroom Preset-Based Editing – Ongoing Work
desc: Hi, I’m looking for a reliable wedding photo editor to help me with ongoing wedding galleries. The editing style is already defined. I will provide my own Lightroom preset, reference images and clear editing guidelines. The job is mainly to apply the preset and make basic corrections to keep the full gallery consistent. This is not advanced retouching or Photoshop work. I’m looking for clean, natural and consistent wedding editing in Lightroom. Project details: Each wedding gallery usually includes around 600–800 photos. The fixed price for each full wedding gallery is €75. The work includes: Applying my Lightroom preset Exposure correction White balance adjustment Basic color correction Contrast and tone adjustments Cropping and straightening when needed Keeping the full wedding gallery visually consistent Matching the overall look to the reference images provided Delivering the edited Lightroom catalog or updated Smart Previews The work does NOT include: Advanced skin retouching Photoshop editing Object removal Background replacement Heavy local adjustments Creative color grading from scratch Editing each image individually in a complex way Workflow: I usually work with Lightroom Classic. I will provide: Lightroom catalog or Smart Previews My own preset Reference images Editing guidelines Notes about the style and corrections needed You will need to return the edited Lightroom catalog / Smart Previews with all adjustments applied. Style: The final gallery should look natural, elegant, warm, clean and consistent. The goal is not to change the style, but to keep my existing wedding style consistent across the full gallery. Important: Please only apply if you are comfortable editing 600–800 wedding photos for the fixed price of €75 per gallery. This can become long-term work if the results are good, as I regularly shoot weddings. Please send: Examples of wedding photo edits you have made Your experience with Lightroom Classic Your usual delivery time for a 600–800 photo wedding gallery Confirmation that you are comfortable working with my preset and Smart Previews Confirmation that you understand this is preset-based editing with basic corrections, not advanced retouching Thank you.
task_type: Wedding Gallery — preset apply + basic corrections (600–800 photos)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file -> image_apply_preset (client's preset) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_color_temperature -> image_adjust_exposure -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast -> image_crop_and_resize / image_auto_straighten -> asset_preview_file (per image)
inputs: 600–800 wedding photos per gallery
Client's own Lightroom preset to apply as the base
Reference images defining the natural/elegant/warm look to match
Per-image: WB, exposure, basic colour correction, contrast/tone, crop & straighten where needed
Explicitly NO advanced retouch/object removal/creative grade-from-scratch
note: All the requested edits (preset, exposure, WB, contrast, crop, straighten, consistency) are connector-doable; gap is delivery FORMAT — client wants the edited Lightroom catalog / Smart Previews returned, whereas the connector outputs rendered image files, not a .lrcat.

title: LOOKING FOR: Experienced Lightroom Classic Editor – Long-term Collaboration
desc: 'm a brand photographer based in Germany. I photograph female entrepreneurs and coaches. I'm looking for a reliable, talented photo editor for long-term collaboration. --- WHAT YOU'LL DO: → Edit 120+photos per shoot in Lightroom Classic → Work with my preset as a base → Deliver within 5-7 days after receiving the files --- WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR: → Strong experience with Lightroom Classic → Clean, modern editing style → Eye for skin tones and color grading → Reliable, fast, and communicative → Long-term mindset – I want someone who grows with my brand --- WHAT YOU'LL GET: → Regular work (multiple shoots per month) → Clear briefings every time → A collaborator who values quality and good communication --- TO APPLY: Please send 3-5 examples of your portrait/brand editing work. Looking forward to working with you! ✨
task_type: Brand/Portrait Gallery — preset-based colour grade (120+ photos/shoot)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file -> image_apply_preset (client's preset base) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_color_temperature -> image_adjust_hsl -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast -> asset_preview_file (per image)
inputs: 120+ portrait/brand photos per shoot (female entrepreneurs/coaches)
Client's preset to use as the base look
Clean, modern editing style with good skin tones and consistent colour grading
Brief per shoot defining the look; deliver within 5–7 days
note: Apply client preset + auto-tone + temperature/HSL/vibrance grading across the set is fully connector-doable. (If skin-tone work crosses into per-blemish retouch a human finishes, but the brief frames it as preset-based grading.)

title: Simple image editing
desc: The attached photo of the crossback chair on a grey background is one of my in stock products which has been shot professionally in the UK. The chair on a white background is one of my made to order products. I am looking for somebody to recreate all of my made to order product images so they are on the same background, facing in the same direction and are to the same scale as my in stock products. Essentially my made to order products need to be presented the same as my in stock products. I believe using AI this is a simple task. If you are interested please get in touch.
task_type: Product Image Standardisation — match made-to-order shots to in-stock (cutout + grey bg + rescale)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file -> image_select_subject -> image_remove_background -> change_background_color (match the in-stock grey) -> image_crop_and_resize (match scale/framing) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Made-to-order product photos shot on white (e.g. crossback chair)
Reference in-stock image on grey background to match (exact grey hex — sample from the reference)
Target: same background, same scale/framing as the in-stock set
Note which products face the wrong way and need re-orienting
note: Cutout, replacing with the matching grey background, and rescaling to match the in-stock set are connector-doable; making a product 'face the same direction' requires a horizontal flip / re-shoot which the connector has no tool for — a human handles any reorientation.

title: Instagram Post Graphics & Reels
desc:
I need fresh, scroll-stopping content for my Instagram feed. Your main focus will be on designing single-image post graphics that look instantly appealing while fitting my brand aesthetic. Each graphic must include well-placed text overlays so key messages pop without overwhelming the visuals.  

Alongside the static posts, I’d also like light video editing for short reels—simple cuts, on-brand colour grading and the same text overlay style—so the grid and reels feel cohesive. All files should be sized correctly for Instagram (1080×1080 for posts, 1080×1920 for reels) and delivered in both ready-to-publish formats (PNG/MP4) and editable source files.

If you have a knack for turning ideas into engaging visuals and can keep a consistent tone across graphics and videos, let’s talk about the first batch right away.
task_type: Instagram Single-Image Post Graphics — 1080x1080 with text overlays
mcp_workflow: search_design (Instagram post template 1080x1080 + fillDescription) -> fill_text -> asset_add_file (brand imagery) -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (on-brand grade of supplied photos) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Brand aesthetic / colour palette and fonts to match
Key message copy for each post's text overlay
Photo(s) or imagery to feature per post
Output: 1080x1080 PNG (ready-to-publish) + editable source
note: Static post graphics come from an Express template (fill_text swaps copy only — not the exact brand font/size/overlay placement, which needs a human in Express). The secondary 'light reel editing with the same overlay style' is a narrative video edit the connector cannot deliver — scope kept to the primary static-graphic deliverable.

title: Print-Ready Advertising Board Graphics
desc:
I’m preparing a set of 1200 mm × 300 mm advertising boards and need each layout turned into a press-ready file. You’ll receive a folder of photographs in mixed proportions along with our logo, tagline and contact details; the job is to weave those elements into a balanced, eye-catching composition that holds sharpness at full size.

Because these will go straight to the sign printer, I’ll need:
• A CMYK, 300 dpi file at full dimensions with 3 mm bleed all round (PDF or AI preferred, with fonts outlined)  
• A web-resolution JPEG for internal approval  
• Any working files in case we update the campaign later

Please allow for subtle colour correction so every photo looks consistent across each board. If you spot any resolution issues in the images I send, flag them early so we can swap them out. The image of the cow should be paired with the shook cow text

I’m ready to supply the assets immediately and can answer layout or brand-guideline questions as you go. Looking forward to seeing how you arrange the shots and bring the design together.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (supplied photos) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_color_temperature -> image_adjust_highlights -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> search_design (wide banner/signage template + fillDescription) -> fill_text -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Folder of mixed-proportion photographs (incl. the cow image to pair with the 'shook cow' text)
Logo, tagline and contact details to typeset
Final size 1200x300mm with 3mm bleed all round
Output spec: CMYK 300dpi PDF/AI with fonts outlined + web JPEG for approval + working files
note: Connector does the per-photo colour correction for cross-board consistency; the precise multi-photo composition into the banner and the CMYK / 300dpi / 3mm-bleed / outlined-font print pre-press (PDF/AI) are beyond Express template fill — a human finishes the press-ready layout.

title: Canvas Eye Detail Enhancement
desc:
I need assistance with a detail enhancement on a canvas painting I created. The focus is on the eyes of a face in the artwork.

Requirements:
- Correct color tones of the eyes
- Align the left eye for accuracy

Ideal Skills & Experience:
- Proficiency in color correction
- Experience in painting detail enhancement
- Attention to detail and precision

Please provide examples of similar work done.
task_type: Artwork Detail Colour Correction — eye tones on a canvas painting
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file -> image_select_by_prompt ('the eyes') -> image_adjust_hsl -> image_adjust_single_color_saturation -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast -> asset_preview_file
inputs: High-res photo/scan of the canvas painting
The eye area to target and the desired corrected eye colour/tone
Note that the left eye also needs geometric alignment (positional fix)
note: Connector can colour-correct the eye tones via a targeted selection; 'aligning the left eye for accuracy' is a geometric warp/reposition (liquify-class) the connector has no tool for — a human finishes that step.

title: Convert JPEG and PNG company logos to EPS & SVG Vector files
desc: We are looking for a graphic designer to convert approximately 6–8 company logos from JPEG/JPG/PNG format into clean vector files. Deliverables: EPS files SVG files High-quality vector recreation of each logo Additional task: One existing color logo (black, white, and yellow) needs to be converted into a clean black-and-white EPS/SVG version. Requirements: Experience with Adobe Illustrator or similar vector design tools Ability to manually recreate logos when auto-tracing does not produce professional results Final files must be clean, scalable, and editable Attention to detail and accurate font/color matching Please include: Examples of previous logo/vector conversion work Estimated turnaround time Confirmation that all files will be delivered in editable vector format All logos are company-owned assets. This is a straightforward project with potential for additional graphic design work in the future. The expectation is that this would take a week or less to complete.
task_type: Logo Vectorization — 6–8 JPEG/PNG logos to EPS & SVG (+1 to B&W)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (EPS) -> document_render_vector (SVG) [per logo]; for the B&W variant: image_adjust_hsl/monochromatic_tint -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector
inputs: 6–8 company logos supplied as JPEG/JPG/PNG (company-owned assets)
One existing black/white/yellow colour logo to also output as a clean black-and-white version
Deliverables: clean, scalable, editable EPS + SVG per logo
Accurate font/colour matching expected
note: image_vectorize auto-traces each logo to clean vector then exports EPS/SVG. The brief notes manual recreation may be needed where auto-trace is imperfect (esp. exact font matching) — connector auto-traces only, so a human may touch up tricky fonts.

title: Vectorize Logos & Packaging
desc:
*** DO NOT BID IF YOU CANNOT DO IT IN THE GIVEN PRICE RANGE ***

I already have AI-generated artwork for my "two" tea brands and one ornamental style logo but it is still in raster form and not ready for production. I need everything redrawn as clean, fully-editable vectors so I can scale them for any application without loss of quality.

Scope  
• Convert the existing logos, standup pouch mock-up into precise vector artwork.  
• Preserve every colour, gradient, and detail exactly as in the original concept while improving line sharpness and overall balance.  
• Create a matching front-facing product label that fits naturally with the new vector style.  

Files & delivery  
Please deliver the final artwork in AI, SVG, and PDF formats. Each file should be neatly organised in layers, include outlined and editable text versions, and be ready for immediate print or digital use. Because I don’t have fixed sizes yet, work with standard retail package dimensions; I’ll adjust later if needed, so keep the artwork flexible. They should easily open in affinity suite, davinci resolve, canva.  Logo to be delivered in various sizes as required by the social media platforms. 

Acceptance  
I’ll approve once the logo scales cleanly to billboard size, the box and pouch dielines align correctly in the PDF, and the label prints crisp on a desktop test sheet.

If this sounds straightforward to you and you’re comfortable refining AI-generated art into production-ready vectors, let’s get started.

Style of Logos: 1. minimalist with strong typography
2. Floral & Watercolor Classy Vintage. 
3. Ornamental Emblem Style

Total no of logos: 3
Total no of stand up pouch packaging design: 5

I have attached file so that you can see the scope of work. 

*** DO NOT BID IF YOU CANNOT DO IT IN THE GIVEN PRICE RANGE ***

I will only interact with people who have written 'I have read the brief' in their bid.
task_type: Vectorize 3 AI-generated raster logos → AI/SVG/PDF (tea-brand minimalist, floral-watercolour vintage, ornamental emblem)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (each raster logo PNG/JPG) -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (AI + SVG + PDF) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: 3 source raster logo files: (1) minimalist strong-typography tea brand, (2) floral & watercolour vintage tea brand, (3) ornamental emblem logo
Output formats: AI + SVG + PDF, each with editable/outlined text versions (note: auto-vectorize outputs paths, not live editable text)
Standard retail package dimensions to scale to — confirm; brief says sizes not yet fixed
Social-platform export sizes required for the logo — confirm exact set
The 5 stand-up pouch packaging designs + the new front-facing product label are a SEPARATE bespoke deliverable (not in connector scope)
note: Connector auto-vectorizes and exports the 3 existing raster logos to AI/SVG/PDF. A human in Illustrator must do the rest: create the new matching product label, design the 5 stand-up pouch packs with aligned dielines, and preserve exact gradients/layers/editable-text fidelity (image_vectorize flattens gradients and outlines text).

title: Indesign album- drop in images
desc: Using the indesign album templates I will send you please drop in the 60-75 images of a clients family that I will send you. I will send you the images but you may need to zoom in on some images to make them look aesthetically pleasing to the client. Typically I like the entire image to show but some will be zoomed in for emotion. I will give you some guidance on that so no worries. The client comes in this week so I would need the pdf’s of the final indesign file pretty quickly. Note that there may be some tweaking and back and forth needed when myself or the client decides on the final album. She may want to move some images around or delete some, etc. It’s really cut and dry. I know how to use in design I just need someone quicker and smarter than me in indesign to do it.
task_type: Family Album — drop 60–75 client images into supplied .indd album templates, export PDFs
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (client .indd album templates + the 60–75 family images) -> document_merge_data_layout (map image filenames to the album page frames per a CSV) -> document_render_layout (export each filled album spread to PDF) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Client's InDesign album template files (.indd/.idml) — the album page designs to drop images into
60–75 client family photos (provide as a folder + filenames)
A frame-to-image mapping (which photo goes in which frame on which page) for the data-merge CSV
Per-image framing notes from the client (which images to show full vs zoom-in 'for emotion')
Output: print-ready album PDFs per spread for client review
Turnaround is tight (client visits this week) — confirm number of albums/spreads
note: document_merge_data_layout fits photos to frames by fit rules and document_render_layout exports the PDFs, but the per-image aesthetic 'zoom-in for emotion' cropping and the client's move/swap/delete back-and-forth are finished by a human in InDesign.

title: Interactive PDF Workbook Formatting
desc:
I'm looking for someone to format self-development workbooks into interactive PDFs. The workbooks will include:

- Fillable text fields (multiple lines per answer)
- Section dividers

Ideal skills and experience:
- Proficiency in PDF editing software (e.g., Adobe Acrobat)
- Experience creating interactive PDFs
- Attention to detail and a good eye for detail
task_type: Interactive PDF Workbook — add multi-line fillable text fields + section dividers
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (client workbook PDF) -> document_convert_pdf (add fillable form fields: multi-line text fields per answer, section-divider breaks) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Client's self-development workbook PDF(s) to make interactive
Where each fillable answer field goes + how many lines (multi-line) per prompt
Section-divider placement/labels between workbook sections
Output: interactive fillable PDF; confirm whether fields stay editable or get flattened on delivery

title: Fillable Commissioning PDF Form
desc:
I have an existing equipment-commissioning form for electrical systems that works perfectly on paper; I now need it reproduced as a fully fillable PDF. Every text field, checkbox, and signature line must mirror the original layout so the document looks identical when printed, yet allows users to:

• Type names and other details directly.  
• Add or delete extra commissioning rows with check boxes without breaking the formatting. 
• Tick or untick check boxes as required.

Please build the form so I can still tweak field labels later if our terminology changes, ideally using Adobe Acrobat or an equivalent tool that supports simple future edits. The finished file should be clean, free of hidden artefacts, and ready for immediate use in the field.
task_type: Fillable Commissioning PDF Form — text fields, checkboxes, signature lines mirroring a paper form
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (existing equipment-commissioning form, e.g. scanned/printed PDF) -> document_convert_pdf (build fillable form: text fields for names/details, checkboxes, signature lines positioned to mirror the original layout) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The existing electrical-systems commissioning form (the paper/PDF to reproduce as fillable)
Exact field map: text fields, checkbox positions, signature lines — must overlay the original layout 1:1
Field labels the client may want to re-edit later (keep labels as editable text)
Output: clean fillable PDF, no hidden artefacts, prints identical to the original
note: document_convert_pdf can reproduce the static fillable form (text fields, checkboxes, signature lines), but the requested dynamic 'add/delete extra commissioning rows without breaking formatting' is scripted (JavaScript) interactive behaviour a human finishes in Acrobat.

title: Adobe Acrobat Pro Expert Needed
desc: We are seeking an expert in Adobe Acrobat Pro to assist with PDF editing and document management tasks. i have documents that need to be cleaned up per line. i need someone with experinced with editing text and copying it with the same format
task_type: PDF text edit / clean-up preserving original formatting
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (client PDFs) -> document_convert_pdf (convert PDF to an editable format, e.g. Word, preserving layout/formatting) -> [human edits text per line] -> document_convert_pdf (re-export to PDF) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The client PDF documents that need per-line text clean-up
The specific text edits / what 'cleaned up per line' means (list of changes) — confirm; brief is vague
Requirement to preserve original formatting when copying/editing text
Output: cleaned PDF(s) matching original format
note: document_convert_pdf can round-trip the PDF to an editable format and back (preserving format), but the actual per-line text clean-up/copy-editing is manual human work; brief doesn't specify the exact edits.

title: Hockey Highlight Reel Creator
desc:
I run a platform dedicated to hockey enthusiasts and I’m looking to add dynamic highlight reels that capture the key moments of each game while weaving in brief analytical insights.  

Scope  
• Receive full-length game footage that I provide and distill it into a polished 3–5 minute highlight reel.  
• Showcase pivotal plays—goals, saves, power-plays, and momentum-shifting hits—while overlaying concise on-screen stats or commentary to give viewers instant context.  
• Maintain broadcast-quality editing with clean transitions, accurate time-stamps, and royalty-free soundtrack selections.  

What I’ll supply  
• Raw video files (HD) plus a rough list of event markers.  
• Team logos, lower-third templates, and any branded assets you’ll need.  

What I expect back  
• One master file in 1080p (MP4 or MOV) and web-ready snippets for social media (≤60 seconds).  
• A brief write-up (150-200 words) summarizing the game’s turning points, suitable for posting alongside the video.  
• Delivery of the first completed reel within a month from project start, with room for one revision round.  

Preferred tools include Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve—use whichever you’re most efficient in. If you love hockey and know how to keep fans on the edge of their seats through sharp editing and succinct analysis, I’d love to see samples of your previous sports work and discuss the schedule.
task_type: Hockey Highlight Reel — 3–5 min cut from full-game footage + ≤60s social snippets
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (full-length HD game footage) -> video_create_quick_cut (auto-cut a 3–5 min highlight reel of pivotal plays) -> video_resize (reframe ≤60s web-ready snippets for social) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Full-length HD hockey game footage (MP4/MOV)
Rough list of event markers (goals, saves, power-plays, momentum hits) to guide highlight selection
Team logos, lower-third templates, branded assets (for the human overlay step)
Royalty-free soundtrack choice (client/editor supplies)
Outputs: one 1080p master (MP4/MOV) + social snippets ≤60s
Note: the 150–200 word game write-up is a separate non-connector deliverable
note: video_create_quick_cut auto-selects highlights and video_resize makes the social cuts, but the on-screen stats/commentary overlays, branded lower-thirds, clean transitions, accurate time-stamps and soundtrack are finished by a human editor; quick_cut selects generically, not from the exact event markers.

title: Soccer Highlight Reel Creation
desc:
I'm looking for a talented video editor to create a high school girl's soccer highlight reel. The footage is from game videos, and I want a mixed-style reel, combining action and skill showcase.

Key elements to highlight:
- Key passes
- Dribbles

Ideal skills and experience:
- Proficiency in video editing software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro)
- Experience with sports highlight reels
- Ability to create engaging, dynamic content
- Attention to detail in showcasing specific skills

Please provide samples of previous sports highlight work.
task_type: Soccer Highlight Reel — mixed action/skill cut from game footage
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (game footage) -> video_create_quick_cut (auto-cut a mixed-style highlight reel) -> video_resize (reframe for platform if needed) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: High-school girls' soccer game footage (raw clips)
Which moments to feature: key passes and dribbles (skill-showcase + action)
Desired length / target platform — confirm; brief doesn't state
Output: edited highlight reel
note: video_create_quick_cut produces the auto highlight cut, but isolating the specific skills (key passes, dribbles) and the 'mixed-style' pacing/styling is human-finished — quick_cut auto-selects highlights generically rather than by named skill.

title: High-Energy Club Recap Videos
desc:
I'm seeking a talented video editor to create high-energy, 30-second recaps of club nights. These videos will be used to promote future events and need to capture the vibrant atmosphere of our nights.

Requirements:
- 30-second videos
- Recaps of club nights
- Energetic and fast-paced vibe

Ideal Skills and Experience:
- Proficiency in video editing software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro)
- Experience with music and event highlight reels
- Ability to create engaging, dynamic content
- Strong portfolio showcasing past work

Please include relevant past work in your application.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (club-night footage) -> video_create_quick_cut (auto-cut an energetic 30s recap) -> video_resize (reframe to vertical/platform for promo) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Raw club-night footage (the night's clips)
Target length 30s; energetic/fast-paced vibe
Target platform/aspect for promotion (IG/TikTok vertical?) — confirm
Music/branding for the human finish step
Output: 30s recap video, web-ready
note: video_create_quick_cut auto-cuts the fast-paced highlight and video_resize reframes it, but the trending-music bed, text/branding overlays and final beat-matched pacing are finished by a human editor.

title: Spiritual Reels & Shorts Editing
desc:
I record 30–60-minute videos filled with spiritual reflections, practical life lessons, and bite-size information that my audience loves to revisit during the day. I now want to turn each long episode into a set of engaging 30- to 60-second reels/shorts suitable for YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and similar platforms.

Your task is to watch the full recording, pick out the most impactful moments, and edit them into polished vertical clips with captions, subtle branding, light background music (when it serves the message), and seamless transitions. Please retain the calm, uplifting tone of the original footage while keeping the final pace snappy enough for short-form viewers.

I am comfortable with whichever tool you prefer—Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, etc.—as long as the output is crisp, properly formatted (1080 × 1920), and instantly publishable.

Deliverables
• A batch of short-form clips (30–60 sec each) exported in MP4 and one editable project file.
• Simple SRT or burned-in subtitles synced accurately to speech.
• A quick preview sheet (timestamps + one-line context) so I know where each clip was pulled from.

Acceptance criteria
• All clips match the original audio levels and color tone; no abrupt cuts or dead air.
• Text overlays remain legible on mobile screens.
• No copyrighted audio or visuals are introduced.

Share a recent sample of similar spiritual or educational shorts you have produced, note your typical turnaround time per batch, and let me know any suggestions you have for maximizing engagement. I’m ready to start as soon as I find the right editor.
task_type: Long-form spiritual recording → batch of 30–60s vertical shorts + transcript/preview sheet
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (30–60 min recording) -> media_summarize (transcribe + identify the most impactful moments with timestamps -> feeds the preview sheet and the SRT base) -> video_create_quick_cut (auto-cut the selected highlight moments) -> video_resize (reframe each clip to 1080x1920) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The 30–60 minute source recording(s) of spiritual reflections / life lessons
Number of shorts wanted per episode + the calm/uplifting tone to preserve
Branding elements (subtle) + light background-music preference for the human step
Outputs: batch of 30–60s MP4 clips (1080x1920), SRT or burned-in subtitles, a preview sheet (timestamps + one-line context), one editable project file
Acceptance: clips match original audio levels/colour tone, captions legible on mobile, no copyrighted audio/visuals
note: media_summarize gives the transcript + impactful-moment timestamps (the preview sheet and SRT base), video_create_quick_cut auto-cuts and video_resize reframes to 9:16 — but the final clip selection for tone, subtle branding, music bed, burned-in captions and the editable project file are finished by a human editor.

title: Pot Painting Reel Editing
desc: I’ve just wrapped up my very first Pot Painting Workshop for my Instagram page, “The Fun Theory,” and recorded plenty of raw footage along the way. Now I need that material transformed into a single, high-quality reel that instantly grabs attention in the feed. The look I’m after is artistic and visually focused, so every brushstroke and splash of color should feel almost tactile on-screen. An upbeat, fun track will set the mood, and smooth, on-beat cuts will keep viewers watching to the end. Key moments to weave in: close-ups of intricate pot-painting details; genuine participant interactions; a quick sense of the overall workshop setup. Final deliverable: one polished 1080x1920 MP4 reel, under 60 seconds, color-graded, caption-ready, formatted for Instagram Reels.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (raw workshop footage) -> video_create_quick_cut (auto highlight cut of best brushstroke/reaction moments, target <60s) -> video_resize (reframe to 1080x1920 vertical) -> media_summarize (generate caption text for the post) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: All raw workshop clips (close-ups of pot-painting detail, participant reactions, room/setup shots) — client to upload
Target spec: single 1080x1920 MP4, under 60s, Instagram Reels
Upbeat music track choice — confirm; client wants an on-beat fun track but hasn't named one
Brand/handle: The Fun Theory (for end card / caption)
note: quick_cut auto-selects highlights and resize delivers the vertical export, but the on-beat music sync to the chosen track and the final artistic colour grade are done by a human in an NLE.

title: Monthly Basis Video Editing
desc: I have a steady stream of raw footage arriving every month and need a reliable editor who can turn it around consistently. Short- to mid-length pieces, some for social channels, others traditional. Each batch comes with briefs, reference links, branding assets. Typical pass: tight cuts that keep pacing engaging; basic colour and audio balancing so everything feels polished; simple titles, lower thirds, and captions when the brief calls for them; final exports in platform-ready resolutions (usually 1080p or square/vertical variants). Project files (Premiere Pro or Final Cut) supplied alongside renders.
task_type: Monthly Footage Cut-down + Multi-format Export (1080p / square / vertical)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (monthly raw footage batch) -> media_enhance_speech (audio balance / clean dialogue) -> video_create_quick_cut (tight pacing cut) -> video_resize (export 1080p + 1:1 + 9:16 variants per brief) -> media_summarize (caption/subtitle text) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Each month's raw footage batch + the per-batch brief and reference links
Branding assets (logo, colours) the client supplies for titles/lower thirds
Required output variants per piece — confirm which of 1080p / square (1:1) / vertical (9:16) each one needs
Premiere Pro or Final Cut project file requested alongside the master — connector exports flattened media only
note: Connector handles audio balancing, the trim, captions and the multi-ratio exports; a human adds the simple titles / lower-thirds graphics and provides the editable .prproj/.fcp project file the client wants.

title: Clip & Subtitle Family Guy Laughs
desc: I need the best physical-comedy, sarcastic, funny moments from every season of Family Guy pulled out, trimmed, and delivered as individual video clips with accurate English subtitles burned in. Isolate each bit as a self-contained clip (10-90s). Add clean, readable subtitles synced to dialogue and sound cues; spelling and timing spot-on. Export MP4/MOV 1080p, named season-episode-timestamp. Every clip should start/end smoothly, no dead air, subtitles matching audio without lag.
task_type: Clip Extraction + Burned-in English Subtitles (10-90s clips)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (episode files) -> media_summarize (transcribe dialogue to timed subtitle text) -> video_create_quick_cut (isolate/trim each gag into a 10-90s clip) -> video_resize (standardise 1080p export) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Source episode files for the seasons in scope — client to provide (note: third-party copyrighted content)
Clip length range 10-90s; output MP4/MOV 1080p
File-naming convention: season-episode-timestamp
Which moment categories count (slapstick falls, fights, visual gags) — the brief's curation criteria
note: media_summarize produces the synced subtitle transcription and the clips can be trimmed/exported, but a human curates WHICH comedic moments qualify across the seasons and does the final burn-in/sync QA; clip-boundary judgement is editorial.

title: Instagram Promo Image Creation
desc: I need a steady stream of professional, clean images for my Instagram feed, each focused on branding and promotions. Every post must feel cohesive with the colours, fonts, and voice already used while still standing out. I'll send campaign briefs, key messages, timing. You turn them into single-frame graphics. Deliverables: 1080x1080 (or 1080x1350) JPG/PNG; layered source file (PSD/AI); short alt-text/caption per image. Acceptance: precise brand colours and typography; clean uncluttered layout; under 2 MB.
mcp_workflow: search_design (find a clean promotional Instagram-post template, fillDescription = the campaign's key message) -> fill_text (drop in the offer/headline/CTA copy) -> asset_add_file (client logo/product photo) -> asset_preview_file -> media_summarize (draft alt-text/caption)
inputs: Per-campaign key message, headline, offer, CTA copy to typeset
Brand colours (hex) and brand fonts — client to supply exact values
Logo file and any product photo to place
Output: 1080x1080 or 1080x1350 JPG/PNG, under 2 MB
Also requested: editable layered PSD/AI source
note: fill_text changes the copy only; an Express template cannot guarantee the client's exact brand font/colour/typography or output the layered PSD/AI source they require — those need a human in Photoshop/Express.

title: Affiliate Product Review Video Editor

No captions, special effects, or graphics needed—just a tight, polished edit. Deliverables: edited MP4 (1080p or higher); one quick revision round.
task_type: Tighten-edit a recorded product review (<5 min, no FX)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (recorded review take) -> media_enhance_speech (clean voice audio) -> video_create_quick_cut (remove pauses/stumbles, tighten intro->CTA flow) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The single recorded review take (under 5 minutes) — client to upload
Output: MP4, 1080p or higher
Note: no captions, effects, or graphics wanted — cut/trim only
Intended start (intro) and CTA end points — confirm any must-keep sections
note: quick_cut auto-tightens the take and enhance_speech cleans the audio, but a human reviews the cut to confirm every stumble/off-topic moment is removed exactly where the client wants and that the intro->CTA flow lands.

title: Brochure/Flyer Insert for Premium/Luxury Supplement brand
desc: Summary We are building an ultra-premium luxury longevity and wellness supplement brand targeting a 35–65+ female demographic, positioned in the "clinical luxury" space. We are looking for an experienced print and marketing collateral designer to create a premium brochure/flyer insert that will be included within our product packaging. This insert will play an important role in the customer experience, introducing the brand, communicating product benefits, reinforcing our premium positioning, and creating a memorable unboxing experience consistent with a luxury supplement brand. The ideal designer will have experience creating high-end print collateral for premium supplement, wellness, skincare, beauty, or luxury consumer brands and understand how to balance education, brand storytelling, and sophisticated design. We have already developed a clear Brand DNA, visual identity, packaging direction, and product positioning. This project is not starting from scratch. We have established creative direction, messaging foundations, and visual references that will be provided. Your role will be to elevate these assets into a polished, premium insert that feels cohesive with the overall product experience and luxury brand positioning. We will provide: - Brand guidelines and visual references - Product information and key messaging - Creative direction and positioning Project Scope: Design a premium printed insert that will be included within our product packaging. Potential deliverables may include: - Welcome card - Product education brochure - Brand story insert - Product usage guide - Longevity philosophy or ritual guide - Fold-out brochure or multi-page insert (depending on final concept) The final format will be determined collaboratively during the design process. What We Need From You: - High-end brochure and print collateral design - Strong editorial layout and typography skills - Ability to organise educational content in a clean and engaging way - Strong understanding of luxury aesthetics and premium print design - Experience designing for premium wellness, supplement, beauty, skincare, or luxury consumer brands - Attention to spacing, hierarchy, readability, and customer experience - Ability to design for real-world print production Ability to deliver: - Print-ready artwork (AI, INDD, PDF) - Source files - Bleed and print specifications where required - Production-ready files suitable for commercial printing Ideal Experience: - Premium supplement brands - Luxury wellness brands - Longevity and health products - Skincare and cosmetic brands - Beauty and wellness packaging inserts - Editorial and brochure design - High-end direct-to-consumer brands Experience creating customer-facing collateral for premium or luxury brands is highly preferred. To Apply, Please Include: - Relevant brochure, editorial, or print design examples - Examples of premium supplement, wellness, beauty, skincare, or luxury brand collateral - Any product packaging inserts, welcome guides, or educational materials you've designed -A short note explaining your experience with luxury print design Screening Question: Please describe one premium or luxury brand project you've worked on that involved printed collateral, packaging inserts, brochures, or customer education materials. What design decisions helped create a high-end, premium customer experience? Please include relevant examples that align with the screening question. Applications without examples will not be considered. We are building a long-term premium supplement brand and are looking for a designer who understands luxury consumer experiences and can create collateral that feels refined, credible, educational, and worthy of a high-end longevity product. Budget is flexible for the right candidate with strong experience designing for premium supplement, wellness, skincare, beauty, or luxury consumer brands.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (brand guidelines, product photos, logo, fonts) -> search_design (Express premium brochure / welcome-card / multi-page insert template) -> fill_text (brand story, product benefits, usage/ritual guide copy) -> asset_add_file (product imagery) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Brand guidelines + visual references (provided)
Product info, key messaging, positioning copy (provided)
Logo, brand fonts, product photography
Final format TBD (welcome card / fold-out brochure / multi-page insert)
Print specs: bleed + CMYK, AI/INDD/PDF deliverables requested
note: Template + fill_text gets the editorial copy in; the bespoke luxury editorial layout, exact typographic hierarchy and print pre-press (bleed/CMYK source files) need a human finisher

title: PHOTOGRAPHY MASKING/BLENDING Realistic Movie Poster Recreation
desc: I have an AI-generated key art for a reality-show poster. Composition, typography, concept approved; it lacks PHOTOGRAPHIC believability. Rebuild from my reference, replacing every AI area with properly-masked hi-res photos of the real subject so it looks shot on set—masking, blending layers, integrating textures, believable lighting and shadows. I don't have the stock photos—find workable photos. Print 24×36", 300 dpi, CMYK + web version. Need back: layered PSD with masks, print PDF, web JPG.
mcp_workflow: asset_search + asset_license_and_download_stock (hi-res photos matching the AI subjects) -> image_remove_background / image_select_subject (cut out each sourced subject) -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_highlights + image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast (match lighting to reference mood) -> [HUMAN: mask/blend/relight composite in Photoshop] -> document_convert_pdf (print PDF) + asset_preview_file (web JPG)
inputs: Reference AI poster (JPG + layered PSD) — client provides
Print spec 24×36", 300dpi, CMYK + a web JPG size (confirm)
Subjects to source as real photos (the AI areas to replace) — list/describe
Final: layered PSD with non-destructive masks, print PDF, flattened web JPG
Lighting/mood reference from the approved key art
note: The connector does a real chunk — sourcing matching Adobe Stock photos, cutting out subjects, and colour/tone-matching them to the reference. But the core ask (seamless non-destructive masking, layer blending, integrating textures and believable shadows so it passes a 200% edge check) is hand Photoshop compositing a human must finish.

The finished mark must scale cleanly from favicons to storefront signage, hold up in full colour as well as single-colour applications, and read crisply on both digital and print materials.  

If you can guide me through font licensing and ensure the files remain fully editable for future tweaks, that will be appreciated.
mcp_workflow: font_recommend (modern/classic/minimalist directions) -> search_design (Express logo / wordmark template) -> fill_text (company name) -> asset_preview_file -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (SVG/EPS/PNG, full-colour + single-colour)
inputs: Company name + any wording (client undecided — collect)
Style direction: modern / classic / minimalist (client open)
Colour palette suggestions (client wants guidance)
Exports needed: scalable vector + favicon-to-signage sizes, full-colour + 1-colour
note: A simple wordmark from a template is fine, but fill_text changes text only; a truly bespoke, "distinctive" custom mark and editable master files need a human — template + vectorize approximates a typographic logo.

title: Inspirational Memoir & Banner Design
desc:
I want to turn a true slice of my life into an uplifting memoir that shows how I moved from struggle to success, and then pair that story with a striking promotional banner.

Story scope  
– Genre: memoir, written in an engaging first-person voice that feels inspirational rather than preachy.  
– Core theme: achieving success – the narrative should highlight key turning points, lessons learned, and tangible results so readers finish motivated to act on their own goals.  
– Length and structure can be discussed, but I expect a concise, well-paced piece that sits comfortably in the short-story/chapbook range and can later expand into a full book if things go well.  

Banner scope  
Once the memoir draft is approved, I’ll need a high-resolution banner to promote it across social media and on a landing page. It should capture the spirit of triumph—think bold, optimistic colours and typography. Final files: layered PSD or AI plus web-ready PNG/JPEG.

Deliverables  
• Edited Word or Google Doc containing the finished memoir  
• One hero banner (1920×1080 px preferred) with source file  

I’ll provide additional background notes, photos, and any brand colours I already use. Please bring strong storytelling skills, an ear for inspirational tone, and solid design chops so the visuals match the words.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express promotional banner template, bold optimistic palette) -> fill_text (book title + tagline) -> asset_add_file (author photo + supplied brand colours) -> image_crop_and_resize (1920x1080) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: One hero banner, 1920x1080 px, for social + landing page
Book/memoir title + a short triumphant tagline
Author background photo + any existing brand colours (client supplies)
Note: the memoir WRITING itself is copywriting, out of connector scope
note: fill_text changes copy only; the actual memoir-writing deliverable is out of scope, and exact bold/optimistic styling needs a human — connector delivers only the promo banner.

title: Minimalist Company-Name Logo
desc:
I need a clean, minimalist logo built around my company name only—no icons or taglines this time. The mark should rely on strong typography, clever negative space, and balanced proportions so it remains instantly legible at every size, from social-media avatars to large signage.

Please craft two or three concept options, refine the chosen direction through a couple of feedback rounds, and supply the final artwork in fully editable vector format (AI, EPS, PDF) along with web-ready PNG and SVG files. 

Adobe Illustrator or an equivalent vector tool is preferred so the files stay resolution-independent. Color mode should be CMYK with a paired RGB/HEX palette, and all fonts either outlined or accompanied by proper licensing information.

I’ll share the company name once we start—looking forward to seeing how you distill it into a timeless minimalist identity.
mcp_workflow: font_recommend (minimalist typefaces) -> search_design (Express minimalist wordmark template) -> fill_text (company name) -> asset_preview_file -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (AI/EPS/PDF/SVG/PNG, CMYK + RGB/HEX)
inputs: Company name (client supplies at start)
Style: clean minimalist type, negative space, balanced proportions (no icon, no tagline)
Exports: AI/EPS/PDF + PNG/SVG; CMYK + paired RGB/HEX palette
Must read at avatar size up to signage
note: A name-only wordmark suits a template, but fill_text changes text only — the clever negative-space treatment and true editable .AI vector source need a human; vectorize gives an approximate scalable file.

title: Modern Store Graphics Package
desc:
I’m refreshing my online shop and need a cohesive set of graphics that instantly tells shoppers they’re in a sleek, modern space. The scope is focused on three key areas:

• Store Banner – a hero image that sets the tone the moment visitors land in the store, sized for desktop and mobile.  
• Product Detail Page (PDP) assets – template-ready image frames, iconography, and typography treatments that make features, specs, and calls-to-action pop without clutter.  
• Store Decoration – background elements, section dividers, and accent graphics that tie everything together and keep the look consistent across every page.

Expect clean lines, bold but balanced color, and plenty of negative space—nothing fussy or ornamental. Source files (PSD or AI) plus optimized JPG/PNG exports for immediate upload are required. Deliver layered files, clearly labeled, so my team can tweak text or swap images later.

When submitting, show a quick style preview or link to a similar modern-focused project you’ve done. Once we lock the direction, I’ll supply brand colors, product shots, and copy, and we can agree on milestones for banner first, then PDP, then decoration assets.

Looking forward to seeing how you’ll turn a plain storefront into a polished, modern shopping experience.
task_type: Online Store Graphics Pack — banner + PDP + decoration
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (brand colours, product shots, copy) -> search_design (Express store banner template) -> fill_text (store headline/CTA) -> image_crop_and_resize (desktop + mobile banner sizes) -> asset_preview_file ; image_remove_background (product shots for PDP frames)
inputs: Hero store banner (desktop + mobile sizes — confirm exact px)
PDP image frames / icon + type treatments for features/specs/CTA
Section dividers + accent/background decoration graphics
Brand colours + product shots + copy (client supplies once direction is locked)
note: fill_text changes copy only; bespoke iconography and a custom cohesive store theme need a human — connector supplies banner templates, photo cutouts and resizes for the requested assets.

title: Fashion Brand Graphics/ Creative Assets
desc:
We're looking for a creative freelance graphic designer for our boho fashion brand. 

You'll be creating a range of assets across:

EDMs (Klaviyo)
Website banners and graphics
In-store signage 
Social media assets
Sale campaigns and collection launches
Packaging and other brand assets

We're looking for someone with:

Experience designing for fashion, lifestyle, or retail brands
Strong skills in Klaviyo, Photoshop, Canva, and Adobe Creative Suite
An eye for typography, layout, colour, and storytelling
The ability to work within an established brand while bringing fresh ideas and creative direction
Understanding of customer-focused design and conversion-driven marketing

Our aesthetic is feminine, bohemian, and vintage-inspired, so we'd love to see examples of work that aligns with a similar style.

This is a freelance role with ongoing work available for the right person.

To apply, please send:

A portfolio or examples of recent work
Your hourly or project rates
A brief introduction about yourself and your experience with fashion or retail brands

We'd love to hear from you
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (brand assets + product photos) -> search_design (Express template per asset: email header, web banner, social post, signage) -> fill_text (campaign copy) -> image_crop_and_resize (per-channel sizes) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Recurring assets: Klaviyo EDMs, website banners, in-store signage, social posts, sale/launch campaigns, packaging
Boho/feminine/vintage aesthetic + existing brand palette (client supplies)
Product photography + campaign copy per asset (collect)
Per-channel output sizes (confirm)
note: fill_text changes copy only; staying inside the established boho brand system (exact fonts/colours/illustration motifs) needs a human — connector fills templates and resizes per channel.

title: YouTube Channel Setup for Original Music
desc:
I need a professional to create , manage and design my YouTube page. The channel will feature original music videos.

Requirements:
- Design a visually appealing and user-friendly layout
- Create a captivating channel banner and logo
- Organize video playlists for easy navigation
- Write engaging channel description
- Optimize for SEO to increase visibility

Ideal skills and experience:
- Proven experience in creating YouTube channels
- Strong graphic design skills
- Knowledge of YouTube SEO and algorithms
- Familiarity with music-related content is a plus

Please provide samples of previous work.
task_type: YouTube Channel Banner + Logo — music channel
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express YouTube channel art template) -> fill_text (channel name) -> asset_add_file (artist photo/logo) -> image_crop_and_resize (2560x1440 banner + safe area; avatar) -> asset_preview_file

Channel name + any artist photo/imagery (client supplies)
Music/original-music vibe + colour direction (collect)
Note: playlist organisation, channel description copy and SEO are admin work, not design
note: fill_text changes copy only; the banner + logo come from a template, but the playlist setup, description writing and SEO the client also wants are non-design channel-management tasks.

title: Modern Album Logo & Cover Design
desc:
I’m getting ready to release a new project and need a striking album cover that also doubles as a recognisable logo for all my promo materials. Visually, I’m after a clean modern aesthetic—nothing vintage or overly ornate—powered by bright, bold colours that jump off the screen and still look sharp in print.

Here’s what I’m thinking:
• One cohesive artwork that can serve as the streaming-service cover, social banner crop, and profile thumbnail.  
• A logo or word-mark element extracted from the main design so I can use it on merch and posters.  
• Final files supplied in layered PSD/AI, plus ready-to-post PNG and JPEG versions in both 3000×3000 px (for Spotify/Apple) and scalable vector for print.

Acceptance criteria:
– Design clearly reads at both large and thumbnail sizes.  
– Colour palette stays vivid and consistent across RGB and CMYK exports.  
– All fonts converted to outlines or included.  

If you need any extra info—track list, mood board references, or brand guidelines—let me know and I’ll provide them straight away. I’m keen to see concepts quickly and am happy to give fast, detailed feedback so we can lock the final artwork in without delays.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express album-cover / music template, bold bright palette) -> fill_text (artist + project title) -> asset_add_file (any supplied imagery) -> image_crop_and_resize (3000x3000) -> image_vectorize (extract wordmark) -> document_render_vector (vector logo for merch/print)
inputs: Cover artwork doubling as a logo: streaming cover + social crop + thumbnail
Artist name + project title
Bright bold colour direction; must read at thumbnail and full size
Exports: 3000x3000 PNG/JPG (Spotify/Apple) + scalable vector wordmark
Track list / mood-board references (collect)
note: fill_text changes copy only; a strong typographic cover suits a template, but bespoke original cover artwork/illustration is not connector-doable — only client-supplied imagery + template + the extracted wordmark.

title: Graphic & Reels Promotion Design
desc:
I’m looking for strong support on the graphic design side of my brand, with a particular focus on creating eye-catching assets that can be repurposed across social channels. The top priority is polished, on-brand graphics—think product-focused visuals, web banners, ad creatives, and any other imagery that pushes brand consistency forward.

Alongside these static pieces, I also want to keep our Instagram presence lively. Short-form Reels (10–30 s) that highlight products in a dynamic, scroll-stopping way are part of the mix, so familiarity with CapCut or Adobe Premiere Pro is welcome. Each Reel should feel cohesive with the static graphics you create, ultimately driving product promotion rather than general awareness.

Key tools in my stack include Photoshop, Illustrator, and Premiere Pro, so please be comfortable jumping between them to keep color, typography, and overall style aligned.

Deliverables  
• 100 % original, layered graphic files (AI/PSD) ready for resizing or quick copy changes  
• 3–5 product-promotion Instagram Reels supplied as .mp4 plus project files  
• All final exports optimized for fast mobile load times and current Instagram specs

Acceptance criteria  
• Visual language matches existing brand palette and tone  
• Reels open with a clear product hook within the first 3 s  
• Final assets submitted within agreed timeline and require no more than one minor revision round

If this combination of graphic design excellence and Reels creativity sounds like a natural fit for you, let’s talk timing and handover details.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (brand assets + product shots) -> search_design (Express product/ad/web-banner template) -> fill_text (promo copy) -> image_crop_and_resize (Instagram + web sizes) -> asset_preview_file ; video_create_quick_cut (product Reel) -> video_resize (9:16 Instagram)
inputs: Static set: product visuals, web banners, ad creatives (on-brand)
3-5 product-promotion Instagram Reels (10-30 s, 9:16) with a hook in first 3 s
Brand palette + product photography (client supplies)
Source product footage for Reels (collect) — connector cuts/reframes, does not film
Output optimised for current Instagram specs
note: fill_text changes copy only; layered AI/PSD source files cannot be produced and Reels are auto-cut/reframed from supplied footage — connector fills templates, resizes, and assembles from client clips.

I'm seeking a designer to create a modern, minimalist sign board for my business. The design should utilize a neutral color palette.

Neutral colour palette; modern minimalist style

title: Vibrant YouTube Logo & Banner
desc:
My channel is ready for a visual refresh and I’m focusing first on the two branding pieces viewers see most: the profile logo and the banner. The vibe I’m after is bold, colorful and unmistakably upbeat—something that pops immediately in feeds yet still feels cohesive across devices.

Here’s where things currently stand:
• I have a rough concept and a loose palette, but the colors aren’t locked in. Guidance on refining and balancing those tones for screen-readability is part of the brief.
• No existing artwork needs to be reused, so you can start from a clean slate while staying within YouTube’s current size guidelines (2560 × 1440 banner with a 1235 × 338 safe area, plus an 800 × 800 avatar).

Deliverables
1. Final banner and logo exported as high-resolution PNGs ready to upload.
2. Layered source files (AI or PSD) so I can tweak copy or colors later.
3. A quick reference of the finalized color codes and fonts used.

Acceptance criteria: artwork renders crisply on desktop, mobile and TV previews; colors match the approved palette; text or visuals in the safe area remain fully legible.

If you’ve crafted energetic, eye-catching YouTube branding before, this should be a straightforward project—we can move quickly once the palette clicks.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express YouTube channel art template, bold colourful) -> fill_text (channel name) -> asset_add_file (any imagery) -> image_crop_and_resize (2560x1440 banner w/ 1235x338 safe area; 800x800 avatar) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Banner 2560x1440 (1235x338 safe area) + 800x800 avatar/logo
Channel name + bold, colourful, upbeat vibe
Loose palette to refine (client wants colour-balancing guidance)
Exports: high-res PNGs + colour-code/font reference
No existing artwork to reuse (clean slate)
note: fill_text changes copy only; layered AI/PSD source cannot be produced and exact palette/typography balancing needs a human — connector fills a template and exports correct sizes.

title: Colorful Storefront Window Graphics
desc:
I need a set of vibrant, eye-catching graphics that will turn my shopfront windows into a giant seasonal billboard. The goal is twofold: reinforce my brand identity (logo, colours, tagline) and highlight our current seasonal product line so passers-by instantly know what’s fresh inside.

Here’s what I’m after:  
• A cohesive design theme spanning three glass panels, each 2 m × 1 m.  
• Full-bleed, print-ready artwork supplied as layered AI or PDF files, 300 dpi minimum, with crop and bleed marks already set.  
• Colour palette that pops from the street yet stays true to our brand PMS codes.  
• Space reserved in the layout for a small QR code the printer will drop in later.

Acceptance criteria  
1. Final files open flawlessly in Adobe Illustrator 2023.  
2. Colours match our supplied brand guide when proofed in CMYK.  
3. Text and logos remain razor-sharp when scaled to full window size.  
4. A quick mock-up on a shopfront photo is included so I can visualise placement before sending to print.

If you’ve designed storefront wraps or large-format vinyl before, I’d love to see a couple of examples. Let’s make this window impossible to ignore!
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (brand guide colours + logo + product shots + copy) -> search_design (Express large-format window/banner template) -> fill_text (seasonal headline + tagline) -> image_crop_and_resize (3x panels 2m x 1m, full-bleed) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf
inputs: 3 glass panels, each 2 m x 1 m, cohesive theme
Full-bleed print-ready, 300 dpi min, crop + bleed marks; reserve space for printer's QR
Brand PMS colours + logo + tagline + seasonal product line (client supplies)
Seasonal product shots to feature
Mock-up on a shopfront photo for placement preview
note: A human finishes large-format prepress in Illustrator: exact PMS/CMYK proofing, 300 dpi bleed/crop marks and native .AI delivery — the connector lays out the template content and resizes the panels.

title: Tamil Service Promotion Banner Design
desc:
I need six banners ( design only), all fully in Tamil, to advertise a service in a clearly traditional style. The breakdown of sizes is:

• 10 × 10 ft – 3 pieces  
• 10 × 20 ft – 2 pieces  
• 10 × 6 ft  – 1 piece  

The message and copy will be supplied in Tamil; the design must respect local cultural aesthetics while remaining eye-catching from a distance. Please create clean, high-resolution artwork ready for large-format printing and include editable source files (AI or PSD) alongside final PDFs/JPEGs. Color mode should be CMYK, 150 dpi minimum at full scale, with 1 inch bleed all around.

Acceptance will be based on:
• Accurate Tamil typography with no spelling errors  
• Consistent traditional look across every size  
• Print-ready files that meet the stated specs  

Kindly allow for a round of minor revisions after I review the first draft.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (Tamil copy + logo/photos) -> search_design (Express traditional promotional banner template) -> fill_text (supplied Tamil copy) -> image_crop_and_resize (10x10 ft x3, 10x20 ft x2, 10x6 ft x1) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf
inputs: 6 banners: 10x10 ft (3), 10x20 ft (2), 10x6 ft (1) — design only
All copy supplied in Tamil; accurate Tamil typography, no spelling errors
Traditional cultural aesthetic, readable from a distance
CMYK, 150 dpi min at full scale, 1 inch bleed all around
Logo/photos to feature (collect)
note: A human finishes large-format prepress: verifying Tamil font rendering/no errors, CMYK proofing, 150 dpi at full scale + 1" bleed, and native .AI/PSD source — connector fills the template copy and resizes per format.

title: Combat Fitness Logo Suite
desc:
I’m building a strength-and-conditioning brand for combat athletes and I need a complete logo pack that feels bold and strong. My working name is “SMB S&C" and I’d like the tagline “The Fitter Fighter” above/below or around the main text.

Colour palette is set: orange, white and black. Most likely the design will be word-driven, but if you see a symbol that truly fits the fight-sport vibe I’m open to exploring it.  

Deliverables I’ll need from you  
• Primary logo with integrated tagline in vector format (AI, SVG, EPS)  
• Profile-picture, banner and thumbnail versions for TikTok, Instagram, Facebook and YouTube, all optimised to platform specs (These need not be different to the primary logo just fitting to the places they're being applied to)
• Print-ready artwork for orange and black T-shirts, jumpers and hoodies (screen-print friendly)  
• Simple motion intro/outro stingers for video content (3-5 sec, MP4 + transparent MOV)  
• A mini style guide covering colour values, fonts and safe-margin rules .

I’m also looking for your professional advice: if there’s a smarter way to use the palette, an alternate slogan placement, or an additional asset I’ve overlooked, let’s discuss it before you start the design work.  

Files should be neatly organised and named so I can hand them to printers and video editors without extra tweaks. If this sounds straightforward, drop me a note with any questions and a quick outline of your approach.
task_type: Combat Fitness Logo Suite — wordmark "SMB S&C"
mcp_workflow: font_recommend (bold strong typefaces) -> search_design (Express bold wordmark/logo template, orange/white/black) -> fill_text ("SMB S&C" + tagline "The Fitter Fighter") -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (AI/SVG/EPS) -> image_crop_and_resize (social profile/banner/thumbnail variants for TikTok/IG/FB/YouTube)
inputs: Wordmark "SMB S&C" + tagline "The Fitter Fighter"; palette orange/white/black
Social variants sized per platform (TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube)
Apparel print versions for orange + black tees/hoodies (screen-print friendly)
Mini style guide (colour values, fonts, safe margins)
Note: 3-5 s motion intro/outro stingers (MP4 + transparent MOV) are out of connector scope
note: A wordmark suits a template (text-driven), but fill_text changes text only; an optional bespoke fight-sport symbol and the motion-graphic stingers need a human/After-Effects — connector does the wordmark, vector export and social resizes.

title: Coaching Institute Banner Design
desc:
I need a clean, premium-looking roadside banner (10 ft × 5 ft) for my coaching institute, DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES. The design must feel professional and serious, stay readable from a distance, and present information without clutter.

Core copy to feature – exactly as written:  
• Institute name: DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES  
• Tagline: Where Learning Meets Success  
• Courses Offered:  
 Pre-Foundation: 5th to 8th (All Subjects)  
 9th & 10th: Maths, Science, Social Science, English  
 11th & 12th: PCMB & Commerce  
• Highlight: ADMISSIONS OPEN  
• Contact (make 8103332901 the most prominent, then 8989871223 and 9589110600)  
• Location: Railway Station Road, Multai  
• Features: Small Batch Size · Personal Attention · Regular Tests · Doubt Sessions

Visual direction  
• Primary colour #046CB8, secondary #02ADEF, CMYK-ready.  
• Big, bold Inter font across headings and key numbers.  
• Icons that hint at subjects, a confident but minimal photo of students, and understated geometric elements to maintain a modern, minimalistic feel.  
• Balanced spacing and alignment so every line is instantly legible from the road.

Deliverables (within 24 hours)  
1. Editable layered PSD  
2. Print-ready PDF in CMYK, high resolution suitable for large-format flex printing (no pixelation)

I’ll sign off once the file opens cleanly, colours match the specs, and text hierarchy is crystal clear at full size.
task_type: Coaching Institute Roadside Banner — 10 ft x 5 ft
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (institute logo + student photo) -> search_design (Express professional banner template) -> fill_text (exact supplied copy) -> image_crop_and_resize (10 ft x 5 ft, large-format) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (CMYK print-ready)
inputs: Banner 10 ft x 5 ft, premium/professional, readable from the road

Exact copy: name "DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES"; tagline "Where Learning Meets Success"; course list (Pre-Foundation 5th-8th; 9th-10th; 11th-12th PCMB & Commerce); "ADMISSIONS OPEN"; contacts (8103332901 most prominent, then 8989871223, 9589110600); Location: Railway Station Road, Multai; features (Small Batch Size, Personal Attention, Regular Tests, Doubt Sessions)
Colours: primary #046CB8, secondary #02ADEF; Inter font for headings/numbers
Subject icons + a confident student photo (collect)
Deliverables: layered PSD + CMYK print-ready PDF (large-format flex)
note: A human finishes prepress: exact Inter font + #046CB8/#02ADEF in CMYK, large-format flex resolution and the requested layered PSD source — fill_text cannot set the precise font/colour/hierarchy; connector fills copy, places photo and resizes.

title: Logo Minimalis Warna Cerah
desc:
Saya mencari ide logo bergaya minimalis dengan palet warna cerah—merah, biru, dan hijau. Nama dan detail merek akan saya berikan segera setelah kontes dimulai; untuk saat ini silakan gunakan placeholder singkat agar fokus pada bentuk dan komposisi.

Apa yang saya butuhkan dari setiap entri:
• Satu versi utama beresolusi tinggi dalam format vektor (AI atau SVG).  
• Pratinjau siap-pakai dalam PNG/JPEG di latar belakang transparan dan putih.  
• Variasi hitam-putih opsional untuk memastikan fleksibilitas cetak.

Kriteria penilaian:
• Kesederhanaan: desain bersih, mudah dikenali.  
• Keseimbangan warna: merah, biru, dan hijau harus tampak harmonis tanpa kehilangan kesan minimalis.  
• Skalabilitas: tetap tajam baik di ukuran favicon maupun billboard.

Pemenang akan diminta menyerahkan file sumber lengkap beserta lisensi penuh hak pakai komersial. Saya menantikan konsep kreatif Anda—selamat berkompetisi!
task_type: Minimalist Wordmark Logo — bright red/blue/green palette, vector
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express minimalist wordmark logo template) -> fill_text (brand name placeholder) -> change_background_color (transparent + white versions) -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (AI/SVG + PNG/JPG)
inputs: Brand name (client says use a short placeholder until name confirmed) — confirm final name
Required palette: bright red, blue, green (harmonious, minimalist) — exact hex to confirm
Deliverables: vector AI/SVG primary + PNG/JPG previews on transparent and white backgrounds, plus optional black-and-white variant
Must scale from favicon to billboard
note: search_design + fill_text place the wordmark name only; exact font choice, the 3-colour balance and a true editable AI master need a human in Express/Illustrator. No custom icon requested, so a wordmark template fits.

title: Wedding Signage for Print (high res, not AI generated)
desc: Applicants, please share examples of similar work in your proposal. Hello! I love the attached signature drinks image I created and would like to keep that sign as is. However, the other signs do not coordinate well with it, and they are not high enough resolution for professional print quality. The current backgrounds were AI-generated, which limits the DPI and makes them risky for large-format printing. I’m looking to create a cohesive new family of signs in print-ready resolution. The attached seating chart and welcome sign drafts show the general sign types that need to be redesigned. Creative direction: Option 1: Continue with the soft floral theme. The flowers should be inspired by this arrangement: https://bifloralsilkflowers.com/producto/emma/arch-arrangement-6/ Option 2: Use a more elevated black script direction, similar to the attached example.jpg. Either way, I’d like the signs to feel like a coordinated family, while allowing the signature drinks sign to remain the more playful, personal piece. Files needed: 1. Welcome Gathering Sign Size: 20 in x 30 in Minimum resolution at 300 DPI: 6000 x 9000 pixels 2. Welcome Wedding Ceremony & Reception Sign Size: 35.4 in x 23.6 in Minimum resolution at 300 DPI: 10,620 x 7,080 pixels 3. Menu / Thank You Long Card Please confirm the final print size before designing. Once confirmed, please prepare at 300 DPI. 4. Seating Chart Size: 47.2 in x 35.4 in Minimum resolution at 300 DPI: 14,160 x 10,620 pixels (I'll provide the name lists soon) 5. Wedding Program Size: 5 x 7 folded, 10 x 7 flat Bleed: 0.125 in on all sides Safe margin: 0.25 in Resolution: 300 DPI minimum 6. Table signs for tables 1-6 5 x 7 inches Resolution: 300 DPI minimum Please provide the final files as print-ready PDFs, preferably with vector text and artwork where possible, plus editable source files. Please also include bleed if required by the printer, ideally 0.125 in to 0.25 in, and keep all important text safely inside the trim area. Thank you!
task_type: Wedding Signage Family — welcome, seating chart, menu, program, table signs (print-ready)
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express wedding signage templates: welcome / seating chart / menu / program / table number) -> fill_text (couple names, event copy, menu, table assignments) -> asset_add_file (client floral reference / script style ref) -> image_crop_and_resize (each exact trim size) -> document_convert_pdf (print-ready PDF w/ bleed)
inputs: Welcome Gathering Sign 20x30in (6000x9000px @300dpi)
Welcome Ceremony & Reception Sign 35.4x23.6in (10620x7080px)
Menu / Thank You long card — confirm final trim size; 300dpi
Seating Chart 47.2x35.4in (14160x10620px) — name list provided later
Wedding Program 5x7 folded / 10x7 flat, 0.125in bleed, 0.25in safe
Table signs tables 1-6, 5x7in, 300dpi
Creative direction: soft floral theme (ref arrangement URL) OR elevated black-script; keep existing signature-drinks sign untouched
Print-ready PDFs with vector text where possible + editable source files
note: Express templates cover these sign types and fill_text sets all copy/names, but matching the exact floral motif and black-script look, hitting the very large 300dpi pixel targets, and true vector-text PDFs at trim size with bleed need a human finish in Express/InDesign.

title: Banner images for industry category pages (royalty-free, consistent format)
desc: I need a set of 19 banner/hero images created for the industry category pages on my website. I have one existing banner that sets the format. Every new image needs to match it exactly so they drop into the same page layout. Format requirements (match the reference image I'll provide): - Wide banner layout, same dimensions and aspect ratio as my reference (See attached) - Also attached you will see how image is used on the website. - Left side darker/faded so overlay text stays readable - Main subject on the right side - Subject can be a person, a machine, equipment, or a relevant scene, as long as it fits the industry and matches the visual style - Each image should feel like part of the same set (consistent tone, lighting, color treatment) What I need an image for (19 total): Construction / Heavy Equipment Material Handling Manufacturing & Robotics Power Generation Equipment Leasing LED Lighting & Installation Medical & Dental Equipment Metal Fabrication Equipment Loans Retail Equipment Leasing Food Processing Mining & Fracking Plastic Manufacturing Renewable Energy Equipment Leasing Food Packaging Office & Tenant Improvement Semiconductor Chip Manufacturing Transportation Equipment Leasing Independent Power Plants Oil & Gas Equipment Leasing IT Equipment Leasing Requirements: All images must be royalty-free and cleared for commercial use. I'll need confirmation of the source and license for each one. Your quote needs to include revisions. We'll work through each image together so I can approve the final fit. Tell me how many revision rounds are included. Timeline matters. I need these done as soon as possible, so include your earliest realistic delivery date. To submit: Send a quote (flat rate or per-image, your call, but be clear) Include your timeline If you've done similar banner/hero work, send a couple of examples
task_type: Industry Category Hero Banners x19 — stock-sourced, format-matched to reference
mcp_workflow: asset_search (Adobe Stock per industry: construction, manufacturing/robotics, medical, mining, semiconductor, oil & gas, etc.) -> asset_license_and_download_stock -> image_crop_and_resize (match reference aspect/dimensions) -> image_apply_color_overlay (left-side darken for text overlay) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_apply_monochromatic_tint (consistent tone across set) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Reference banner image (exact dimensions/aspect to match) + example of in-page usage
Layout rule: left side darker/faded for overlay text, main subject on the right

19 named industries (Construction/Heavy Equip, Material Handling, Manufacturing & Robotics, Power Gen, Equipment Leasing, LED Lighting, Medical & Dental, Metal Fabrication, Equipment Loans, Retail Equip Leasing, Food Processing, Mining & Fracking, Plastic Mfg, Renewable Energy, Food Packaging, Office/Tenant Improvement, Semiconductor, Transportation Leasing, Independent Power Plants, Oil & Gas, IT Equip Leasing)
All must be royalty-free / commercial-cleared with source + license recorded
