title: Logo designer
desc: Who We Are Modern Perfumery is a Scandinavian-based fragrance brand built for people who take scent seriously. We are the go-to destination for premium and niche perfumes in the Nordic market — curating the best fragrances in the world and making them accessible to a new generation of perfume lovers in Denmark and across Scandinavia. We are not a department store. We are not fast fashion for fragrance. We are a world built entirely around the art, knowledge, and culture of perfume. What We Do We curate, sell, and educate. Our platform — built primarily through Instagram and online retail — is a universe dedicated to perfume. We share the stories behind fragrances, the craft behind the ingredients, the culture behind the houses. And we sell the pieces worth owning. Think of us as the place where someone goes when they want to understand perfume — not just wear it. Our Market We operate online, serving customers across Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Scandinavia is experiencing a growing wave of interest in niche and premium fragrances — and there is a significant gap in the market for a brand that speaks to this audience with authority, taste, and warmth. We are here to fill that gap. Brand Personality Warm Not cold luxury. Not intimidating. Approachable but elevated. We want people to feel welcomed into this world, not excluded from it. Knowledgeable We know this world deeply. Our audience trusts us because we speak with genuine authority — not because we shout the loudest. Curated We do not sell everything. We sell the right things. Every fragrance we feature has earned its place. Modern We bring the world of niche perfumery into a contemporary, digital-first space. Clean, considered, and current — never stuffy or old-fashioned. Grounded There is an earthiness to what we do — amber, oud, resin, wood. Our aesthetic is rooted in nature, warmth, and depth. Nothing artificial. Nothing cold. Visual Direction The aesthetic reference is warm afternoon desert light — think amber glass, cream linen, warm sand, and soft shadows. Think quiet luxury. The kind that does not need to announce itself. The mood is: a beautifully lit room that smells extraordinary. Unhurried. Rich. Considered. References in this direction: high-end fragrance houses with a warm, editorial aesthetic. Nothing clinical, nothing cold, nothing that belongs in a pharmacy. Color Palette We want warm, natural, and earthy tones — think cognac amber, warm sand, soft cream, and warm copper. The palette should feel like afternoon light through amber glass. We do not want anything with gold, black, or hard coloring. These feel too generic, too flashy, or too heavy for what we are building. The brand should feel warm and breathable — never cold, never loud. Logo Direction The name is Modern Perfumery. We want the logo to feel timeless, not trendy. It must work across all contexts: on a cream background, on a dark background, on packaging, on a website header, on a phone screen, and potentially on a physical label. What We Do Not Want — Anything that looks generic or mass-market — Decorative flourishes that feel dated or feminine in a clichéd way — Anything that could belong to a drugstore, a spa brand, or a candle company — Overly complicated symbols or icons — Anything shiny, metallic, or flashy — Stark black or bright white — always warm tones Typography Feeling The typography should feel refined but breathable. Not heavy. Not condensed. Imagine a high-end fragrance house meets a Scandinavian editorial brand. Clean lines, warm tone, quiet confidence. How the Logo Should Work — On cream or sand backgrounds — primary use — Reversed out on deep amber or dark cognac — for packaging — In full color and in a single warm tone (monochrome-ready) — At small sizes on a phone screen and at large sizes on a website banner In One Sentence Modern Perfumery is where serious fragrance culture meets the modern Scandinavian consumer — warm, knowledgeable, curated, and built to become the defining perfume brand in the North.
task_type: Wordmark Logo — "Modern Perfumery" (warm earthy palette, monochrome-ready)
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express refined wordmark/serif logo template) -> fill_text ("Modern Perfumery") -> image_apply_color_overlay (warm cognac/amber tone) -> change_background_color (cream + dark-cognac reversed versions) -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (AI/SVG + PNG)
inputs: Brand name: "Modern Perfumery"
Palette: cognac amber, warm sand, soft cream, warm copper (no gold/black/stark white) — exact hex to confirm
Must work on cream/sand (primary), reversed on deep amber/cognac, full-colour and single warm tone (monochrome-ready), favicon to banner scale
Refined, breathable serif/editorial feel — confirm exact font
note: Brief explicitly wants a clean wordmark and to AVOID complicated icons, so a wordmark template fits; but fill_text changes copy only — the exact refined typeface and warm-tone tuning need a human in Express.

title: URGENT: Reformat Retractable Banner Design for High-Resolution FedEx Print
desc: I need a graphic designer who can quickly clean up and reformat an existing retractable banner design so it is high-resolution, print-ready, and accepted by FedEx Office. I need someone to: Recreate or upgrade the design in high resolution Make sure the final file is print-ready for a retractable banner Ensure the QR code scans clearly before final delivery Preserve the overall look, colors, branding, and layout Improve sharpness, spacing, and overall polish if needed Deliver final files in the correct format for FedEx printing Final Deliverables Needed Please provide: Print-ready PDF Editable source file: AI, PSD, Canva, or InDesign High-resolution PNG/JPG preview QR code tested and confirmed scannable File formatted at actual banner size with bleed/safe margins Banner Info This is for a dental office called Premier Smiles of Charlotte. title: Sleek Digital Menu Design
desc:
I need a professionally designed menu that will live entirely online, so everything you create must be optimised for digital use—think sharp on retina displays, quick to load, and easy to read on phones, tablets, and desktop screens alike. I will supply the dish names, descriptions, prices, and any brand assets (logo, colour palette, preferred fonts). What I want from you is a cohesive layout that makes the food shine, guides the reader’s eye naturally, and fits neatly within the overall brand look.

Because the project is digital-only, please keep print-specific considerations out unless you feel a dual-purpose file would add value; if you can include that as a bonus, let me know. Your design tools are up to you—Adobe InDesign, Illustrator, Figma, Canva Pro, or any other professional platform you prefer—so long as the final files meet the requirements below.

Deliverables:
• Editable source file (AI, PSD, INDD, or Figma).  
• Ready-to-publish PDF plus high-resolution PNG/JPEG slices for web and social media.  
• Web-friendly typography or embedded fonts to avoid display issues.  

I’d like two rounds of tweaks after the first draft so we can nail every detail, then a quick hand-off with clear instructions for any future updates. If this sounds like your next design win, tell me how you’ll approach the layout and any previous digital menus you’ve crafted.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express menu template, digital-optimised) -> fill_text (dish names, descriptions, prices) -> asset_add_file (logo + brand assets) -> image_crop_and_resize (retina web + 1080x1080 social slices) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (publish-ready PDF) ; also export high-res PNG/JPEG
inputs: Dish names, descriptions and prices (client supplies all copy)
Brand assets: logo, colour palette, preferred fonts (client supplies)
Outputs: publish-ready PDF + high-res PNG/JPEG slices for web & social; web-friendly typography
Two rounds of tweaks expected

PROJECT: THE GEORGE INN OUNDLE – MENU DESIGN

I need a professional tri-fold (3-panel foldable) menu design created in Canva for The George Inn, Oundle.

The design should be fully editable in Canva so that we can easily update prices, add or remove products, and print new copies in the future without requiring further design work.

DELIVERABLES

1. Main Drinks Menu (Tri-Fold)
Draught Beer & Cider
Bottled Beer & Cider
Gin
Vodka
Rum

Brandy & Cognac

Premium Wines
Sparkling Wines
Prosecco
Port
Wine by the Glass & Bottle Pricing

3. Cocktail Menu (Tri-Fold)

Signature Cocktails
Classic Cocktails
Spritz Selection
Mocktails
Premium Serves
Seasonal Specials

DESIGN REQUIREMENTS
 Use The George Inn Oundle logo provided.
Traditional premium British pub/gastro pub style.
Elegant and timeless design.
Suitable for table presentation and printing.
Consistent branding across all three menus.
Professional typography with clear pricing.
High readability.
Print-ready layout.
Include page margins and bleed considerations.
Canva editable source files must be supplied.
All fonts and elements used must be available within Canva.
Design should be easy to update in future.

COLOUR PALETTE
Traditional pub styling.
Cream/parchment background.
Dark green accents.
Black text.
Optional subtle gold highlights.

FILES REQUIRED
Canva editable link/file.
Print-ready PDF versions.
High-resolution PNG exports.

The final design should feel similar to a premium countryside pub, gastro pub, or boutique inn rather than a modern nightclub or chain restaurant.

title: Convert Image to Vector Graphic
desc: I need someone to convert an image file into a vector graphic and make some modifications to part of the image. A .png file will be provided for reference. The ideal candidate should have experience in vector graphics and image editing.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the reference .png) -> image_vectorize (convert raster to vector paths) -> document_render_vector (export as SVG/AI/EPS/PDF) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The reference .png file the client will provide
Description of which part of the image needs modification (the 'modifications to part of the image')
Target vector output format(s) — confirm with client (brief doesn't state)
note: Auto-vectorization is full; the 'modifications to part of the image' may require manual vector editing the connector can't do beyond tracing — scope the edit with the client.

title: Ecommerce Product Photo Editor
desc: I’m looking for a highly skilled product photo editor/retoucher for a Shopify product website. The product is a pregnancy pillow, so accuracy is extremely important. I need someone who is excellent with color correction and color matching, especially fabric colors. The editor must be able to keep the product’s exact shape, size, seams, logo tag placement, and texture intact while improving the photo quality. Tasks may include smoothing wrinkles, correcting fabric color, cleaning backgrounds, making images look premium, and creating polished Shopify-ready product images. Please only apply if you have strong experience with e-commerce product retouching, fabric color matching, and Shopify/Amazon-style product images. Please include before-and-after examples.
task_type: Product Photo Retouching & Fabric Color Correction — Shopify Pillow
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the raw pregnancy-pillow JPEGs) -> image_apply_auto_tone (baseline) -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_hsl + image_adjust_single_color_saturation (match the exact fabric colour, the client's #1 priority) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_highlights (premium pop) -> image_select_subject -> image_invert_selection -> change_background_color or image_fill_area (clean the background without touching the product) -> image_crop_and_resize (Shopify-square) -> asset_preview_file (before/after check)
inputs: The raw pregnancy-pillow product JPEGs (count not stated — confirm how many SKUs/angles)
The exact target fabric colour as hex/Pantone or a physical/master reference swatch — color-matching fabric is the client's stated #1 priority
Required Shopify output dimensions & background (white vs lifestyle) — confirm; brief says 'Shopify-ready' but no px given
Confirmation that shape/seams/logo-tag placement must stay pixel-accurate (no warping)
note: Connector handles fabric color-matching, background cleanup, crop and overall premium grade; smoothing fabric wrinkles/creases needs a human clone/heal pass (no generative heal in the connector).

title: Expert Video and Photo Editor Required
desc: I am looking for a talented and creative video editor to help me with my video projects. I will provide the raw footage, and I need someone who can cut it smoothly, add good transitions, color grade, and enhance the overall quality. High-quality output and timely delivery are highly preferred. Please share your portfolio or past work samples with your proposal.I am looking for a professional photo editor to enhance and retouch my images. The work involves color correction, sharpening facial features, cinematic lighting adjustments, and background clean-up to deliver high-resolution, professional-looking images. Photoshop expertise is required. Please share your previous work samples.I need an experienced video editor to create engaging Instagram Reels and Shorts. The job includes cutting raw clips, adding trending music, syncing audio, and adding text captions/subtitles to make the content highly engaging. If you have experience in creating viral short-form content, please apply with your best samples.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the raw photos) -> image_apply_auto_tone (baseline correction) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_hsl (cinematic color grade) -> image_select_subject + image_adjust_light_portions/image_adjust_highlights (cinematic lighting on the subject) -> image_remove_background or change_background_color (background clean-up) -> asset_preview_file. For the footage: asset_add_file (video) -> video_create_quick_cut (smooth highlight cut) -> video_resize (reframe to 9:16 for Reels/Shorts) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The raw image set to enhance (count not stated in brief — confirm how many stills, and the target output resolution they call 'high-resolution')
Per-image direction on the 'cinematic lighting' look (warm/cool, contrast level) — confirm reference look
The raw video footage files for the highlight cut
Target platform/aspect for the cut: Instagram Reels/Shorts implies 9:16 (1080x1920) — confirm
Trending music track + voice-over for sync (the connector cannot add music or sync audio — client must accept that part is out of scope)
note: Photo color/lighting/background-cleanup and the auto highlight video cut + 9:16 reframe are doable; adding trending music, audio sync, and burned-in captions/subtitles are not — needs a human/NLE for the audio+caption layer.

title: Graphic Designer for Product Images
desc: We are currently seeking a highly skilled graphic designer to enhance the visual presentation of product images on our website. The responsibilities of this role include refining design elements, adjusting image dimensions, and incorporating new designs to elevate the aesthetic appeal of product pages. The ideal candidate will possess substantial experience in image editing and design.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload product images) -> image_remove_background (clean product cutouts for web) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast / image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> image_crop_and_resize (standardize web dimensions) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The product images for the website (count unspecified — confirm)
Target web display dimensions to resize to (confirm with client)
Whether a white/transparent background is wanted
Any brand style reference for the product pages
note: Tone/background/resize are connector-doable, but 'incorporating new designs' (adding original graphic elements) is generation/layout work the connector cannot do.

title: High-End Image Editor
desc: Looking for a high-end photo editor/retoucher who can seamlessly match my editing style: https://shefaliparekh.com/ I specialize in newborn, maternity, and family portraits, so I’m looking for someone with a refined, natural editing approach - skin tones, color consistency, and softness are extremely important. Images will be delivered via Lightroom catalog or RAW files. Each session typically consists of 400–900 images, which will be culled down to approximately 75–100 final selects. The focus is always quality over quantity. You will receive between 3-4 catalogs per week to edit. A base preset will be provided, but I’m looking for someone who can elevate the images beyond that with a strong eye for detail and consistency. High-end retouching requires careful color correction, skin refinement, and overall polish while maintaining a cinematic look . Requirements: - Several years of professional photo editing/retouching experience (portrait work preferred) - Strong proficiency in Lightroom + Photoshop - Ability to match an existing style with precision and consistency - Attention to detail, especially with skin tones and natural retouching - Quick turnaround times and willingness to collaborate/learn I’d love to start with a test edit before moving forward with a long-term working relationship.
task_type: Batch Preset Grade & Color Consistency — Newborn/Family Selects
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the ~75–100 final selects per session) -> image_apply_preset (apply the client's provided base preset) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_hsl (refined, natural skin tones; cinematic consistency) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The provided base Lightroom preset + RAW/Lightroom-catalog selects (~75–100 finals per session, 3–4 catalogs/week)
Reference for the shefaliparekh.com style (soft, natural, cinematic newborn/maternity/family look) to match and elevate beyond the preset
Confirmation that skin tones/softness/color consistency are the top priority
Output/export spec and per-catalog turnaround
note: Connector applies the provided preset and drives color/tone consistency across the cull; high-end skin refinement/retouching and the human eye to 'elevate beyond the preset' are a human's.

title: LinkedIn Photo Replacement
desc:
I need an image edit for my LinkedIn profile. 

Requirements:
- Replace an existing picture with my provided picture.
- The existing picture is a portrait.

Ideal Skills:
- Proficiency in photo editing software (e.g., Photoshop)
- Experience with portrait image manipulation
- Attention to detail for a seamless replacement
task_type: Portrait Prep / Background Swap — LinkedIn Profile
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (ingest the client's provided portrait) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_color_temperature (clean, professional headshot look) -> image_remove_background (isolate subject) -> image_fill_area with a clean solid backdrop colour (or change_background_color) -> image_crop_and_resize to a square LinkedIn profile size (e.g. 400x400 / 800x800) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The client's new provided portrait photo (the replacement image)
Confirm the actual intent: is this just preparing the provided portrait (crop/tone/background) for upload, or compositing the client's face into an existing portrait? — brief is ambiguous
Desired background colour/style for the headshot (confirm hex or 'keep original')
Output: square LinkedIn profile dimensions (confirm px)
note: Doable as portrait cleanup/background-swap/resize of the supplied photo; if the real ask is a face-swap or seamless composite into the old photo, that is generative manipulation the connector cannot do — confirm intent.

title: Luxury Product Photo Editor Needed – Consistent Premium Shopify Hamper Images (13 Photos)
desc: I’m launching a premium English-made gifting brand and need an experienced photo editor/retoucher to professionally edit 13 product/lifestyle hamper images for Shopify and web use. The goal is a clean, high-end, consistent luxury look across the full image set. Tasks include: * Consistent exposure, white balance, and colour grading * Match wicker tones and overall image warmth between photos * Improve lighting balance and contrast * Clean and refine backgrounds where needed * Remove distractions/equipment where possible * Subtle premium retouching while keeping images natural * Consistent shadows and depth * Prepare web-ready exports for Shopify * Maintain a quiet luxury / editorial aesthetic (Fortnum & Mason / Daylesford style) * Enhance visibility of the embossed leather badge/logo on the front centre of the hamper while keeping it natural and premium-looking * Remove the fabric/material beneath the hamper in one of the images so the hamper can later be cut out and placed onto a light grey background Important: * These images will ultimately be cut out and placed onto a light grey website background, so clean edges and realistic shadows are important. * I am NOT looking for overprocessed, fake-looking, or heavily AI-styled edits. * Images should remain realistic, calm, premium, and consistent. * Consistency across the full image set is extremely important. Please include: * Examples of luxury product, food/drink, or ecommerce photo editing work * Your rate for editing 13 images * Estimated turnaround time * Whether you work in Lightroom only or Photoshop as well Deliverables: * Final high-resolution edited JPGs * Consistent editing style/settings across the full set There is strong potential for ongoing work as the website grows and additional products and hampers are added.
task_type: Consistent Luxury Color Grade + Cutout Prep — 13 Hamper Images
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the 13 hamper product/lifestyle images) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_apply_preset (lock one quiet-luxury look across all 13) -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_hsl (match wicker tones & warmth between photos) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_highlights + image_adjust_dark_portions (lighting balance, consistent shadows/depth) -> image_remove_background on the flagged image (so the hamper can later be placed on a light-grey bg) -> image_crop_and_resize (Shopify web-ready) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The 13 hamper product/lifestyle images
Target aesthetic reference: quiet-luxury / Fortnum & Mason / Daylesford editorial look
The one image where the fabric beneath the hamper must be removed for a later cutout onto light-grey (#? — confirm grey value)
Shopify web export spec (dimensions/format) — confirm
note: Connector delivers the consistent exposure/WB/color grade across all 13, wicker-tone matching and the background cutout; removing distracting equipment/the under-fabric (clone/generative removal) and subtly enhancing the embossed leather badge need a human.

title: Motorcycle Fairing Photoshop
desc:
I need a skilled Photoshop artist to add a motorcycle fairing to an image of my motorcycle. 

Requirements:
- Use the image I provide of my motorcycle.
- Accurately apply the specific fairing design I will supply.
- No additional modifications needed, just the fairing.

Ideal Skills & Experience:
- Proficiency in Adobe Photoshop
- Experience with vehicle image manipulation
- Attention to detail and design accuracy

Please provide a portfolio of similar work.
task_type: Photo Composite — Add Fairing to Motorcycle (cutout prep)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the supplied fairing design image) -> image_remove_background (cut the fairing out to a clean transparent PNG) -> asset_add_file (upload the motorcycle photo) -> image_crop_and_resize (scale the fairing cutout to match the bike) -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast (match the fairing's light/colour to the bike scene) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The client's motorcycle photo (the base image)
The specific fairing design image to apply (client will supply)
Constraint: only add the fairing, no other modifications
Confirm desired output format/resolution — brief doesn't state
note: Connector can cut out the fairing, scale it, and colour/light-match it, but it cannot precisely warp/place it onto the bike's geometry or blend edges convincingly (no layer compositing/transform); a human must do the final placement and blend.

title: Photo Culling & Editor for Emotive Family Photography
desc: I'm seeking a talented freelance photographer/editor to assist with post-production for family and portrait sessions. The ideal candidate will be able to: Review and cull a full gallery of RAW images Thoughtfully narrow the gallery down to the requested final image count Select images that align with my photography style, prioritizing: Genuine connection Authentic emotion Love, warmth, and storytelling Natural interactions and meaningful moments Edit images in a way that is consistent with my established brand aesthetic Maintain natural skin tones, cohesive color grading, and a polished, timeless look Deliver completed galleries within 48 hours of receiving the files Culling: My work focuses on emotive family and portrait photography and human connection, so I'm looking for someone who understands that the strongest image isn't always the most technically perfect one—it's often the image that best captures connection, feeling, and authenticity. When culling, I want someone who can recognize the subtle moments—a hand squeeze, a genuine laugh, a child leaning into a parent, a quiet glance—that often become the most meaningful images in a gallery. I'll always need a handful of traditional portraits where everyone is looking at the camera, but overall, technically perfect images should not be selected over images that tell a stronger emotional story. My editing style is warm, emotive, and timeless, with rich yet natural color tones. I prioritize authentic connection, genuine emotion, and storytelling over perfectly posed images. Edits should feel polished and artistic while remaining true-to-life, preserving natural skin tones, beautiful light, and the emotional integrity of each moment. My work is designed to evoke feeling—capturing love, joy, tenderness, and the quiet in-between moments that tell a family's story: Words to describe my brand: Emotive Storytelling Fine art Warm and inviting Rich tones Authentic Heartfelt Connection-focused Timeless Organic Natural light Genuine emotion Love notes for the soul (your branding phrase) Requirements: -Experience editing family, lifestyle, or portrait photography -Strong culling instincts and storytelling ability -Proficiency in Adobe Lightroom (Photoshop experience is a plus) -Ability to closely match an existing editing style -Reliable communication and consistent turnaround times When applying, please include: Portfolio examples of family or portrait work Examples of galleries you've culled and edited Your typical turnaround time Your rate structure (per gallery or hourly) I'm looking to build a long-term relationship with the right person as my business continues to grow.
task_type: Batch Style-Match Grade for Portrait Gallery (Culling is Human)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the selected family/portrait images) -> image_list_presets / image_apply_preset (apply the photographer's warm-emotive look across the set for consistency) -> image_apply_auto_tone (per-frame baseline) -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_hsl + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (rich-yet-natural tones, true-to-life skin) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The chosen final selects to grade (client culls down to a requested count from a full RAW gallery — selection itself is human)
The photographer's base preset / a reference edited gallery that defines the 'warm, emotive, timeless, rich-but-natural' look to match
Target export specs + 48-hour turnaround expectation per gallery
Confirmation skin tones must stay natural and untouched-looking
note: Connector applies the consistent warm grade/tone across the gallery; the core culling/selection (judging emotional moments) and precise style-matching to the photographer's hand are human.

title: Photo editing
desc: I have some graduation photos that I need to get edits on. I ended up losing all of the good ones on a hard drive that crashed so the ones I have left are not in the best shape, but I need somebody that can turn them into great graduation photos. I need a provider that is really good at editing photos, adjusting color situation and white balance, so I can give a quality product to the customer.
task_type: Photo Retouching / Enhancement — Graduation Photos (color + white balance)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload graduation photos) -> image_apply_auto_tone (recover poor exposure) -> image_adjust_color_temperature (fix white balance) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast / image_adjust_highlights / image_adjust_dark_portions -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The remaining graduation photos that need rescuing (count unspecified — confirm)
Note that source files are in poor shape (low quality) — confirm original resolution
Desired final look (natural/bright graduation style)
note: Color/white-balance/tone recovery is connector-doable; if the photos need genuine restoration (damage, heavy noise, detail reconstruction) that exceeds the tool's adjustment-only capabilities.

title: Photo editing
desc: I need multiple colour versions created from a single image. The subject is a guitar. I need 10 different colour versions created as variants for my website. They need to be very high quality and must reference colours from our catalogue.
task_type: Color Variants — Guitar Recolor (10 catalogue colours)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the master guitar image) -> image_select_by_prompt ('the guitar body') -> image_adjust_hsl / image_adjust_single_color_saturation (shift to each catalogue colour) -> [repeat per colour] -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The single master guitar image
The 10 catalogue colours to produce, with exact hex/Pantone references
Confirm only the body recolors (vs hardware/fretboard staying constant)
Output: high-quality web variants — confirm dimensions
note: HSL/saturation hue-shifting can create recolored variants, but matching exact catalogue colours precisely across all 10 (especially large hue jumps) while keeping wood-grain/finish realistic may need manual masking a human handles.

title: Photo Editing and Enhancement
desc: We are seeking a skilled freelancer to touch up and enhance a photo. The ideal candidate will have experience in photo editing and image editing, with a strong understanding of Adobe Photoshop. The project requires an intermediate level of proficiency and is expected to be completed in less than a month. The budget for this project is between $15 and $35 per hour.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the photo) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast / image_adjust_highlights / image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The photo to touch up / enhance (single image per brief)
What specifically needs enhancing (confirm — brief is generic)
Output format/resolution (confirm with client)
note: Tone/color enhancement is connector-doable; if 'touch up' means object/blemish removal or compositing, that part needs a human.

title: Photo Editing and Retouching
desc: I need someone to help me edit photos using Photoshop or similar software. The task involves enhancing and retouching images to improve their quality and appearance. The ideal candidate should have experience in photo editing and be able to deliver high-quality results efficiently.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload client photos) -> image_apply_auto_tone (base correction) -> image_adjust_exposure / image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast / image_adjust_highlights / image_adjust_dark_portions (fix quality issues) -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation + image_adjust_color_temperature (improve appearance) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The photos to enhance/retouch — confirm count and subject matter (brief is generic; no quantity or subject stated)
What 'improve quality' means here — specific issues to fix (exposure, colour cast, sharpness?) — confirm with client
Output format/dimensions — confirm with client; brief doesn't state
note: Connector covers global tone/colour/light retouching; brief is vague, so if 'retouching' means object/blemish removal or compositing the connector can't do generative inpaint — scope to be confirmed with client.

title: Photo Editing and Retouching Needed for 10 images
desc: I'm looking for a Photo Editor and Retoucher for 10 high-resolution product images. I need to edit, retouch, and color-correct the images professionally. You must have the experience of editing and retouching product images while delivering 100% realistic, authentic studio-quality photographs.
task_type: Photo Color Correction / Enhancement — 10 Product Images
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the 10 product images) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_color_temperature / image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast / image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> image_remove_background (if clean/white product background wanted) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The 10 high-resolution product images (client states 10)
Whether a clean/white studio background is required
Confirm output resolution/format for studio-quality delivery
note: Color correction and tone enhancement are connector-doable; product 'retouch' (dust/scratch removal, reflections, compositing) needed for 'studio-quality' exceeds the tool's adjustment-only capabilities.

title: Photo Editing and WordPress Specialist
desc: Photo Editor & WordPress Photo Content Assistant (Long-Term Position) We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Photo Editor & WordPress Photo Content Assistant to join our team long term. You will work under our head of multi media and assist him. Your primary responsibilities will include: Finding and selecting high-quality, relevant images for blog articles Editing and optimizing photos using Adobe Lightroom (very simple edits and sometimes none) Uploading and formatting images in WordPress Ensuring all images are web-optimized and visually consistent Maintaining attention to detail across all blog content Requirements: Some experience with Adobe Lightroom (entry level) Experience working in WordPress Excellent eye for detail, composition, and image quality Ability to follow brand/style guidelines Good organizational and time management skills Reliable internet connection and ability to communicate consistently Hours & Commitment: Minimum 25 hours per week Long-term opportunity for the right person We’re looking for someone dependable who takes pride in producing clean, polished work and can become a valuable long-term part of our team. If you have any experience in marketing, that would also be considered an asset. When applying, please include: Examples of previous Lightroom editing work Experience with WordPress Your weekly availability The word “Detail” at the top of your application so we know you read the full posting
task_type: Photo Enhancement & Web-Optimization for Blog Images
mcp_workflow: asset_search (StockAsset) -> asset_license_and_download_stock (source relevant blog imagery when client doesn't supply it) -> asset_add_file (ingest client photos) -> image_apply_auto_tone (one-click base correction for the 'very simple edits' brief) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast / image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (light touch-ups for visual consistency) -> image_crop_and_resize (resize/compress to web dimensions for blog/WordPress) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The batch of blog article photos to edit each cycle (client says edits are 'very simple and sometimes none') — confirm typical count per article
Brand/style guidelines doc they reference (so adjustments stay 'visually consistent')
Target web output: pixel width and file-size cap for WordPress — confirm with client; brief doesn't state (e.g. 1200px wide, <300KB JPG)
Topics/keywords for sourcing stock imagery for articles where no photo is provided
note: Connector handles the Lightroom-style editing, web resize and stock sourcing, but cannot upload/format the images inside WordPress — the CMS publishing step is out of scope.

title: Photo editor
desc: Real Estate Photo Editing Specialist – Photos (Vacation Rentals) About Us We are a vacation rental management company based in Saint-Martin (Caribbean) managing approximately 100 villas, apartments and luxury vacation rentals. We are looking for an experienced real estate photo editor who is already familiar with Nodalview HDR photography workflows. The objective is not simply to edit photos, but to create a consistent, premium visual identity across our entire portfolio. Source Material Photos are taken using: Nodalview HDR mode Villas and apartments Sea views Pools Tropical landscapes Luxury interiors Most photos are already correctly exposed through HDR processing. Your role is therefore to enhance and finalize the images while maintaining a natural and realistic look. Required Editing Perspective & Geometry Vertical correction Perspective correction Straightening Lens correction Exposure & Colors Fine exposure adjustments White balance correction Color consistency across the entire property Natural contrast Exterior Enhancement Pool water enhancement Sea color enhancement Sky improvement when necessary Vegetation enhancement Interior Enhancement Improve brightness and clarity Balance indoor/outdoor exposure through windows Correct color casts Create a clean and welcoming atmosphere Object Removal Occasionally remove: Electrical cables Reflections Minor stains Small distracting objects What We DO NOT Want ❌ Fake HDR look ❌ Over-saturated colors ❌ Unrealistic blue pools ❌ Artificial skies ❌ Excessive sharpening ❌ "Real estate brochure" look We prefer: Natural Elegant Premium Realistic Deliverables For each photo: High-resolution JPG Optimized for Airbnb, VRBO, Booking.com and direct website use Consistent visual style across all photos Volume We manage approximately 100 properties. Potential volume: 200 to 1,000 photos per month Continuous long-term collaboration Application Requirements Please provide: Real estate editing portfolio Before/after examples Examples specifically edited from Nodalview HDR photos (if available) Turnaround time Price: per photo per batch of 50 photos per batch of 100 photos Paid Test Shortlisted candidates will receive a paid test consisting of: Interior photos Sea view photos Pool photos Difficult window exposure photos The selected freelancer will become our preferred photo editing partner for long-term collaboration.
task_type: Batch Real-Estate Photo Editing — Vacation Rentals (Nodalview HDR)
mcp_workflow: asset_create_folders (per-property folders) -> asset_add_file (batch-upload each property's HDR JPEGs) -> image_auto_straighten (horizon/vertical straighten) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_color_temperature (white-balance correction) -> image_adjust_exposure / image_adjust_highlights / image_adjust_light_portions / image_adjust_dark_portions (fine exposure, balance indoor vs window-blown exterior) -> image_adjust_single_color_saturation (targeted lift on blues for pool water & sea, greens for vegetation, sky) -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (gentle, natural — avoid over-saturation) -> image_list_presets + image_apply_preset (save one 'natural premium' look and apply it across the whole property for consistency) -> image_crop_and_resize (export high-res JPG) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The batch of Nodalview HDR JPEGs per property (volume: 200-1,000/month across ~100 villas/apartments — confirm first batch size)
A reference / approved sample edit defining the target natural-premium look (client explicitly rejects fake-HDR, over-saturated, unreal blue pools, artificial skies)
Per-property shot list so colour/exposure can be matched consistently across all photos of the same villa
Output spec: high-res JPG optimised for Airbnb / VRBO / Booking.com + direct site (confirm exact px dimensions and sRGB)
List of recurring distractions to remove (electrical cables, reflections, minor stains, small objects) so the human-handoff items are flagged up front
note: Connector covers tone, white balance, exposure balancing, selective pool/sea/sky/vegetation colour and batch consistency, but cannot do true perspective/keystone or lens-distortion correction (only horizon straighten) and cannot remove cables/objects generatively (fill is solid-colour only) — those go to a human.

title: Photo editor
desc: Hi! I want to work with you consistently. I will give you Photoshoots everyday. For example I am taking 400 photos during event, you have to pick from them 100 photos estimatedly and edit just colors, that is it. It will take 1 hour max your time. Can you do one photoshoot for 10$?
task_type: Color Correction / Grading — Event Photo Batch
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the selected event photos) -> image_list_presets / image_apply_preset (apply one consistent grade across the batch) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_color_temperature / image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation -> asset_preview_file
inputs: A representative event photoshoot (client estimates ~100 selects out of ~400 shot)
Confirm the agent/editor makes the selection, or client pre-selects the 100 keepers
A reference look or saved preset for a consistent color grade across the set
Output format/resolution for delivery (confirm)
note: Color-only batch grading is squarely connector-doable; the 'pick 100 from 400' culling judgement is a human/curatorial step the connector can't perform.

title: Photo Restoration for A4 Prints
desc:
I need several old photo scans restored for A4 printing to mount on my wall. 

Restoration is needed due to:
- Color fading
- Scratches or tears
- Blurriness

The main focus should be on improving overall clarity. 

Ideal skills and experience:
- Proficiency in photo editing software like Photoshop
- Experience with photo restoration techniques
- Attention to detail
- Ability to work with a variety of damage types
task_type: Photo Restoration — Old Scans for A4 Print
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload each old scan) -> image_apply_auto_tone (recover faded colour/contrast) -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (restore faded colour) -> image_crop_and_resize (frame and size to A4 at print resolution) -> asset_preview_file (review clarity)
inputs: The set of old scanned photos (confirm how many — brief says 'several')
Target output: A4 print, so confirm scans are high enough resolution for 300dpi A4
Note: scratch/tear repair and de-blur sharpening are required by the brief but exceed the connector's tone/colour tools
note: Connector handles colour-fade recovery, tonal restoration, crop/resize to A4 — but cannot repair scratches/tears (no generative inpaint) or meaningfully de-blur; a human retoucher must do the physical-damage repair.

title: Photo Retoucher for Fine Art & Painting Documentation
desc: I'm looking for a retoucher to help with documentation photos of my paintings for my website and portfolio. I'd like to start with a paid test on a single image. I'll provide the RAW file along with a few reference images that reflect the overall presentation I'm after. My priorities are: - Accurate color reproduction of the artwork - Even, natural wall illumination - Clean, professional gallery-style presentation -Perspective correction if needed -Maintaining a soft, natural feel - reflections removed from painting if needed -general wall cleanup from paint markings I am not looking for an HDR or real-estate photography look. The painting should remain the focus of the image. attached are reference images and current image I would like edited. Overall wall and space needs to have a bright clean look. title: Photoshop Expert - For new corporate website - MUST READ BEFORE BID
desc:
We are looking for a highly skilled photo retoucher and image editor to professionally enhance a complete set of images for our corporate website.

Project Scope

All website images must be professionally edited, optimized, and prepared for a premium corporate online presence. The goal is to create a clean, high-quality, and visually impressive look that reflects the professionalism of our brand.

Required Editing Work
Professional-grade photo retouching
Correct lighting imbalances and exposure issues
Enhance color, contrast, and sharpness
Remove distractions, unwanted objects, and background clutter
Improve composition where required
Ensure images maintain a natural and realistic appearance
Create a consistent visual style across all website images
Resize and optimize images for web performance without compromising quality
Prepare images for desktop, tablet, and mobile responsiveness
Enhance key images to create a strong visual impact on the website
Desired Outcome

The final images should:

Look premium, modern, and professional
Be suitable for a high-end corporate website
Showcase our facilities, operations, equipment, and people in the best possible light
Stand out visually while maintaining authenticity
Be optimized for fast website loading and excellent user experience
Ideal Freelancer
Proven experience in high-end corporate photo retouching
Strong portfolio demonstrating before-and-after image enhancements
Experience preparing images for modern websites
Excellent attention to detail
Ability to deliver consistent, high-quality results within tight deadlines


We will allocate Job to the person who will give us the demo by working on any picture from our website www.atlasfuel.com.au and show the capability.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the corporate website image set) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_exposure + image_adjust_highlights + image_adjust_dark_portions (fix lighting imbalances) -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (color & contrast) -> image_apply_preset (lock one consistent look across all images) -> image_crop_and_resize (web-optimized sizes for desktop/tablet/mobile) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The full set of corporate website images (facilities, operations, equipment, people) — atlasfuel.com.au; confirm count
Demo image: they will pick one picture from www.atlasfuel.com.au to prove capability
Target web dimensions for desktop/tablet/mobile responsive variants (confirm pixel sizes)
A consistent house look/brand reference so the whole set matches
note: Tone, color, contrast, sharpen-via-adjust and resize/optimize are doable; 'remove distractions/unwanted objects/clutter' and 'improve composition' need generative removal the connector lacks.

title: Professional Photoshop Editor Needed for Ongoing eCommerce Product Editing & Color Swapping
desc: We require FAST turnaround on all work. We are looking for a highly skilled and reliable Photoshop professional for ongoing eCommerce product photo manipulation. While the technical requirements of this job are relatively basic, the final execution must be flawless. We frequently need to update our existing product catalog (e.g., for our brand, by changing colors on specific product parts without having to reshoot the images. Examples of Work Needed: Changing the colors of text/design on product labels. Swapping colors on hardware (e.g., winch hooks, accessories) from existing photos. General eCommerce product image manipulation. The Catch: You must be able to change these colors without ruining the original image. The lighting, shadows, and textures must remain completely natural. We cannot accept sloppy masking, flat colors, or unrealistic edits. It might be basic Photoshop work, but we need a true professional's eye for detail. Requirements: Expert proficiency in Adobe Photoshop (specifically masking, color replacement, and non-destructive editing). Strong portfolio showing before-and-after examples of eCommerce product manipulation. Excellent attention to detail edges must be clean, and colors must look authentic. Reliable communication and the ability to handle ongoing, ad-hoc batches of images. Please Note: Simple copy paste Ai edits will not work for our projects, we have the ability to all of this ourselves, we need professional photoshop operators.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload product photo) -> image_select_by_prompt (target the specific part, e.g. 'winch hook' or 'label text') -> image_adjust_hsl / image_adjust_single_color_saturation (shift hue of the masked part while keeping underlying luminance/shadow detail) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The existing product catalog photos needing recolor (winch hooks/hardware/accessories and labels), delivered in ad-hoc batches
The exact part to recolor per image (which hardware piece / which label element)
Target colour per part — brand hex or named colour the new variant should be
Hard requirement from client: lighting, shadows and textures must stay natural — no flat fills (so HSL/hue shift on the mask, not solid-colour fill)
note: Hue/saturation shifts on a prompt-selected mask preserve texture and shadow, but the connector cannot do true Photoshop hue/color-replacement layers or fine edge clean-up; client explicitly rejects flat solid-colour fills, so deep recolors may need a human in Photoshop.

title: Retouching 11-50 Foto Profesional
desc:
Saya memerlukan bantuan untuk meretouch 11-50 foto. Fokus utamanya adalah:

• Menghaluskan detail tanpa membuat hasil terlihat “plastik”  
• Mengoreksi pencahayaan, kontras, dan tone agar konsisten  
• Mempertahankan resolusi asli—tidak boleh ada penurunan kualitas  

Format akhir yang saya butuhkan: JPEG beresolusi tinggi dan, bila memungkinkan, file PSD terpisah layer agar saya bisa melakukan penyesuaian kecil di kemudian hari. Silakan gunakan workflow favorit Anda—Adobe Photoshop, Lightroom, atau aplikasi retouching serupa—selama hasilnya bersih dan natural.

Lampirkan portofolio singkat berisi contoh before-after retouching, serta perkiraan waktu penyelesaian untuk batch ini.

inputs: The batch of 11–50 photos to retouch (confirm exact count)
Deliver high-res JPEG at original resolution — no quality loss
Look: smooth detail without a 'plastic' look; consistent lighting/contrast/tone across the set
Note: layered PSD is requested 'if possible' — connector outputs flattened rasters, not layered PSD
note: Connector does the lighting/contrast/tone correction and consistent batch grade, but cannot deliver a layered PSD (output is flattened); offer flattened high-res JPEGs.

title: Short-Term Rental OTA & PMS Specialist Required
desc:
I am looking for an experienced short-term rental specialist to complete a one-off project across my accommodation portfolio.

The goal is simple: every listing professionally presented, fully synced across all OTAs, connected correctly to our PMS/channel manager, and operating without errors.

Scope of work includes:

• Audit and optimise all existing property listings.• Improve titles, descriptions, amenities, policies, photos, and overall presentation.• Create and/or optimise listings on Airbnb, Expedia, Booking.com, Agoda, Vrbo, Trip.com and other relevant OTAs.• Ensure all listings are consistent across every platform.• Connect all listings correctly to our existing PMS and channel manager.• Verify rates, availability, reservations, messaging, and calendars are syncing correctly.• Identify and fix any mapping, connection, pricing, or listing issues.• Review our OTA setup and recommend any additional platforms that may increase bookings.• Work with our existing photographs by colour correcting, straightening, cropping, sharpening, and improving image quality where required.• Replace outdated images on all live listings and ensure galleries are professionally ordered for maximum conversion.• Test all OTA connections and confirm everything is functioning correctly.

Bonus skills (not essential but highly desirable):

• PMS automation setup• Guest messaging templates• Automated check-in workflows• Review request automation• Upsell workflows• Dynamic pricing setup• Revenue management recommendations

To apply, please provide:

• Your experience with short-term rental properties and accommodation businesses.• Which PMS and channel manager systems you have worked with (Channex, Guesty, Hostaway, Lodgify, Cloudbeds, Hospitable, etc.).• Which photo editing software you use.• Examples of live accommodation listings you have personally optimised.• Your estimated timeframe for completion.

This is a one-off project, however there may be ongoing work available for the right person if the results are excellent.

Success will be measured by having every listing published correctly, fully synchronised, visually consistent, professionally presented, and operating without any calendar, pricing, reservation, or messaging errors.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the existing property photos) -> image_auto_straighten (level horizons/verticals on interior shots) -> image_crop_and_resize (crop to each OTA's gallery ratio) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_highlights/image_adjust_dark_portions (balance interior exposure, correct color casts) -> image_apply_preset (apply ONE saved look across the whole portfolio for visual consistency) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The existing property-photo library to remediate (number of properties / total images — confirm, brief says 'all existing')
Which images are flagged 'outdated' and must be replaced/reprocessed vs. kept
Target gallery dimensions/aspect per OTA (Airbnb, Expedia, Booking.com, Agoda, Vrbo, Trip.com) — confirm the canonical size to crop to
Whether shots are mostly interior (exposure/white-balance heavy) so the batch preset is tuned for that
note: Only the photo bullet is connector-doable (colour correct, straighten, crop, sharpen-via-contrast, consistent batch grade). All the OTA/PMS/channel-manager sync, listing copy, mapping, pricing, and actually uploading/reordering galleries on the platforms are out of scope and need the human specialist.

I need HDR photos edited for real estate. The goal is to make them look vibrant and appealing.

Requirements:
- Edit 3 photos with different exposures
- Focus mainly on interior shots
- Make the photos look vibrant and appealing

Necessary adjustments include:
- Color correction
- Contrast enhancement
- Exposure balance

Ideal Skills and Experience:
- Proficiency in HDR photo editing
- Strong understanding of color theory and contrast adjustment
- Experience with real estate photography

Please provide examples of previous work.

inputs: The 3 real-estate interior photos (stated: 3, mainly interiors)
Whether the 3 are separate listing shots to enhance individually, OR a bracketed set to merge into one HDR frame (connector cannot merge exposure brackets — confirm)
Desired vibrancy level / reference look ('vibrant and appealing' — confirm how punchy)
Output dimensions/format for the MLS or listing — confirm with client
note: Per-image exposure/contrast/color enhancement to a vibrant look is fully doable; if the brief means a true HDR merge of 3 bracketed exposures into one file, the connector has no exposure-bracket/HDR-stack tool.

title: Vintage Portraits Editing
desc:
I need professional editing for some portraits. The photos were taken on a cloudy day and need adjustments. 

Requirements:
- Color correction
- Skin retouching
- I don’t want a spotlight look or anything.  But these photos are going to be mailed out on a baby shower invite so I need them to all be some kind of theme if that makes sense. 

I also want the portraits to have a vintage theme. 

Ideal Skills and Experience:
- Experience in portrait editing
- Strong color correction and retouching skills
- Ability to create a cohesive vintage theme

I need this done ASAP.

Output use is a mailed baby-shower invite — confirm print resolution/dimensions and whether they need print-ready files
note: Color correction and a cohesive vintage grade across the set are fully doable; 'skin retouching' (blemish/clone work) is not — fill is solid-colour only, so detailed retouch needs a human.

title: Cut Out Furniture in Photoshop
desc: I need someone to cut out a piece of furniture in Photoshop. title: Photo Crop & Background Removal
desc:
I have a set of photos that need quick but precise attention. The job is limited to two edits on each file: tidy cropping/resizing to the dimensions I’ll supply and clean background removal so the final images sit on pure transparency. No colour work or creative retouching is required—just accurate cuts and a flawless knockout.

Please deliver each photo back in PNG (transparent) and the original resolution JPG for archive. If you prefer Adobe Photoshop, Affinity, or a comparable editor, that’s fine by me; consistency across all images is what matters.

Acceptance criteria  
• Precise crop matches the pixel dimensions I provide  
• Background fully removed with no halos or artefacts at 100 % zoom  
• Files returned in both PNG (transparent) and source-size JPG

Let me know how many images you can handle in 24 hours and I’ll send them straight over.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the photo set) -> image_crop_and_resize (crop/resize to the exact pixel dimensions the client supplies) -> image_remove_background (clean knockout to pure transparency) -> asset_preview_file (verify no halos at 100%) -> export transparent PNG + keep source-size JPG
inputs: The set of photos (count TBD — client asks how many can be done in 24h)
The exact pixel crop dimensions the client will supply (required, not stated)
Two deliverables per file: transparent PNG + original-resolution JPG for archive
Acceptance: no halos/artefacts at 100% zoom, consistent treatment across all images

title: Photo editing - Background removal

Output: transparent-background PNG for each (so they drop into PowerPoint)
Confirm whether any shadow should be kept or fully removed

title: Product Photo Background Removal
desc:
I have between one and ten product shots that need their backgrounds removed cleanly. The finished files must be delivered as high-resolution PNGs with full transparency—no stray pixels, halos, or color fringing. Consistency across the set is crucial because these images will be used in an e-commerce catalog.

Please keep each image at its original resolution and color profile; I’ll handle any resizing afterward. A layered PSD alongside the transparent PNGs would be a welcome extra, although not mandatory.

Speed matters, but precision matters more. Let me know your expected turnaround time and the tools you’ll use (Photoshop, Affinity Photo, or similar). I’ll inspect every edge at 100 % zoom, so clean masks and intact product details are the acceptance criteria.

Deliverables
• 1–10 product images with backgrounds removed, supplied as transparent PNGs  
• Optional: corresponding layered PSD files for future edits

If that fits your skill set and schedule, I’m ready to get started right away.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the 1-10 product shots) -> image_select_subject (isolate the product) -> image_remove_background (clean transparent cutout, no halos/fringing) -> asset_preview_file (inspect edges before export as transparent PNG at original resolution)
inputs: The 1-10 product photos for the e-commerce catalog
Output: high-resolution transparent PNG, original resolution and color profile preserved
Clean edges required (client inspects at 100% zoom — no stray pixels/halos/fringing)
Optional layered PSD per image (note: connector exports PNG, not layered PSD)

title: Recreate Icon in HD Formats
desc:
I already have the original icon—unfortunately it is only low-resolution. I need an exact, pixel-perfect recreation in high definition, keeping every color, line, and proportion identical. No stylistic tweaks; simply a clean, crisp rebuild.

Deliverables  
• PNG, SVG, and JPEG versions, each on a transparent background where applicable  
• At least 1024 px square for the raster files, with perfect scalability for the SVG  
• Ready for immediate use in web and print

Please include a couple of past icon or logo redraws you have handled so I can see the quality of your vector work. If you can turn this around quickly and match the original flawlessly, let’s get started right away.

mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the low-res icon) -> image_remove_background (produce the transparent cutout) -> image_vectorize (rebuild as clean scalable vector paths, preserving colours/lines/proportions) -> document_render_vector (export SVG) -> document_render_vector (export PNG ≥1024px square, transparent) -> document_render_vector (export JPEG) -> asset_preview_file (verify exact match)
inputs: The existing low-resolution icon file to recreate (the asset the client already has)
Required exports: SVG (scalable), PNG ≥1024px square (transparent), JPEG

Required background: pure white (#FFFFFF) flat

desc: I run an online sports card store (pushingpacks.com) and need a photo editor to process product images taken on iPhone for upload to Shopify. What the job involves: Remove background from product photos (trading cards, graded PSA slabs, sealed hobby boxes) Center the product on a clean white or transparent background Resize and export to Shopify's recommended dimensions (2048 x 2048px) Deliver files named to match Shopify product handles for easy upload Maintain a consistent look across all product images Volume: Approximately 85 products with 2- 5 images each to start, with ongoing work as new inventory is added. Requirements: Proficient in Photoshop, Canva Pro, or equivalent Experience with Shopify product image standards Clean, consistent results — cards must be straight, centered, no shadows Fast turnaround (24–48 hours per batch) Attention to detail — these are collectibles worth up to $2,000 Deliverables: PNG files with transparent or white background 2048 x 2048px square format Files named by product handle (e.g. peyton-manning-1998-bowman-1-psa-10.png) Category to post under: Design & Creative → Photo Editing Skills to tag: Photo Editing, Adobe Photoshop, Background Removal, Shopify, Product Photography Contract type: Ongoing/hourly or fixed-price per batch — fixed price per image works best for this type of work so you're not paying for slow editors.

mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload each iPhone product photo: trading cards, PSA slabs, sealed hobby boxes) -> image_remove_background (transparent cutout) -> image_auto_straighten (cards must be straight) -> image_crop_and_resize (center on white/transparent canvas, output 2048x2048 square) -> change_background_color (set clean white where white-bg is wanted) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: ~85 products x 2-5 images each (iPhone photos of trading cards, graded PSA slabs, sealed hobby boxes) to start, ongoing
Output: 2048x2048px square PNG, transparent OR clean white background
Product centered, straight, no shadows (consistent look across all)
Files named to match Shopify product handles (e.g. peyton-manning-1998-bowman-1-psa-10.png) — confirm handle list/mapping with client
Constraint: real photo, no color/detail loss (collectibles up to $2,000)

title: Batch Photo Editing Project
desc:
I have a set of roughly 11–50 images that need professional attention. The focus is photo editing—clean, consistent, and ready for use online and in print. Each file may require a mix of light retouching, colour correction, or full background removal; I’ll share exact notes once you see the shots, but I’m happy for you to recommend the best approach image by image.

The workflow can be in Adobe Photoshop or Lightroom as long as the final results are sharp, natural-looking, and colour-accurate.

Deliverables  
• Edited high-resolution JPEGs (sRGB, 300 dpi)  
• Layered PSD or TIFF originals so future tweaks remain possible  
• Quick preview thumbnails for easy review  

Please keep naming consistent with the originals and preserve any embedded metadata.

When the first 5-image sample looks right, the rest can follow the same style for a smooth sign-off.

First 5-image sample to set the consistent house style before the rest follow
Layered PSD/TIFF originals requested (note: connector exports flattened rasters, not layered files)

desc: I have developed a series of four Scottish bird-themed T-shirt designs and require an experienced apparel graphic designer to adapt the artwork for garment printing. The artwork is already created (see example). The task is not to redesign the concepts, but to refine them into premium T-shirt graphics suitable for DTF printing. Use the T-shirt colour as part of the design. Reduce print coverage and ink. Introduce halftone and distressed print effects. Create feathered/faded edges. Preserve typography, slogans and branding. title: Convert Flat Logo to Clean Vector Redraw (AI, SVG, EPS) + Refinements for Brand Use
desc: I have an existing logo concept created (flat design). I am looking for a professional designer to recreate this logo as clean, original vector artwork and refine it for professional use. The goal is to preserve the current concept while improving overall polish, balance, and scalability so the logo is suitable for commercial use and trademark consideration. Scope of Work * Manually recreate the existing logo as fully editable vector artwork (not a simple trace) * Refine spacing, alignment, and typography where needed for a more professional finish * Ensure the final design is clean, original, and not based on Canva assets, templates, or stock elements * Maintain the overall concept and layout while improving visual quality * The wordmark should be designed in a flexible, modular way so it can be reused across future service lines while maintaining consistent brand identity What I will provide * PNG and PDF exports of the current Canva logo * Notes on elements to keep, adjust, or improve Deliverables * Editable vector files: AI, SVG, EPS * High-resolution transparent PNG * Font recommendations or font files (if applicable) * Final logo suitable for scaling, branding, and commercial use Important Requirements * No stock icons or templates * No Canva elements reused in final artwork * Final design must be original vector work created in professional software * Must be suitable for future brand use and trademark application Logo Style Combination mark (wordmark + icon). The structure is already defined; this project focuses on rebuilding it professionally in vector format with minor refinements for clarity and balance. Application Requirement Please include relevant examples of logo/vector work you’ve completed. Start your proposal with the word “Vector” to confirm you’ve read the full description.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the supplied flat-logo PNG) -> image_vectorize (trace raster combination mark to clean vector paths) -> document_render_vector (export the traced vector as AI, EPS, SVG, and transparent PNG) -> font_recommend (suggest a wordmark typeface to match) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The existing Canva logo as PNG and PDF exports (client states they will provide both)
It is a combination mark (wordmark + icon) — confirm both elements are in the supplied raster at highest available resolution
Client's notes on which elements to keep / adjust / improve
Required output set: editable AI + SVG + EPS, plus high-res transparent PNG
Wordmark text and intended brand name (to drive font_recommend); request any current font name if known
note: Connector auto-vectorizes to paths but cannot 'manually redraw' as bespoke original art or refine spacing/typography per the client's no-Canva/trademark-grade requirement; a human is needed to clean nodes and re-letter the wordmark.

I have selected my final logo: a standalone "Flow and Circularity" emblem. Your job is to prepare this into a pixel-perfect, mathematically balanced file package. 1. I need the following for logo design: • High-resolution SVG files (optimized for web) • PNG files with transparent background in multiple sizes (512px, 1024px, 2000px, and 5000px+) • EPS and PDF versions (for print) • High-resolution JPG versions • Editable source files (.AI or Figma preferred) 2. Need these variations for the same logo: • Full colour version • Black version • White version (for dark backgrounds) • Monochrome / grayscale version • Vertical / Stacked version • Icon-only version • Text-only version 3. I will provide: • Exact color codes (Hex) • Font name(s), weight and styles used in the logo • Minimum clear space / exclusion zone and minimum size recommendations for logo design 4. Other Important Points • Please confirm I will receive full commercial ownership and rights to the final designs. • Also let me know the timeline for final delivery.

title: Jewellery retouch

Required Deliverables JPG files PNG files (transparent background) Quality Standards The final result must match the quality level of the attached sample images — clean metal, sharp and well-defined stones, accurate reflections, consistent lighting, clean background, and stable quality across the entire volume. Consistency across thousands of images matters just as much as the quality of a single image. What to Include in Your Proposal 1-Price per image, and whether you offer special bulk pricing for large volumes. 2-Expected turnaround time — per image or per batch — and how many images you can deliver per day. 3-Links to previous work specifically in jewelry retouching (not general retouching). 4-How you ensure consistent quality across large volumes. Selection Criteria: Final selection will be based on the best balance of highest quality and lowest price. Please make sure your sample work reflects the quality level shown in our reference images, and that your pricing is competitive for high-volume work. We prefer working with someone who has proven experience in jewelry retouching and can commit to a steady monthly volume throughout the year.

I do NOT want a redesign or new concept — I want the same logo, same colors, same layout, and same style, professionally converted into production-ready files. title: Pastel Social Media Photo Edits
desc:
I have a batch of 11-30 images that I’ll be posting across Instagram, Facebook, and Pinterest, and I need them to share the same soft, pastel aesthetic. Each file already has solid composition; what I’m missing is the finishing touch that makes a feed look cohesive.

Here’s exactly what I’m after:
• Subtle color correction that shifts the palette toward gentle, pastel tones while keeping skin tones believable.  
• Clean background removal wherever distractions exist so products and people sit on a neutral, scroll-stopping backdrop.  
• Light retouching to clear up minor blemishes and stray hairs without giving the photos an over-edited feel.  

Please return the images in both high-resolution JPG (for archiving) and optimized PNG or JPG sized for social platforms. Consistent naming and layered PSDs are a plus if you work in Photoshop. Turnaround within a week would be ideal, but let me know if you need a bit more time; quality comes first.

title: Intricate Ornamental Logo Vectorization
desc:
I have an AI-generated emblem that combines a lion motif with fine ornamental leaf work. I need the artwork meticulously redrawn as a true vector so every swirl, vein, and contour holds up at billboard scale yet still looks crisp on a favicon.

Scope  
• Preserve the full, high-detail styling—no simplification.  
• Ensure it scales flawlessly for both print and digital placements, from letterpress to web banners.  
• Keep the file structure clean: logical groups, minimal anchor points, and editable text where applicable.

Required deliverables  
1. Layered files that open natively in Affinity Designer, DaVinci Resolve’s Fusion page, and Canva.  
2. Additional exports in Adobe Illustrator (.ai) and pure SVG.  
3. Color-accurate master plus a single-color version suitable for foil or embossing.  

Acceptance criteria  
• Visual fidelity to the original AI concept, including the lion’s facial features and every ornamental leaf.  
• No pixel data or raster artifacts; all paths must be vector.  
• Proof of compatibility shown via a quick screen recording or stills of the file open in the requested apps.  

If you’re comfortable handling complex filigree and can deliver clean, professional vectors, I’m ready to move forward.

Part of the logo is uploaded.

I am ready to pay 300 to 350INR for this task, so bid only if you can do it in this cost and within 3 to 4 days. 

Write ' I have read the brief' before the bid, only then will interact with the freelancer.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload the AI-generated lion/ornamental-leaf emblem raster) -> image_vectorize (high-detail trace preserving swirls, veins, lion facial features, no simplification) -> document_render_vector exported as AI and SVG (color-accurate master) -> image_fill_area / vectorize for a single-color version suitable for foil/embossing -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The AI-generated emblem raster (lion motif + ornamental leaf filigree) — partial uploaded
Full high-detail trace, no simplification; must hold from favicon to billboard
Exports: Adobe Illustrator (.ai) + pure SVG; plus a single-color foil/emboss version
Native Affinity Designer / DaVinci Fusion / Canva layered files (note: connector cannot produce these native formats)
note: Auto-vectorize plus AI/SVG/PNG export and a single-color version are doable; native Affinity/Fusion/Canva layered files and hand-cleaned logical groups/editable text are not.

title: Adult Content Video Editor
desc:
I need a skilled video editor for adult content with a quick turnaround. 

Requirements:
- Edit raw footage into 5-20 minute videos (depending on the project)
- Color correction and sound optimization
- Create thumbnail and 30-second trailer
- Pull 5 stills and 5 videos/gifs per project
- Style: Solo Femdom POV
- No background music, just natural sounds

Deliver in MP4 format. Ideal skills include video editing, familiarity with adult content, and ability to meet deadlines. This would become an ongoing job if successful. I currently pay £160 per 8 videos so would need someone reliable and able to communicate efficiently. Please don’t hesitate to ask any questions.

mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload raw footage) -> video_create_quick_cut (auto-assemble the 5-20 min cut and pull a 30s trailer from the same footage) -> media_enhance_speech (sound optimization, natural audio cleanup since no music wanted) -> image_apply_auto_tone + image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (grade the exported stills / thumbnail frame) -> asset_preview_file
