title: Daily Promotional Video Editing
desc:
Fresh promotional footage lands on my desk every day, and I need it shaped into an engaging, public-facing video within 24 hours of delivery. Your main tasks are straightforward:

• Trim and sequence the raw clips into a clear, dynamic story  
• Blend in background music that suits the message (I can supply tracks or you can source royalty-free options)  
• Sync and balance the provided voice-over so it sits cleanly in the mix  
• Export a polished 1080p (or higher) MP4 plus the editable project file

The style should feel lively and accessible to the general public—nothing overly corporate or niche. Smooth transitions, tasteful color correction, and the occasional caption or lower-third are welcome if they help keep viewers’ attention, but the core priority is quick, consistent turnaround and audio-visual polish that makes each spot ready to post the same day it’s filmed.

Raw clips and voice-over arrive via shared drive each afternoon; simply drop the finished files back in the folder and let me know they’re done. If this daily rhythm fits your workflow, I’m ready to start right away.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the day's raw promo clips) -> video_create_quick_cut (auto trim/sequence into a dynamic story cut) -> video_resize (export at 1080p / 16:9, or other named delivery frame) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The day's raw promotional clips (shared-drive link/upload — arrives each afternoon)
Delivery spec: 1080p MP4 or higher — confirm exact frame (16:9 1920x1080 assumed)
The supplied voice-over track + chosen background music (connector cannot mix/sync audio — client must accept audio is added downstream)
Any required captions/lower-thirds copy (connector cannot burn in lower-thirds)
note: Auto trim/sequence and 1080p export are doable; background-music blending, voice-over sync/balance, captions/lower-thirds, and handing back an editable NLE project file are not — those need a human editor in an NLE.

title: Dynamic IG Reels Editor
desc:
I’m looking for an experienced short-form video editor who can turn my raw, educational footage into punchy, dynamic Instagram Reels that boost reach and engagement. My content is strictly “how-to” and informative, but I need it to feel lively—not a lecture—so fast pacing, clever cuts, and eye-catching motion are essential.

Here’s what I’m after:  
• A dynamic, engaging style that holds viewers in the first three seconds.  
• Consistent use of text overlays to highlight key takeaways and calls-to-action.  
• Well-synced music and sound effects that complement, not distract.  
• Smooth, creative transitions that keep the flow energetic across 15- to 30-second reels.  

I’ll supply the talking-head clips, B-roll, and any branding assets. You take care of sequencing, colour correction, audio balancing, caption placement, and final export in the correct vertical format (1080×1920). Turnaround is important; ideally each reel would be ready within three days of file delivery so I can maintain a regular posting cadence.

If you have a portfolio that showcases educational or explainer content edited in a TikTok/IG style, I’d love to see it. Let me know how you like to collaborate and how many revisions are included, and we’ll get started.
task_type: IG Reels — Vertical Highlight Cut + Reframe
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (talking-head clips + B-roll) -> video_create_quick_cut (fast-paced sequence with cuts) -> video_resize (export vertical 1080x1920 for Reels) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The raw talking-head clips + B-roll for each reel (and branding assets)
Target length 15-30s and output 1080x1920 vertical (stated) — confirm per-reel runtime
Music/SFX tracks (connector cannot place or balance audio)
Caption/CTA copy and key takeaways (connector cannot place text overlays/captions)
note: Auto cut + vertical 1080x1920 reframe are doable; text overlays/caption placement, music+SFX sync/balance, and bespoke motion graphics are not — those need a human editor.

title: Dynamic Social Reels & Shorts Editing
desc:
I’m looking for an editor who can take raw footage and turn it into eye-catching, fast-paced reels and YouTube Shorts that keep viewers watching until the very last frame. The goal is pure social media engagement, so everything you create should be optimised for vertical viewing, snappy pacing, and instant visual impact.

Here’s what I expect:
• Turnaround of multiple 15-60 second edits, ready for upload on Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts.  
• Energetic cuts, rhythmic beat-syncing, punchy text call-outs, and the kind of trending effects that stop thumbs from scrolling.  
• Basic colour grading and sound balancing so every clip feels polished and cinematic—even at high speed.  
• Delivery in 1080 × 1920 (and 1920 × 1080 when needed), H.264, plus the project files so I can make tweaks later.

Send a quick sample or link that shows you’ve mastered dynamic editing, and let me know how soon you can start. Once we click, I’ll share the footage and we’ll get straight into creating scroll-stopping content together.
task_type: Short-Form Video Cut + Vertical Reframe + Grade
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload raw footage) -> video_create_quick_cut (snappy 15-60s edit) -> video_resize (1080x1920 vertical; also 1920x1080 when needed, H.264) -> image_apply_auto_tone + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (basic punchy grade on frames) -> media_enhance_speech (sound balancing) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Raw footage (shared after start)
Multiple 15-60s edits; deliver 1080x1920 (and 1920x1080 when needed), H.264
Platforms: Instagram Reels + YouTube Shorts
Text call-outs, trending effects and beat-sync music (note: connector cannot add these)
note: Connector does the cut, vertical reframe, grade and audio balance; beat-syncing, text call-outs, trending effects and project-file delivery need an NLE.

title: Engaging Travel Video Editor Required
desc:
# Video Editor Needed for Travel/Cinematic Video

## Project Overview

I am looking for a skilled video editor to edit my travel video and create a professional, cinematic, and engaging final video for social media.

## Requirements

* Edit raw video clips into a smooth and visually appealing video.
* Add cinematic transitions and effects where appropriate.
* Color correction and color grading.
* Add suitable background music (copyright-free).
* Sync video clips with music beats.
* Add text overlays/titles if needed.
* Improve video quality and stabilization if required.
* Deliver high-quality final output (1080p or 4K).

## Video Details

* Type: Travel/Lifestyle Video
* Platform: Instagram Reel / YouTube / Social Media
* Expected Duration: 1–3 minutes
* Raw Footage: Will be shared after project award

## What to Include in Your Proposal

* Portfolio or previous work samples.
* Estimated delivery time.
* Total project cost.
* Experience with travel/cinematic video editing.

## Preferred Style

* Aesthetic and elegant editing style.
* Smooth transitions.
* Cinematic storytelling.
* Modern social media-friendly look.

Looking forward to working with a creative editor who can make the video visually stunning and engaging.
task_type: Travel Video Cut + Color Grade + Platform Reframe
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload raw travel clips) -> video_create_quick_cut (assemble a smooth 1-3 min cinematic cut) -> image_apply_auto_tone + image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (cinematic grade on representative frames) -> video_resize (reframe per platform: 9:16 Reel and/or 16:9 YouTube) -> asset_preview_file (1080p/4K)
inputs: Raw travel/lifestyle footage (shared after award)
Final length 1-3 min; deliver 1080p or 4K
Platforms: Instagram Reel (9:16) and YouTube/social (16:9) — confirm which ratios
Style reference: aesthetic/elegant/cinematic look
Copyright-free music track and text/titles (note: connector cannot add these)
note: Connector does the cut, grade and platform reframe; cinematic transitions, beat-synced music, text overlays and stabilization need a video editor.

title: Graduation Highlight Video Montage
desc:
I have a folder full of raw phone videos, short camera clips, and hundreds of photos covering an entire two years of school including 11th and 12th I need all of that material shaped into one cohesive story, running roughly 6–10 minutes.

Here’s the feel I’m after: open with a warm, nostalgic build-up that captures the years emotion, then shift into a faster, fun-spirited pace for the fun time that followed. Think smooth pacing, tasteful transitions, upbeat graphics where they make sense, and music cues that amplify both moods without overpowering the speeches.

Deliverables  
• Edited video (MP4, 1080p or better) between 6–10 minutes  
• Clean audio mix, colour correction, and simple titles/lower thirds for key moments  
• One round of revisions after the first cut so we can fine-tune timing or swap clips if needed

I’ll provide:  
– A download link to all footage and photos (organised by scene)  
– A shortlist of must-include moments and any favourite songs (royalty-free or I can supply licences)

If you’re comfortable blending emotional storytelling with lively edits and can turn around polished social-ready files, this project should be straightforward. Let me know your usual turnaround time and any sample work that matches this vibe, and I’ll share the footage right away.
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the raw phone videos + camera clips) -> video_create_quick_cut (assemble a paced 6-10 min highlight story from the footage) -> video_resize (export 1080p MP4, 16:9 or social vertical as needed) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Download link to the full footage folder (raw phone videos + short camera clips), organised by scene
The shortlist of must-include moments (so the cut prioritises them)
Target length 6-10 min and output spec: MP4 1080p or better — confirm aspect (16:9 vs vertical)
Chosen songs / licences (connector cannot place music or mix audio — supplied for the downstream audio pass)
note: The auto highlight cut from footage + 1080p export is doable; the hundreds of still photos won't ingest into the video cut, and the music mix, color grade, titles/lower-thirds, and emotional pacing tweaks need a human in an NLE.

title: Photo Colour Correction for Mood
desc:
I have a batch of photos that need a careful colour treatment to evoke a distinct mood and artistic feel—not simply boosting saturation, but balancing tones, shadows and highlights so the viewer senses the intended atmosphere at first glance.  

The raw or high-resolution originals will be supplied immediately after we agree on the job. Please work in Photoshop, Lightroom, Capture One or any comparable professional tool; non-destructive editing is essential because I may ask for minor tweaks once I review your first pass.  

Turnaround is tight: I’d like the fully corrected images back within a couple of days.  

Deliverables  
• Colour-graded, high-resolution JPEGs (sRGB)  
• Layered PSD or TIFF files with adjustments intact  
• Brief note on the primary adjustments or LUTs used  

I will sign off once the images share a coherent mood, preserve natural skin tones where relevant, and display no clipping or banding. If this matches your skill set and timeframe, let’s get started right away.
task_type: Color Correction / Mood Grading — Photo Batch
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (ingest each supplied RAW/high-res original) -> image_apply_auto_tone (neutralise exposure baseline before stylising) -> image_adjust_color_temperature (set the mood's warm/cool cast) -> image_adjust_highlights + image_adjust_dark_portions (balance shadows/highlights to avoid clipping & banding) -> image_adjust_hsl (shape the atmospheric palette while protecting skin hues) -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (lift mood without crushing skin tones) -> image_list_presets/image_apply_preset (save the first-pass look and reapply across the rest of the batch for a coherent grade) -> asset_preview_file (client review of first pass for minor tweaks)
inputs: The batch of RAW / high-resolution original photos (count not stated — confirm how many files)
The exact mood / reference look wanted (e.g. moody-cool, warm-nostalgic) — ask for 1–2 reference images or a LUT they like
Which images contain people so skin tones can be protected during the HSL/saturation grade
Output: colour-graded sRGB JPEGs at full resolution (delivery dimensions — confirm)
Whether a single consistent grade applies to the whole set or per-image looks are wanted
note: Connector outputs graded raster (JPEG/PNG); it cannot deliver the requested layered PSD/TIFF with live adjustment layers, and 'LUTs used' would be a written note, not an exported .cube.

title: Photo colour edit - lifestyle images + ghost mannequin
desc: For the lifestyle images of model with the black pants - I need the pants to be colour corrected to brown to match PANTONE 19-1322 TPG (brown stone). For the ghost mannequin images of the red bra, I need this to be colour corrected to navy to match PANTONE 19-4029 TPG (navy peony). Keep high resolution and make the colour and shadows look natural - especially for the lifestyle images with the model. I will add more images and reference colours via message
task_type: Targeted Color Replacement — Garment Recolor to Pantone
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload lifestyle + ghost-mannequin images) -> image_select_by_prompt ('the black pants' / 'the red bra' to isolate the garment) -> image_adjust_hsl / image_adjust_single_color_saturation (shift hue toward the target Pantone while preserving shadow/fabric detail) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Lifestyle images of the model in black pants (recolor to PANTONE 19-1322 TPG, brown stone)
Ghost-mannequin images of the red bra (recolor to PANTONE 19-4029 TPG, navy peony)
Pantone target hex/RGB equivalents for both colours
Note: client will send more images/reference colours via message
Requirement: keep high resolution; shadows must stay natural
note: HSL/single-color hue shift can recolor the garment, but matching an exact Pantone precisely while keeping shadow realism (especially black->brown which has little hue to shift) may need manual masking/dodge-burn a human handles.

title: Polished Tutorial Video Editing
desc:
I record 5–15 minute tutorial videos that still feel rough around the edges. What I need is your post-production touch so every upload looks and sounds studio-made.

Here is the workflow: you trim away dead space and stumbles, weave in smooth transitions, balance the audio, and lay in background music or subtle FX that enhance rather than distract. A light colour grade should keep skin tones natural and the overall look consistent across my channel. Whenever a key step or concept appears on screen, add clean lower-thirds, titles, or captions so viewers can follow along even with the volume low.

The finished file must come back to me as a high-quality MP4 ready for immediate upload to YouTube, with a turnaround that lets me stay on my weekly release schedule. I do expect revisions if something feels off, but clear communication and timestamps usually keep that quick.

If you regularly edit YouTube tutorials and understand audience retention tactics—jump-cuts, pacing, hook intros—your style will fit perfectly. Share a link to recent projects that showcase similar work; seeing before-and-after examples is even better.

I’m eager to find a reliable editor I can return to for every new tutorial, so show me what you can do and let’s make these lessons shine.

Add clean, synchronized on-screen captions adhering to provided style guidelines. Perform basic color correction and audio leveling to ensure professional sound quality. Source and integrate royalty-free background music and subtle sound effects where appropriate. Send Your Proposal! If you have a strong eye for timing and a portfolio packed with engaging, high-retention vertical video edits, we want to work with you. Please submit your proposal along with 2 or 3 links to short-form videos you have recently edited. We are ready to fund the escrow and kick off immediately—look forward to reading your proposals!

title: Smooth Cinematic Nepal Reels Editor
desc:
I’m building a series of Instagram Reels shot across Nepal and I want each clip to feel polished, immersive, and cinematic. The footage ranges from spontaneous street moments to sweeping mountain drone shots—so it doesn’t neatly fit into the usual “travel, culture, or food” buckets. What matters most is telling a compelling visual story that instantly hooks viewers and makes them linger past the three-second mark.

Here’s the editing approach I need:
• Overall look: smooth, cinematic pacing rather than frenetic jump-cuts.  
• Must-have touches: tasteful slow-motion effects where they elevate emotion, plus seamless transitions that keep scenes flowing organically.  
• Consistent grade: warm, true-to-life tones that celebrate Nepal’s natural colours without over-saturation.

Typical workload will be 3–5 Reels per batch, each 25–30 seconds, delivered in vertical 9:16, ready for upload (H.264, 1080 × 1920). Please supply both the final exports and the project files so I can tweak captions later if needed.

Acceptance criteria:  
1. Every Reel opens with an attention-grabbing frame and retains smooth motion throughout.  
2. Slow-mo segments are stabilised and properly blended with real-time footage.  
3. Transitions feel invisible—no hard cuts unless they serve a specific beat.  
4. Colour grading is consistent across the batch.  
5. Final files pass Instagram’s compression without noticeable artefacts.

If this sounds like your style, send over a quick sample Reel or portfolio link that shows cinematic editing with slow-mo and seamless transitions. Excited to collaborate and showcase Nepal beautifully.
task_type: Reel Cut + Vertical Reframe + Color Grade
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload Nepal footage: street + mountain drone clips) -> video_create_quick_cut (assemble a 25-30s cinematic cut per reel, attention-grabbing opening frame) -> video_resize (reframe to vertical 9:16, 1080x1920, H.264) -> image_apply_auto_tone + image_adjust_color_temperature (warm, true-to-life) + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (lifted but not over-saturated) on representative frames to define the grade -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Raw Nepal footage per batch (3-5 reels worth; street + drone clips)
Per-reel length 25-30s, vertical 9:16, export 1080x1920 H.264
Grade direction: warm, true-to-life, no over-saturation — supply a reference reel/look
Which clips/moments should be slow-mo (note: connector cannot retime/slow footage)
note: Connector handles the cut, 9:16 reframe and grade; slow-motion retiming, custom seamless transitions and project-file delivery need an NLE.

desc: Brochure Design & Brand Template Creation for Engineering / Industrial Company We are looking for an experienced graphic designer to redesign and modernise a suite of brochures for Polymax, one of the UK’s leading manufacturers and distributors of rubber solutions and engineering products. The project forms part of a wider rebrand alongside the launch of a new contemporary website, so the brochure designs must align closely with the new digital brand style. Scope of Work The project includes: Redesign of existing brochures Creation of a new corporate brochure Design of multiple new product family brochures Creation of editable brochure templates for future internal use Development of a consistent visual style system across all brochures Creation of a set of custom icons for use in both brochures and on the website One brochure family (O-rings) will require: A marketing-focused version A more technical version with expanded engineering information These must be visually differentiated while remaining within the same overall brand system. Design Requirements The brochures should: Reflect the styling of the new website Be modern, technical, professional and visually engaging Appeal primarily to engineers, technical buyers and industrial distributors Use strong imagery with concise copy Incorporate icons, tables, bullet points and clean layouts Be adaptable for use by a sister company (alternative colour variants and logos) The initial brochure format will be: A5 Print-ready and digital PDF formats Deliverables The successful freelancer will provide: Print-ready PDF artwork Adobe InDesign packaged files with bleed setup Editable InDesign templates SVG and PNG icon assets created during the project Assets Provided We will supply: Existing brochures Draft brochure content Product information Brand guidance Images and graphics Icon requirements list Working Style This will be a collaborative and iterative project with several rounds of feedback. The Marketing Manager will act as the main point of contact throughout the project. Ideal Freelancer We are looking for someone with: Strong brochure/editorial design experience Experience creating corporate or industrial marketing materials Excellent typography and layout skills Ability to create clean technical visuals/icons Strong Adobe InDesign skills Attention to detail and consistency across multiple documents Experience working with engineering, manufacturing or industrial brands would be highly beneficial. Timeline Target completion dates: Corporate, O-Rings & Gaskets brochures: 26 June 2026 AV Mounts & Mouldings brochures: 17 July 2026 Site Safety, Fenders, Silicone & Technical O-Ring brochures: 31 July 2026

title: Nagpur Silver Jewelry Photoshoot
desc:
I have about 2,000 pieces of 925 sterling-silver jewellery that now need clean, consistent imagery for our online catalogue and marketplaces. All shots must be taken here in Nagpur, preferably in a simple studio set-up so the metalwork stays the hero and lighting is fully controlled.

For most pieces a plain white sweep is enough, but a few designs could benefit from a quick prop to help them stand upright—simple jewellery stands or holders will do. If a piece already balances well on its own, no prop is required; I’ll decide on the day which ones need the extra support.

After shooting, each file has to be delivered on pure #FFFFFF with no visible shadows or colour cast. I’m comfortable if you run the clean-up through an AI workflow, Photoshop, or Lightroom—as long as the final images are crisp, evenly exposed and ready for immediate upload. High-resolution JPEGs (3,000 px on the long edge) plus the corresponding layered PSDs are the deliverables I expect.

Because volume is high, batch processing skills and an organised naming convention are essential. Share a quick sample workflow or a before/after if you have one shot on an iPhone; smartphone capture is perfectly fine as long as the finished result meets standard jewellery e-commerce quality.

I’d like a realistic timeline and checkpoint plan—for example, a small pilot batch first, followed by rolling deliveries until all 2,000 pieces are complete. Let me know your availability to start in Nagpur and how many finished images you can comfortably supply per week.
task_type: Product Photo Cleanup — Pure-White Jewelry Catalog (batch)
mcp_workflow: asset_create_folders (one intake/output set, organised naming for the ~2000 pieces) -> asset_add_file (ingest each shot JPEG, looped) -> image_apply_auto_tone (even exposure, remove colour cast on the silver) -> image_remove_background (clean cut-out of each piece, kills shadows) -> image_fill_area on the inverted/empty background with #FFFFFF (guarantee pure white, no cast) [alt: change_background_color to #FFFFFF] -> image_crop_and_resize (3000 px long edge, consistent framing) -> asset_preview_file (pilot-batch checkpoint before rolling deliveries)
inputs: The studio-shot product JPEGs (~2,000 pieces of 925 sterling-silver jewellery; capture is the client/photographer's job, not the connector's)
Exact output spec already stated: pure #FFFFFF background, no visible shadow/colour cast, 3,000 px on the long edge, high-res JPEG
Naming convention / SKU mapping for the organised batch
Pilot-batch size for first checkpoint (client wants a small pilot then rolling deliveries — confirm count)
Whether any pieces are shot on prop stands that must also be cut away cleanly
note: Connector only handles post (white-bg cleanup/resize); the photoshoot in Nagpur is out of scope, and it cannot deliver the requested layered PSDs — only flattened JPEG/PNG.

title: Horticulture Company Profile With Photos
desc:
I need a fresh, visually engaging company profile that I can send straight to the printer and hand out at trade shows or client meetings. Its primary job is marketing, so every page has to sell our horticulture expertise at a glance.

Content  
• Company history and mission – a concise narrative that highlights our roots, values and long-term vision in sustainable horticulture.  
• Services offered – clear, persuasive copy that outlines greenhouse construction, crop consultation and turnkey project management.  

Imagery  
High-resolution, royalty-free photos are essential. Where possible, weave in shots of our nurseries, equipment in action and finished landscape projects so the reader feels the scale and quality of our work.

Design & Format  
Print-ready, CMYK PDF (and the working file in InDesign or Illustrator) at 300 dpi, A4 size with a 3 mm bleed. Clean typography, plenty of white space and colours that echo natural greens.

Acceptance criteria  
1. Eight to twelve polished pages.  
2. All text proof-read and aligned with UK English.  
3. Image rights documented.  
4. Final files pass a pre-press check (no missing fonts, links or colour-space issues).

If you have a portfolio of similar brochure or profile work, especially in agriculture or horticulture, I’d like to see it before we start so we can lock in a visual direction quickly.
mcp_workflow: asset_search (StockAsset, query royalty-free horticulture: nurseries / greenhouses / landscaping) -> asset_license_and_download_stock (license the selected high-res shots, gives rights documentation) -> asset_preview_file (proof the licensed imagery) -> asset_create_folders (collect the licensed image set for handoff to the layout designer)
inputs: List of image themes the client named: nurseries, equipment in action, finished landscape/greenhouse projects (royalty-free, high-res for 300dpi A4 print)
Confirm how many hero/supporting images are needed for the 8–12 page profile
Brand colour direction: natural greens — ask client for exact hex/CMYK if they have brand colours, brief only says 'echo natural greens'
Note the deliverable spec: CMYK, 300dpi, A4, 3mm bleed (drives min stock resolution to license)
note: Connector can source/license the royalty-free imagery and document rights, but cannot do the InDesign/Illustrator multi-page brochure layout, copywriting, or CMYK pre-press — a human designer must build the 8–12 page profile.

title: YouTube Sci-Fi Explainer Editor
desc:
I run a small but steadily growing YouTube channel where I break down Science-Fiction and Fantasy films. Each episode needs brisk, energetic pacing—quick cuts, punchy transitions, and the kind of visual rhythm that keeps viewers glued for every plot twist. I also want subtle animated graphics woven in to highlight key scenes or concepts, giving the recap extra flair without overwhelming the footage.

Here’s how the workflow will look: I supply the raw voice-over, movie clips, and a rough beat sheet. Your task is to craft a polished 8-12-minute edit in 1080p, sync everything to the script, layer in suitable royalty-free SFX or risers if you feel they boost impact, and hand back both the final render and the project file so we can refine future episodes more quickly.

I’m still a student, so the budget starts lean, but consistency is guaranteed—expect a new video every week. As watch time and ad revenue climb, the rate will climb with it. Clear, friendly communication is important to me, and I always keep deadlines realistic so no one scrambles at the last minute.

Deliverables per episode:  
• Final video (H.264, 1080p, 25-30 fps)  
• Project file with all media relinked  
• Any custom animated assets created  

If high-energy edits and futuristic storytelling speak to you, let’s build a channel—and a long-term partnership—together.

inputs: The raw movie clips and rough beat sheet
Target 8-12 min, output H.264 1080p 25-30fps (stated)
The voice-over track + chosen SFX/risers (connector cannot sync VO or place SFX)
Any 'custom animated graphics' the brief wants (connector cannot create motion graphics)
